Identification.LongTitle: A second Act to implement certain provisions of the budget tabled in Parliament on November 4, 2025
Identification.BillSponsor: MINISTER OF FINANCE AND NATIONAL REVENUE

Provision.Text: Her Excellency the Governor General recommends to the House of Commons the appropriation of public revenue under the circumstances, in the manner and for the purposes set out in a measure entitled √¢¬Ä¬úA second Act to implement certain provisions of the budget tabled in Parliament on November 4, 2025√¢¬Ä¬ù.
Part 1 implements certain measures in respect of the Income Tax Act and the Income Tax Regulations by

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: providing temporary immediate expensing for eligible manufacturing or processing buildings;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: delivering automatic federal benefits for lower-income individuals;

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: expanding the anti-avoidance rule for direct trust to-trust transfers to include indirect transfers of trust property to other trusts;

Provision.Label: (d)
Provision.Text: limiting the deferral of tax on investment income resulting from the use of tiered corporate structures with mismatched year ends;

Provision.Label: (e)
Provision.Text: clarifying the expenses that qualify as Canadian exploration expenses;

Provision.Label: (f)
Provision.Text: implementing the Crypto-Asset Reporting Framework;

Provision.Label: (g)
Provision.Text: removing bankrupt corporations, trusts and partnerships from the exception to the debt forgiveness rules;

Provision.Label: (h)
Provision.Text: introducing a supplementary rule to strengthen the tax debt anti-avoidance rule;

Provision.Label: (i)
Provision.Text: expanding the clean hydrogen investment tax credit to include hydrogen produced from methane pyrolysis as an eligible production pathway;

Provision.Label: (j)
Provision.Text: enhancing the efficiency and effectiveness of information gathering during tax audits;

Provision.Label: (k)
Provision.Text: providing that no Canada Carbon Rebate payments would be made in respect of tax returns, or adjustment requests, filed after October 30, 2026;

Provision.Label: (l)
Provision.Text: simplifying, streamlining and harmonizing the qualified investment rules; and

Provision.Label: (m)
Provision.Text: making a number of technical amendments, including to correct inconsistencies and to better align the law with its intended policy objectives.

It also amends the Excise Tax Act, in relation to certain measures in respect of the Income Tax Act, and the Income Tax Conventions Implementation Act, 1996, which suspends the operation of the Canada-Russia Income Tax Agreement. Finally, it amends the Air Travellers Security Charge Act, the Excise Act, 2001 and the Select Luxury Items Tax Act in relation to certain measures in respect of the Income Tax Act.

Part 2 amends the Global Minimum Tax Act to, among other things, implement the UTPR that subjects the Canadian constituent entities of certain MNE groups to top-up tax in respect of the low-taxed profits of constituent entities of those MNE groups not already subject to an IIR or qualified domestic minimum top-up tax, implement certain aspects of the administrative guidance in respect of the GloBE Model Rules approved by the Inclusive Framework and published by the OECD and implement a number of technical amendments to correct mistakes or inconsistencies and to better align that Act with its intended policy objectives. This Part also makes amendments to the Access to Information Act, the Income Tax Conventions Interpretation Act and the Tax Court of Canada Act.
Part 3 amends the Excise Tax Act, the Excise Act, the Excise Act, 2001 and other related texts to implement various measures.
Division 1 of Part 3 implements certain measures in respect of the Excise Tax Act and related texts by

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: clarifying the tax treatment of federally regulated credit unions for Goods and Services Tax/Harmonized Sales Tax (GST/HST) purposes;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: extending the application of the special GST/HST rules for certain investment plans to first home savings accounts;

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: clarifying the application of the imported supply rules to financial institutions in respect of insurance policies or loans relating to persons resident in, or property located in, Canada;

Provision.Label: (d)
Provision.Text: clarifying the GST/HST treatment of certain services supplied by the Canadian Payments Association or any of its members as a consequence of a recent amendment to the Canadian Payments Act;

Provision.Label: (e)
Provision.Text: ensuring that special GST/HST rules for financial institutions apply correctly to certain small investment plans, master pension entities, insurers that issue only annuities and sureties of performance bonds;

Provision.Label: (f)
Provision.Text: making technical corrections to the input tax credit rules respecting the change in use of property following a sale of a business and to the GST/HST rules for financial institutions relating to mergers of investment plans;

Provision.Label: (g)
Provision.Text: ensuring that the GST/HST applies properly to Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs Insurance;

Provision.Label: (h)
Provision.Text: clarifying, in respect of financial institutions that do business in an HST province and at least one other province, filing requirements and rules related to the recovery of embedded GST/HST amounts;

Provision.Label: (i)
Provision.Text: providing a six-month period, following the death of an individual who is a GST/HST registrant, during which no return of the individual or their estate is required to be filed;

Provision.Label: (j)
Provision.Text: ensuring that a GST/HST reporting election between a supplier and its agent continues to apply despite the amalgamation, merger or wind-up of either party;

Provision.Label: (k)
Provision.Text: authorizing the Canada Revenue Agency to share information with international tax authorities with which Canada has an information-sharing agreement, in a manner consistent with the Income Tax Act; and

Provision.Label: (l)
Provision.Text: making a number of technical amendments to correct inconsistencies and to better align the law with its intended policy objectives.
Division 2 of Part 3 implements certain measures in respect of the Excise Act, the Excise Act, 2001 and other related texts by

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: making technical corrections in respect of the computation of the additional excise duty on cigars and the computation of negative amounts generated by statutory formulas;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: clarifying the tax treatment of certain cannabis and vaping products that are unaccounted for or that are taken for use;

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: implementing a new limit in respect of packaged raw leaf tobacco for importation for personal use and making consequential amendments to ensure the proper enforcement of the new limit;

Provision.Label: (d)
Provision.Text: allowing the Canada Revenue Agency to consider and grant relief to brewers in certain circumstances;

Provision.Label: (e)
Provision.Text: extending the maximum validity period for certain licences from two years to three years; and

Provision.Label: (f)
Provision.Text: authorizing the Canada Revenue Agency to share information with international tax authorities with which Canada has an information-sharing agreement, in a manner consistent with the Income Tax Act.
Part 4 enacts an Act and amends several Acts in order to implement various measures.

Division 1 of Part 4 amends the Trust and Loan Companies Act, the Bank Act and the Insurance Companies Act to prohibit financial institutions from issuing documents in bearer form and provide for the replacement of documents that are currently in bearer form.

Division 2 of Part 4 amends the Trust and Loan Companies Act, the Bank Act and the Insurance Companies Act to provide that no action lies against His Majesty in right of Canada and federal government officials for any acts or omissions made in good faith under those Acts.
Division 3 of Part 4 amends the Bank Act to require an institution to offer or sell deposit products in a non-discriminatory manner in certain circumstances.

Division 4 of Part 4 amends the Financial Administration Act to provide the Governor in Council with authority to make regulations with respect to the conditions under which contracts may be entered into by His Majesty or a Crown corporation. The Division also amends the Department of Public Works and Government Services Act to provide the Governor in Council with authority to make regulations respecting the complaints that may be reviewed by the Procurement Ombudsman and the persons who may file a complaint. The Division also makes a related amendment to the National Capital Act.
Division 5 of Part 4 increases the maximum amounts for accessing the Tax Court of Canada√¢¬Ä¬ôs informal procedure for appeals under the Income Tax Act and Part IX of the Excise Tax Act.

Division 6 of Part 4 amends Schedule II to the Access to Information Act to prohibit the disclosure of confidential information obtained under the Retail Payment Activities Act or prepared from information obtained under that Act.

Division 7 of Part 4 amends the National Housing Act to increase the total of Canada Mortgage and Housing Corporation outstanding guarantees that are in force. The Division also amends the Protection of Residential Mortgage or Hypothecary Insurance Act to increase the limit for loans that are insured under that Act.

Division 8 of Part 4 amends the Bankruptcy and Insolvency Act to provide the Superintendent of Bankruptcy with the power to request various orders from the court if an unlicensed person acts or represents itself as a licensed trustee, and if a person solicits from another person insolvency filings under that Act or makes representations that are false or misleading in a material respect in relation to bankruptcy and insolvency. The Division also increases the maximum fines for certain offences under that Act.
Division 9 of Part 4 amends the Canada Labour Code to, among other things, prohibit non-compete clauses and other employment-related restrictions, except in certain circumstances.

Division 10 of Part 4 amends the Canadian Human Rights Act to eliminate the position of Deputy Chief Commissioner of the Canadian Human Rights Commission and to provide that the person holding that office is deemed to have been appointed as Chief Commissioner.

Division 11 of Part 4 amends the International Development Research Centre Act to, among other things, reduce the number of members of the Board of Governors of the International Development Research Centre from 14 to 12.

Division 12 of Part 4 amends the Tobacco and Vaping Products Act to provide that a review of the provisions and operation of that Act must be undertaken within five years after the report on the previous review has been tabled in both Houses of Parliament rather than every two years and to specify the period within which the report on the review must be tabled.

Division 13 of Part 4 amends the Pest Control Products Act to replace the mandatory re-evaluation of registered pest control products with a requirement for the Minister of Health to initiate a re-evaluation if, after carrying out an assessment, that Minister has reasonable grounds to believe that the health or environmental risks of a product have increased significantly.
Division 14 of Part 4 amends the Territorial Lands Act to, among other things,

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: empower the Governor in Council, if the Governor in Council is of the opinion that it is in the national interest, to make orders

Provision.Label: (i)
Provision.Text: to take certain measures with respect to certain lands in Nunavut, including to cancel licences to prospect, the recording of claims or leases of recorded claims or to provide that claims are not to be recorded, that leases of recorded claims are not to be issued or that licences to prospect or leases of recorded claims are not to be renewed, and

Provision.Label: (ii)
Provision.Text: to provide for prohibitions associated with those measures for the persons that are the subject of the orders, including prohibiting the making of an application for a licence to prospect, to record a claim or to lease a recorded claim;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: provide that the Minister of Northern Affairs may determine whether compensation is to be paid to certain mineral rights holders that are the subject of the orders referred to in paragraph (a) and, if so, the amount; and

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: empower the Governor in Council to make regulations respecting the implementation of the orders referred to in paragraph (a) and the compensation referred to in paragraph (b).

Division 15 of Part 4 amends the Red Tape Reduction Act to, among other things, ensure that the provisions of the Official Languages Act, or the provisions of an instrument made under that Act, cannot be the subject of an exemption under Part 2 of the Red Tape Reduction Act.
Division 16 of Part 4 contains measures relating to procurement, production and investment in respect of national defence and national security.

Subdivision A of Division 16 enacts the Defence Investment Agency Act. That Act establishes the Defence Investment Agency, whose mandate is to assist the Minister who presides over that Agency in the exercise of the Minister√¢¬Ä¬ôs powers and performance of the Minister√¢¬Ä¬ôs duties and functions relating to production, procurement and investment in respect of national defence or national security. That Act also provides for certain other powers, duties and functions of that Minister. Subdivision A also makes related and consequential amendments to other Acts.
Subdivision B of Division 16 amends the Defence Production Act to, among other things,

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: extend the application of that Act to supplies and projects related to national security and to services related to national defence and national security;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: provide that the Minister who presides over the Defence Investment Agency has exclusive authority to acquire supplies and services related to national defence and national security that are required for the purposes of a department, board or agency of the Government of Canada, subject to certain exceptions;

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: extend the purposes for which that Minister may engage in stockpiling to include national defence and national security, including economic security, and the defence and security of an associated government or other state;

Provision.Label: (d)
Provision.Text: provide that Minister with new financial authorities, including the authority to enter into financial transactions for the purpose of investment in national defence and national security sectors; and

Provision.Label: (e)
Provision.Text: establish procurement rules in relation to national defence and national security.
Subdivision B also makes consequential amendments and terminology changes to certain legislative texts.
Division 17 of Part 4 amends the Canada Transportation Act to, among other things,

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: authorize the Governor in Council to choose to have the backlog of air travel complaints resolved by third parties engaged by the Minister of Transport or the Canadian Transportation Agency;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: transfer responsibility for the resolution of air travel complaints from the Canadian Transportation Agency to the Minister of Transport;

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: authorize the Governor in Council to choose to have future air travel complaints resolved by third parties approved by the Minister of Transport;

Provision.Label: (d)
Provision.Text: transfer authority to make regulations respecting air passenger rights from the Canadian Transportation Agency to the Minister of Transport;

Provision.Label: (e)
Provision.Text: remove mandatory confidentiality requirements regarding air travel complaints; and

Provision.Label: (f)
Provision.Text: increase the maximum administrative penalty payable by corporations for certain violations of the Canada Transportation Act or its regulations.

Heading.TitleText: A second Act to implement certain provisions of the budget tabled in Parliament on November 4, 2025

Heading.Label: PART 1
Heading.TitleText: Amendments to the Income Tax Act and Other Legislation

Heading.Label: PART 2
Heading.TitleText: Amendments to the Global Minimum Tax Act and Related Legislation

Heading.Label: PART 3
Heading.TitleText: Amendments to the Excise Tax Act, the Excise Act, the Excise Act, 2001 and Other Related Texts

Heading.Label: DIVISION 1
Heading.TitleText: Excise Tax Act and Other Related Texts (GST/HST)

Heading.Label: DIVISION 2
Heading.TitleText: Excise Act, Excise Act, 2001 and Other Related Texts

Heading.Label: DIVISION 1
Heading.TitleText: Legislation Related to Financial Institutions (Bearer Instruments)

Heading.Label: DIVISION 2
Heading.TitleText: Legislation Related to Financial Institutions (Immunity √¢¬Ä¬î Good Faith Actions)

Heading.Label: DIVISION 7
Heading.TitleText: Outstanding Guarantee and Protected Loan Limits

Heading.Label: DIVISION 16
Heading.TitleText: Defence Investment Agency Act and Defence Production Act
An Act to establish the Defence Investment Agency

Provision.Text: His Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the Budget 2025 Implementation Act, No. 2.

Heading.Label: PART 1
Heading.TitleText: Amendments to the Income Tax Act and Other Legislation

Section.Text: Paragraph 6(1)(e.1) of the Income Tax Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.MarginalNote: Group sickness or accident insurance plans
Paragraph.Label: (e.1)
Paragraph.Text: the total of all amounts contributed in the year in respect of the taxpayer by the taxpayer√¢¬Ä¬ôs employer to a group sickness or accident insurance plan (including a plan that is administered or provided by an employee life and health trust), except to the extent that the contributions are attributable to benefits under the plan that, if received by the taxpayer, would be included in the taxpayer√¢¬Ä¬ôs income under paragraph (f) in the year the benefits are received if that paragraph were read without regard to its subparagraph (v);

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph 8(1)(e) of the Act after subparagraph (ii) is replaced by the following:

Provision.Text: to the extent that the taxpayer has not been reimbursed and is not entitled to be reimbursed in respect thereof and has not received and is not entitled to receive an allowance in respect thereof the inclusion of which is not required under this Act in computing the taxpayer√¢¬Ä¬ôs income;

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph 8(1)(g) of the Act after subparagraph (ii) is replaced by the following:

ContinuedParagraph.Text: amounts so disbursed by the taxpayer in the year to the extent that the taxpayer has not been reimbursed and is not entitled to be reimbursed in respect thereof and has not received and is not entitled to receive an allowance in respect thereof the inclusion of which is not required under the Act in computing the taxpayer√¢¬Ä¬ôs income;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 12(1)(z.6) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: z.6)
Paragraph.Text: la somme re√É¬ßue par le contribuable au cours de l√¢¬Ä¬ôann√É¬©e √É¬Ý titre de remboursement d√¢¬Ä¬ôun montant qui a √É¬©t√É¬© d√É¬©duit en application de l√¢¬Ä¬ôalin√É¬©a 20(1)vv) dans le calcul du revenu pour une ann√É¬©e d√¢¬Ä¬ôimposition;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 12(13)(b) of the Act is amended by adding the following after subparagraph (i):

Subparagraph.Label: (i.1)
Subparagraph.Text: if a taxpayer is a trust, a deemed disposition by the taxpayer by application of paragraph 104(4)(a),

Subsection.Text: Subsection (2) applies to the period throughout which a flipped property of a taxpayer is owned or held by the taxpayer in respect of a disposition that occurs after 2022.
Subsection 13(7) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (d):

Paragraph.Label: (d.1)
Paragraph.Text: if a taxpayer is subject to a manufacturing building recapture event in respect of a building in a taxation year, the taxpayer is deemed to have

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: disposed of the building at the time of the event for proceeds of disposition equal to the amount that would have been the undepreciated capital cost of the building if

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the building had never qualified for the deduction under subparagraph 1100(1)(a.1)(i) of the Income Tax Regulations,

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the building were the only property of the prescribed class to which it would have belonged, and

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: the taxpayer had deducted the maximum amount allowed under paragraph 20(1)(a) in respect of that class for all prior taxation years,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: reacquired the building as property of a separate prescribed class immediately after that time at a cost equal to the amount that was the capital cost to the taxpayer of the building immediately before that time, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: deducted, in computing income for taxation years that ended before that time, an amount under paragraph 20(1)(a) in respect of the building equal to the amount determined under clause (i)(C);

Subsection.Text: The description of F in the definition fraction non amortie du co√É¬ªt en capital in subsection 13(21) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

FormulaDefinition.Text: le total des sommes dont chacune est une somme √É¬Ý l√¢¬Ä¬ô√É¬©gard d√¢¬Ä¬ôune disposition, avant ce moment, d√¢¬Ä¬ôun bien (sauf un avoir forestier) de cette cat√É¬©gorie dont le contribuable est propri√É¬©taire, et qui correspond √É¬Ý la moins √É¬©lev√É¬©e des sommes suivantes√¢¬Ä¬Ø:

FormulaParagraph.Label: a)
FormulaParagraph.Text: le produit de disposition du bien moins les d√É¬©penses engag√É¬©es ou effectu√É¬©es en vue de la disposition,

FormulaParagraph.Label: b)
FormulaParagraph.Text: le co√É¬ªt en capital que ce contribuable a support√É¬© pour le bien;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 13(21) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: manufacturing building recapture event√¢¬Ä¬Çmeans an event in respect of a building of a taxpayer if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in a prior taxation year, the taxpayer deducted an amount in respect of the building under paragraph 20(1)(a) pursuant to subparagraph 1100(1)(a.1)(i) of the Income Tax Regulations, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in a taxation year that begins within 10 calendar years of the end of the prior taxation year, the taxpayer, or a lessee of the taxpayer, began to use more than 10% of the floor space of the building for one or more income-earning purposes other than the manufacturing or processing (as defined in subsection 1104(9) of the Income Tax Regulations) in Canada of goods for sale or lease;√¢¬Ä¬Ç(√É¬©v√É¬©nement de r√É¬©cup√É¬©ration relatif √É¬Ý un b√É¬¢timent de fabrication)

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (3) are deemed to have come into force on November 4, 2025.

Section.Text: The portion of paragraph 56(4.1)(c) of the French version of the Act before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: c)
Paragraph.Text: le revenu du particulier donn√É¬© pour une ann√É¬©e d√¢¬Ä¬ôimposition provenant du bien vis√É¬© √É¬Ý l√¢¬Ä¬ôalin√É¬©a b), qui se rapporte √É¬Ý une ou plusieurs p√É¬©riodes de l√¢¬Ä¬ôann√É¬©e tout au long desquelles le cr√É¬©ancier ou la fiducie cr√É¬©anci√É¬®re r√É¬©side au Canada et le cr√É¬©ancier ou le c√É¬©dant initial a un lien de d√É¬©pendance avec le particulier donn√É¬©, est consid√É¬©r√É¬©√¢¬Ä¬Ø:

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 60(j)(i) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Provision.Label: (i)
Provision.Text: a benefit (other than any amount in respect of the benefit that is deducted in computing the taxable income of the taxpayer for a taxation year because of subparagraph 110(1)(f)(i) or a benefit that is part of a series of periodic payments) payable out of or under a pension plan that is not a registered pension plan, included in computing the income of the taxpayer for the year because of

Clause.Text: subparagraph 56(1)(a)(i) as a superannuation or pension benefit attributable to services rendered by the taxpayer or a spouse or common-law partner or former spouse or common-law partner of the taxpayer in a period throughout which that person was not resident in Canada, or

Text.Ins: paragraph 6(1)(g) as a benefit paid from a foreign plan as defined in subsection 6804(1) of the Income Tax Regulations under which pension credits described in subsection 8308.1(2) of the Regulations were reported in respect of the taxpayer, or a spouse or common-law partner or former spouse or common-law partner of the taxpayer, for all years of service rendered under the plan by the taxpayer or a spouse or common-law partner, or

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2024.

Section.Text: Subsection 62(2) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Un contribuable peut d√É¬©duire dans le calcul de son revenu pour une ann√É¬©e d√¢¬Ä¬ôimposition la somme qu√¢¬Ä¬ôil pourrait d√É¬©duire en application du paragraphe (1) s√¢¬Ä¬ôil n√¢¬Ä¬ô√É¬©tait pas tenu compte du sous-alin√É¬©a a)(i) de la d√É¬©finition de r√É¬©installation admissible au paragraphe 248(1) et si l√¢¬Ä¬ôalin√É¬©a c) de cette d√É¬©finition √É¬©tait remplac√É¬© par ce qui suit√¢¬Ä¬Ø:

Paragraph.Label: √Ç¬´√¢¬Ä¬Øc)
Paragraph.Text: sauf si le contribuable est absent du Canada mais y r√É¬©side, l√¢¬Ä¬ôancienne r√É¬©sidence ou la nouvelle r√É¬©sidence est situ√É¬©e au Canada ou les deux y sont situ√É¬©es;√¢¬Ä¬Ø√Ç¬ª

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph (a) of the definition Canadian exploration expense in subsection 66.1(6) of the Act before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any expense incurred by the taxpayer (other than an expense incurred in drilling or completing an oil or gas well or in building a temporary access road to, or preparing a site in respect of, any such well) for the purpose of determining the existence, location, extent or inherent natural qualities of an accumulation of petroleum or natural gas (other than a mineral resource) in Canada, including such an expense that is

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph (f) of the definition Canadian exploration expense in subsection 66.1(6) of the Act before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: any expense incurred by the taxpayer (other than an expense incurred in drilling or completing an oil or gas well or in building a temporary access road to, or preparing a site in respect of, any such well) for the purpose of determining the existence, location, extent or inherent natural qualities of a mineral resource in Canada including such an expense for environmental studies or community consultations (including, notwithstanding subparagraph (v), studies or consultations that are undertaken to obtain a right, license or privilege for the purpose of determining the existence, location, extent or inherent natural qualities of a mineral resource in Canada) and any expense incurred in the course of

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (2) are deemed to have come into force on November 4, 2025.

Section.Text: Clause 66.7(10)(j)(ii)(B) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: ce que serait sa part du revenu de la soci√É¬©t√É¬© de personnes, pour l√¢¬Ä¬ôexercice de celle-ci se terminant au cours de l√¢¬Ä¬ôann√É¬©e, qu√¢¬Ä¬ôil est raisonnable de consid√É¬©rer comme attribuable √É¬Ý la production tir√É¬©e de l√¢¬Ä¬ôavoir, si elle √É¬©tait d√É¬©termin√É¬©e en fonction de la part, exprim√É¬©e en pourcentage, vis√É¬©e au sous-alin√É¬©a (i).

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (i) of the description of B in the definition forgiven amount in subsection 80(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: if the debtor is an individual (other than a partnership or trust) who is a bankrupt at that time, the principal amount of the obligation,

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies in respect of bankruptcy proceedings of corporations that are commenced on or after April 16, 2024. It also applies in respect of bankruptcy proceedings of partnerships and trusts that are commenced on or after August 12, 2024.
Paragraph 87(2)(j.6) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (j.6)
Paragraph.Text: for the purposes of paragraphs 12(1)(t) and (x), subsections 12(2.2) and 13(7.1), (7.4) and (24), paragraphs 13(27)(b) and (28)(c), subsections 13(29) and 18(9.1), paragraphs 20(1)(e), (e.1), (v) and (hh), sections 20.1 and 32, paragraph 37(1)(c), subsection 39(13), subparagraphs 53(2)(c)(vi) and (h)(ii), paragraph 53(2)(s), subsections 53(2.1), 66(11.4), 66.7(11) and 84.1(2.31) and (2.32), sections 110.61 and 110.62, subsections 127(10.2), (10.31) and (10.32) and 129(1.3) to (1.32), section 139.1, subsection 152(4.3), the determination of D in the definition undepreciated capital cost in subsection 13(21), the determination of L in the definition cumulative Canadian exploration expense in subsection 66.1(6) and the definitions qualifying business transfer and qualifying cooperative conversion in subsection 248(1), the new corporation is deemed to be the same corporation as, and a continuation of, each predecessor corporation;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 87(2)(ii) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (ii)
Paragraph.Text: where a predecessor corporation was a public corporation immediately before the amalgamation, the new corporation shall be deemed to have been a public corporation at the commencement of its first taxation year unless

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: after the last time a class of shares of the capital stock of the predecessor corporation became listed on a designated stock exchange in Canada and before the amalgamation, an election or designation was made in respect of the predecessor corporation under paragraph (c) of the definition public corporation in subsection 89(1),

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: immediately before the amalgamation, the predecessor corporation was a subsidiary wholly-owned corporation of another corporation (other than a public corporation) (in this paragraph referred to as the √¢¬Ä¬úparent√¢¬Ä¬ù), and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the amalgamation was an amalgamation of the parent and the predecessor corporation to which subsection (11) applies;

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to taxation years that begin on or after November 4, 2025.
Subsection (2) applies to amalgamations that occur after the day on which this Act receives royal assent.
Section 91 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (4):

For the purpose of the definition foreign accrual tax in subsection 95(1), a portion of income or profits tax paid by a particular foreign affiliate, or a shareholder affiliate (within the meaning assigned by subparagraph (a)(ii) of that definition), of a taxpayer to the government of a country under a domestic minimum top-up tax regime (as defined in subsection 5907(1) of the Income Tax Regulations) may reasonably be regarded as applicable to an amount included under subsection (1) in computing the taxpayer√¢¬Ä¬ôs income for a taxation year of the taxpayer in respect of the particular affiliate only if that portion can reasonably be considered to be in respect of income or profits, as determined under that tax regime, of the particular affiliate or the shareholder affiliate, as the case may be, that are derived from an activity the income, profit or gains from which are included in the particular affiliate√¢¬Ä¬ôs foreign accrual property income that gives rise to the amount included under subsection (1).

For the purpose of subsection (4.01), the income or profits tax payable under a domestic minimum top-up tax regime (as defined in subsection 5907(1) of the Income Tax Regulations) for a fiscal year (as defined in subsection 5907(1) of the Income Tax Regulations) that can reasonably be considered to be in respect of the income or profits, as determined under that tax regime, of a foreign affiliate of a taxpayer is the amount determined under subsection 5907(1.192) of the Income Tax Regulations.

Despite any other provision of this Act, if a particular amount of income or profits tax payable under a domestic minimum top-up tax regime (as defined in subsection 5907(1) of the Income Tax Regulations) of a country other than Canada was determined taking into account any tax imposed under this Act (other than any tax imposed under Part XIII), no amount paid in respect of that particular amount is to be included in computing the foreign accrual tax applicable to an amount included under subsection (1) in computing a taxpayer√¢¬Ä¬ôs income for any taxation year of the taxpayer.
Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on December 31, 2023.

Section 126 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (4.6):

For the purpose of the definition business-income tax in subsection (7), a portion of income or profits tax paid by a taxpayer to the government of a country other than Canada under a domestic minimum top-up tax regime for a fiscal year can reasonably be regarded as tax in respect of the income of the taxpayer from a business carried on by the taxpayer in a business country (within the meaning of the definition business-income tax in subsection (7)) only if that portion can reasonably be considered to be in respect of income or profits, as determined under that tax regime, of the taxpayer that are derived from an activity the income, profit or gains from which would be included in the taxpayer√¢¬Ä¬ôs income from a business carried on in the business country.

For the purposes of subsection (4.7) and paragraph (j) of the definition non-business-income tax in subsection (7), the income or profits tax payable under a domestic minimum top-up tax regime for a fiscal year that can reasonably be considered to be in respect of the income or profits, as determined under that tax regime, of a taxpayer that is a member of a DMTT group for the fiscal year is,

Paragraph.Label: (a)

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in any other case, the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total amount of income or profits tax payable in respect of the DMTT group under that tax regime for the fiscal year,
is the income or profits, as determined under that tax regime, of the taxpayer for the fiscal year, and
is the total of all amounts, each of which is the income or profits, as determined under that tax regime, of a member of the DMTT group for the fiscal year.

Subparagraph.Label: (ii.1)
Subparagraph.Text: that is used to produce all or substantially all hydrogen from the pyrolysis of eligible hydrocarbons (determined without reference to any captured carbon or solid carbon that is produced), including pre-heating equipment, purification equipment, fired heaters, equipment used for compression and storage of hydrogen, oxygen production equipment, solid carbon separation equipment and property that is part of a pyrolysis reactor system,

Subsection.Text: Clause (c)(iii)(C) of the definition eligible clean hydrogen property in subsection 127.48(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: oxygen and nitrogen production equipment, or

Subsection.Text: The definition eligible pathway in subsection 127.48(1) of the Act is amended by striking out √¢¬Ä¬úor√¢¬Ä¬ù at the end of paragraph (a), by adding √¢¬Ä¬úor√¢¬Ä¬ù at the end of paragraph (b) and by adding the following after paragraph (b):

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: from the pyrolysis of eligible hydrocarbons.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(m√É¬©thode admissible)

Subsection.Text: Clauses (a)(ii)(A) and (B) of the definition eligible power purchase agreement in subsection 127.48(1) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the same province as the taxpayer√¢¬Ä¬ôs clean hydrogen project and is directly connected to the project or to the electricity grid of that province,

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the exclusive economic zone of Canada and is directly connected to the taxpayer√¢¬Ä¬ôs clean hydrogen project or to the grid of the province in which the project is located, or

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (b) of the definition eligible power purchase agreement in subsection 127.48(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: grants, or will grant, the taxpayer the sole and exclusive right to the environmental attributes associated with the electricity, as may be evidenced by one or more environmental attribute certificates issued through a provincially designated authority; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: that, if acquired by the taxpayer under an agreement, the agreement grants, or will grant, the taxpayer the sole and exclusive right to the environmental attributes associated with the substance, as may be evidenced by one or more environmental attribute certificates issued through a provincially designated authority; and

Subsection.Text: The definition excluded property in subsection 127.48(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (a):

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: equipment used to generate electrical energy, heat energy or a combination of electrical and heat energy to support the production of hydrogen through electrolysis of water, other than equipment that is used for the generation of heat energy and that is physically and functionally integrated with high-temperature electrolysis equipment;

Subsection.Text: The definition excluded property in subsection 127.48(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (a.1):

Paragraph.Label: (a.2)
Paragraph.Text: used to produce hydrogen from the pyrolysis of eligible hydrocarbons and is

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: acquired before December 16, 2024, determined without reference to subsection (5),

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: equipment used for the collection, processing or storage of solid carbon, including dryers, pulverizers, bag collectors, densifiers and pin mixers, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: equipment used for the off-site transmission, transportation or distribution of solid carbon;

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph (b)(i) of the definition qualified clean hydrogen project in subsection 127.48(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: is less than four and is determined in accordance with subsection (6), and

Subsection.Text: The definition qualified clean hydrogen project in subsection 127.48(1) of the Act is amended by striking out √¢¬Ä¬úand√¢¬Ä¬ù at the end of paragraph (b), by adding √¢¬Ä¬úand√¢¬Ä¬ù at the end of paragraph (c) and by adding the following after paragraph (c):

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: if the project is intended to produce hydrogen from the pyrolysis of eligible hydrocarbons, the taxpayer has demonstrated that

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the expected hydrogen production contained in the taxpayer√¢¬Ä¬ôs most recent clean hydrogen project plan can reasonably be expected to be achieved based on the project design,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the expected hydrogen use percentage set out in the end-use plan is 90% or greater,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the project is expected to consume less than 50% of the hydrogen produced during the compliance period, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: the expectations set out in the end-use plan can reasonably be expected to be achieved.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(projet admissible pour l√¢¬Ä¬ôhydrog√É¬®ne propre)

Subsection.Text: Subsection 127.48(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: oxygen and nitrogen production equipment√¢¬Ä¬Çmeans equipment that is

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: part of a clean hydrogen project; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: used for the generation of oxygen or nitrogen to be used all or substantially all in at least two of the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: hydrogen production,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: ammonia production,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: electricity or heat production in support of the project, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: a CCUS process in support of the project.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(mat√É¬©riel pour la production d√¢¬Ä¬ôoxyg√É¬®ne et d√¢¬Ä¬ôazote)

Subsection.Text: Subsection 127.48(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: actual hydrogen use percentage, in respect of a taxpayer√¢¬Ä¬ôs clean hydrogen project that produces hydrogen from the pyrolysis of eligible hydrocarbons, means the percentage determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the quantity of hydrogen (other than excluded hydrogen), in kilograms, produced by the project and used for a qualifying economic purpose during the project√¢¬Ä¬ôs compliance period; and
is the quantity of hydrogen (other than excluded hydrogen), in kilograms, produced by the project during the compliance period.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(pourcentage r√É¬©el d√¢¬Ä¬ôutilisation d√¢¬Ä¬ôhydrog√É¬®ne)

Definition.Text: combustion, in relation to solid carbon, includes any reaction that results in a specified greenhouse gas being produced.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(combustion)
end-use plan, in respect of a clean hydrogen project of a taxpayer that is intended to produce hydrogen from the pyrolysis of eligible hydrocarbons, means a plan that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: sets out the expected uses of any solid carbon to be produced by the project during the first seven years of hydrogen production (referred to in this definition as the √¢¬Ä¬úend-use period√¢¬Ä¬ù), including the quantity, in kilograms, and percentage share of solid carbon expected to be

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: treated as a waste product and disposed of in a manner that would not lead to any of the carbon being released into the atmosphere,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: used for combustion or incorporated into a product intended for combustion,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: used in or incorporated into a product that is not intended for combustion, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: used for any other purpose;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if any solid carbon to be produced by the project is expected to be sold during the end-use period, includes copies of any purchase agreements or other arrangements in writing

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: supporting the sale of the solid carbon,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: setting out the expected uses of the solid carbon as described in paragraph (a), and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: requiring the provision of information to the taxpayer in relation to the actual use of the solid carbon;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: sets out the project√¢¬Ä¬ôs expected hydrogen use percentage;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: includes copies of agreements or other arrangements in writing supporting the sale of hydrogen expected to be produced by the project during the compliance period; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: contains any information required in guidelines published by the Minister of Natural Resources, including the Clean Hydrogen Investment Tax Credit √¢¬Ä¬ì Validation and Verification Guidance Document.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(plan d√¢¬Ä¬ôutilisation finale)

Definition.Text: excluded hydrogen, in respect of a taxpayer√¢¬Ä¬ôs clean hydrogen project that produces hydrogen from the pyrolysis of eligible hydrocarbons, means hydrogen that is or is expected to be vented or flared

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: for the purpose of safety or system integrity; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: due to the bona fide termination of an agreement supporting the sale of the hydrogen or a reasonably unforeseen change to the intended use of the hydrogen by the project, where such venting or flaring occurs for a cumulative maximum period of 180 days during the project√¢¬Ä¬ôs compliance period.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(hydrog√É¬®ne exclu)

Definition.Text: expected hydrogen production√¢¬Ä¬Çmeans a clean hydrogen project√¢¬Ä¬ôs expected gross average annual hydrogen production, in tonnes, over the first 20 years of the project√¢¬Ä¬ôs operations.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(production d√¢¬Ä¬ôhydrog√É¬®ne attendue)

expected hydrogen use percentage, in respect of a taxpayer√¢¬Ä¬ôs clean hydrogen project that produces hydrogen from the pyrolysis of eligible hydrocarbons, means the percentage determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the quantity of hydrogen (other than excluded hydrogen), in kilograms, expected to be produced by the project and used for a qualifying economic purpose during the project√¢¬Ä¬ôs compliance period, as evidenced in the project√¢¬Ä¬ôs clean hydrogen project plan; and

is the quantity of hydrogen (other than excluded hydrogen), in kilograms, expected to be produced by the project during the compliance period.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(pourcentage attendu d√¢¬Ä¬ôutilisation d√¢¬Ä¬ôhydrog√É¬®ne)

Definition.Text: pyrolysis reactor system, in respect of a clean hydrogen project, means all property, other than excluded property, consisting of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: one or more pyrolysis reactors;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any equipment that performs the function of a pyrolysis reactor; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any equipment that is physically and functionally integrated with property described in paragraph (a) or (b).√¢¬Ä¬Ç(syst√É¬®me de r√É¬©acteur de pyrolyse)

Definition.Text: qualifying economic purpose, in relation to hydrogen produced by a taxpayer√¢¬Ä¬ôs clean hydrogen project, means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the use by the taxpayer of the hydrogen for a bona fide economic purpose; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the sale of the hydrogen by the taxpayer to a third party in circumstances where it is reasonable to conclude that the hydrogen is or will be used for a bona fide economic purpose.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(fins √É¬©conomiques admissibles)

Definition.Text: solid carbon√¢¬Ä¬Çmeans a product that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is in a solid state;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: contains the chemical element carbon; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: is derived from the pyrolysis of eligible hydrocarbons.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(carbone solide)

Subsection.Text: Subsection 127.48(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:
A payment on account of tax payable under subsection (2)

Paragraph.Text: is deemed not to have been paid if the taxpayer does not file with the Minister the prescribed form containing prescribed information referred to in subsection (2) in respect of the amount on or before the later of

Subparagraph.Text: the day that is one year after the taxpayer√¢¬Ä¬ôs filing-due date for the year, and

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: December 31, 2027, if the amount under subsection (2) is in respect of a clean hydrogen project that is intended to produce hydrogen from the pyrolysis of eligible hydrocarbons, or
December 31, 2026, in any other case; and

Paragraph.Text: if the prescribed form is filed after the taxpayer√¢¬Ä¬ôs filing-due date for the year, is deemed not to arise until the prescribed form containing prescribed information has been filed with the Minister.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 127.48(6)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the most recent version of the Fuel LCA Model at the time of filing by the taxpayer of the most recent related clean hydrogen project plan with the Minister of Natural Resources shall be used, unless, at the time of filing any compliance report under subsection (16), the taxpayer elects to use a subsequent version of the Fuel LCA Model in calculating the actual carbon intensity of the project;

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 127.48(6)(d)(i) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: any captured carbon that is subject to an ineligible use (as defined in subsection 127.44(1)) is deemed not to be captured, and

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 127.48(6)(d) of the Act, as amended by subsection (20), is amended by striking out √¢¬Ä¬úand√¢¬Ä¬ù at the end of subparagraph (i) and by adding the following after subparagraph (ii):

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: any solid carbon produced by the project is deemed to be permanently stored if the carbon is

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: treated as a waste product and disposed of in a manner that would not lead to any of the carbon being released into the atmosphere, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: used in or incorporated into a product that is not intended for combustion,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: any solid carbon produced by the project that is not used in a manner described in clause (iii)(A) or (B) is deemed to be converted into carbon dioxide that is released into the atmosphere by the project, and

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: the contribution to expected carbon intensity of any solid carbon to be produced by the project is to be weighted based on the aggregate quantity of solid carbon, in kilograms, in each expected use, as described in accordance with paragraph (a) of the definition end-use plan in subsection (1);

Subsection.Text: Clause 127.48(6)(e)(i)(B) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: on-site generation equipment that is used solely to convert any one or a combination of hydrogen, heat described in any of subparagraphs (i)√¢¬Ä¬ç(i) to (iii) or eligible hydrocarbons into electricity that supports the production of hydrogen from eligible hydrocarbons, the contribution of the electricity to carbon intensity is to be modelled as part of the project,

Subsection.Text: Clause 127.48(6)(e)(i)(D) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Clause.Label: (D)
Clause.Text: a generation source other than as described in any of clauses (A) to (C),

Subclause.Text: if the project√¢¬Ä¬ôs compliance period has not yet commenced, the expected carbon intensity of the project is deemed to be greater than 4.5, and

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: if the project√¢¬Ä¬ôs compliance period has commenced, the average actual carbon intensity of the project is deemed to be greater than 4.5 and subsection (18) applies as if the compliance period of the project had ended;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 127.48(6)(e) of the Act is amended by striking out √¢¬Ä¬úand√¢¬Ä¬ù at the end of subparagraph (ii) and by adding the following after that subparagraph:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii.1)
Subparagraph.Text: generated, or to be generated, from an eligible electricity generation source and purchased, or to be purchased, by the taxpayer pursuant to an agreement that is not an eligible power purchase agreement,

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the contribution of the electricity to carbon intensity is to be calculated as the higher of the input carbon intensity of the technology-specific electricity and the provincial grid in the Fuel LCA Model, and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the contribution of the electricity to expected carbon intensity is to be calculated in proportion to the number of years for which the agreement will be in place during the first 20 years of the project√¢¬Ä¬ôs operations,

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 127.48(6)(e) of the Act is amended by adding √¢¬Ä¬úand√¢¬Ä¬ù at the end of subparagraph (iii) and by adding the following after that subparagraph:

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: from a source that is not described in any of subparagraphs (i) to (iii),

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: if the project√¢¬Ä¬ôs compliance period has not yet commenced, the expected carbon intensity of the project is deemed to be greater than 4.5, and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: if the project√¢¬Ä¬ôs compliance period has commenced, the average actual carbon intensity of the project is deemed to be greater than 4.5 and subsection (18) applies as if the compliance period of the project had ended;

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 127.48(6)(h) and (i) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: if the taxpayer disposes of any environmental attributes associated with any electricity described in subparagraph (e)(i) or (ii) or any eligible renewable hydrocarbon described in subparagraph (g)(i),

Subparagraph.Text: if the project√¢¬Ä¬ôs compliance period has not yet commenced, the expected carbon intensity of the project is deemed to be greater than 4.5, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if the project√¢¬Ä¬ôs compliance period has commenced, the average actual carbon intensity of the project is deemed to be greater than 4.5 and subsection (18) applies as if the compliance period of the project had ended;

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: if, in connection with hydrogen production or electricity production in support of hydrogen production, the taxpayer uses, or proposes to use, heat energy

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: recovered from hydrogen production by the taxpayer, recovered from electricity production by the taxpayer in support of hydrogen production or produced by the taxpayer from the combustion of hydrogen or eligible hydrocarbons (with carbon dioxide captured using a CCUS process), the contribution of the heat to carbon intensity is to be modelled as part of the project,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: recovered from ammonia production by the taxpayer√¢¬Ä¬ôs clean hydrogen project, the contribution of the heat to carbon intensity may be disregarded,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: recovered from a production process of the taxpayer, other than as described in subparagraph (i) or (ii), or purchased from a vendor that produced the heat from eligible hydrocarbons or recovered the waste heat from a production process, the contribution of the heat to carbon intensity is to correspond with the input carbon intensity of purchased steam in the Fuel LCA Model, and
from a source other than as described in subparagraphs (i) to (iii),

Clause.Text: if the project√¢¬Ä¬ôs compliance period has not yet commenced, the expected carbon intensity of the project is deemed to be greater than 4.5, and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: if the project√¢¬Ä¬ôs compliance period has commenced, the average actual carbon intensity of the project is deemed to be greater than 4.5 and subsection (18) applies as if the compliance period of the project had ended;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 127.48(6)(k) of the Act is amended by striking out √¢¬Ä¬úand√¢¬Ä¬ù at the end of subparagraph (iii), by adding √¢¬Ä¬úand√¢¬Ä¬ù at the end of subparagraph (iv) and by adding the following after subparagraph (iv):

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: solid carbon, unless the solid carbon is used in or incorporated into a product that is not intended for combustion;

Subsection.Text: Section 127.48 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (6):
For the purposes of subsections (6) and (7), where a taxpayer has acquired an environmental attribute certificate in connection with the purchase of electricity or eligible renewable hydrocarbons,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the disposition of environmental attributes by the taxpayer does not include the retirement of the certificate to claim the use of the attributes with the appropriate provincially designated authority in relation to the taxpayer√¢¬Ä¬ôs clean hydrogen project; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for the purpose of calculating the project√¢¬Ä¬ôs actual carbon intensity, if the taxpayer has not retired any certificate associated with the electricity or eligible renewable hydrocarbons used by the taxpayer in relation to the project during the project√¢¬Ä¬ôs compliance period, the environmental attributes associated with the certificate are deemed to have been disposed of by the taxpayer immediately before the end of the compliance period.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 127.48(7) of the Act is amended by striking out √¢¬Ä¬úor√¢¬Ä¬ù at the end of paragraph (c) and by replacing paragraph (d) with the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: any environmental attributes associated with an eligible power purchase agreement referenced in the most recent clean hydrogen project plan of the taxpayer or an agreement for the acquisition of eligible renewable hydrocarbons have been disposed of by the taxpayer; or

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the taxpayer notifies the Minister and the Minister of Natural Resources in writing that it intends to file a revised clean hydrogen project plan for the project.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 127.48(7) of the Act, as amended by subsection (29), is amended by striking out √¢¬Ä¬úor√¢¬Ä¬ù at the end of paragraph (d) and by adding the following after that paragraph:

Paragraph.Label: (d.1)
Paragraph.Text: the project is intended to produce hydrogen from the pyrolysis of eligible hydrocarbons and

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the taxpayer reasonably expects that there will be a decrease (as compared to the most recent project plan for the project) to the project√¢¬Ä¬ôs expected hydrogen production, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: any agreement supporting the sale of any hydrogen, or sale and use of any solid carbon, referenced in the most recent end-use plan of the taxpayer

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: has not been finalized and executed so as to become legally binding, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: has been materially modified or terminated; or

Subsection.Text: Subparagraphs 127.48(8)(b)(ii) and (iii) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if the taxpayer previously deducted a clean hydrogen tax credit in respect of the project,

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the amount of the clean hydrogen tax credit previously deducted shall not be increased, and

subsection (18) applies as if the compliance period ended on the date of the filing of the revised plan and the average actual carbon intensity of the project was equal to the expected carbon intensity set out in the revised plan, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: any new clean hydrogen tax credit of the taxpayer determined on or after the date of the confirmation of the revised plan shall be based on the expected carbon intensity set out in the revised plan;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 127.48(8)(b) of the Act, as amended by subsection (31), is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the Minister of Natural Resources is satisfied that the project will meet the requirements in paragraphs (a) to (d) of the definition qualified clean hydrogen project in subsection (1),

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the Minister of Natural Resources shall confirm, with all due dispatch, the revised plan,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if the taxpayer previously deducted a clean hydrogen tax credit in respect of the project,

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the amount of the clean hydrogen tax credit previously deducted shall not be increased,

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: subsection (18) applies as if the compliance period ended on the date of the filing of the revised plan and the average actual carbon intensity of the project was equal to the expected carbon intensity set out in the revised plan, and

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: subsection (10.3) applies for the taxation year in which the revised plan is filed, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: any new clean hydrogen tax credit of the taxpayer determined on or after the date of the confirmation of the revised plan shall be based on the expected carbon intensity and, if applicable, the expected hydrogen production, set out in the revised plan;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 127.48(9) of the Act is amended by striking out √¢¬Ä¬úand√¢¬Ä¬ù at the end of paragraph (d) and by replacing paragraph (e) with the following:

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister of Natural Resources may request from the taxpayer all documentation and information necessary for the Minister of Natural Resources to fulfill a responsibility under this section and the taxpayer shall provide such documentation or information on or before the later of the day that is

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: 180 days after the documentation or information was requested, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: 60 days after the documentation or information becomes available; and

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: if the taxpayer fails to provide the documentation or information as required under paragraph (e), in addition to any penalties applicable under this Act, the Minister of Natural Resources may refuse to confirm the taxpayer√¢¬Ä¬ôs clean hydrogen project plan or revised clean hydrogen project plan.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 127.48(10)(a) of the Act is amended by striking out √¢¬Ä¬úor√¢¬Ä¬ù at the end of subparagraph (ii) and by adding the following after that subparagraph:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii.1)
Subparagraph.Text: that is a qualified expenditure (as defined in subsection 127(9)) in respect of which an investment tax credit was deducted by any person, or

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph 127.48(10)(g) of the Act before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: after applying paragraph (f), if the property is oxygen and nitrogen production equipment, dual-use electricity and heat equipment, project support equipment or equipment described in any of subparagraphs (c)(iv) to (vi) of the definition eligible clean hydrogen property in subsection (1) and that property is used in the production of hydrogen and ammonia, be allocated between two separate capital cost amounts, with each amount determined based on the percentage of the expected use of the equipment that is attributable to hydrogen production and ammonia production over the first 20 years of the project√¢¬Ä¬ôs operations, based on the project√¢¬Ä¬ôs most recent clean hydrogen project plan, and

Subsection.Text: Section 127.48 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (10):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Maximum capital cost of pyrolysis reactor system
Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this section, if a clean hydrogen project produces or is intended to produce hydrogen from the pyrolysis of eligible hydrocarbons,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the aggregate capital cost to a taxpayer of all eligible clean hydrogen property forming part of the pyrolysis reactor system of the project is deemed to be the lesser of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is $3,000, and
the project√¢¬Ä¬ôs expected hydrogen production as set out in the most recent clean hydrogen project plan in respect of the project, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the aggregate capital cost of all eligible clean hydrogen property forming part of the system, determined without reference to this subsection;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the taxpayer acquires a particular property forming part of the system and the acquisition causes the aggregate capital cost of the system to exceed the amount determined by the formula in subparagraph (a)(i), the capital cost of the particular property is deemed to be the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount determined by the formula in subparagraph (a)(i), and
is the aggregate capital cost of all other property forming part of the system; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: subject to subsection (10.2), if paragraph (b) has applied, the capital cost of each new property that the taxpayer acquires and that forms part of the system is deemed to be nil.

Subsection.Text: If the amount determined by the formula in paragraph (10.1)(a) in respect of a pyrolysis reactor system (in this subsection referred to as the √¢¬Ä¬úsystem capital cost limit√¢¬Ä¬ù) increases because of an increase to the expected hydrogen production of a taxpayer√¢¬Ä¬ôs clean hydrogen project, as indicated in a revised clean hydrogen project plan that is filed with the Minister of Natural Resources, the increased system capital cost limit shall apply only to property acquired after the date of the confirmation by the Minister of Natural Resources of the revised plan in accordance with paragraph (8)(b).

Subsection.MarginalNote: Pyrolysis system recapture √¢¬Ä¬î decrease in expected production
Subsection.Text: If, before the first day of the compliance period of a taxpayer√¢¬Ä¬ôs clean hydrogen project that is intended to produce hydrogen from the pyrolysis of eligible hydrocarbons, the expected hydrogen production included in a revised clean hydrogen project plan in respect of the project filed with the Minister of Natural Resources is less than the expected hydrogen production used to determine the most recent clean hydrogen tax credit in respect of the project, there shall be added to the taxpayer√¢¬Ä¬ôs tax otherwise payable under this Part the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the aggregate capital cost amount determined under paragraph (10.1)(a) using the expected hydrogen production included in the revised plan;
is the aggregate capital cost amount determined under paragraph (10.1)(a) used to determine the most recent clean hydrogen tax credit in respect of all property forming part of the system; and
is the clean hydrogen tax credit amount deducted in respect of all property forming part of the system.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 127.48(16)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any shutdown time of the project in respect of the year during which the project does not produce hydrogen;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 127.48(16)(d) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c.1)
Paragraph.Text: if the project produces hydrogen from the pyrolysis of eligible hydrocarbons, the end use of the solid carbon produced by the project during the year;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: for the compliance report in respect of the fifth operating year, a report prepared by a qualified verification firm in respect of the project that verifies

Subparagraph.Text: the actual carbon intensity of the hydrogen produced during each operating year of the compliance period, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if the project produces hydrogen from the pyrolysis of eligible hydrocarbons,

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the project√¢¬Ä¬ôs actual hydrogen use percentage, and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the end use of the solid carbon produced by the project during each operating year of the compliance period; and

Subsection.Text: Subsection 127.48(18) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Pyrolysis recapture √¢¬Ä¬î end of compliance period
Subsection.Text: If a qualified clean hydrogen project produces hydrogen from the pyrolysis of eligible hydrocarbons and, at the end of the project√¢¬Ä¬ôs compliance period, the project√¢¬Ä¬ôs actual hydrogen use percentage is less than 90%, or the project has consumed 50% or more of the hydrogen produced during the compliance period, the average actual carbon intensity of the project is deemed to be greater than 4.5.

In the taxation year of a taxpayer in which the compliance period of the taxpayer√¢¬Ä¬ôs qualified clean hydrogen project ends, if the average actual carbon intensity of the hydrogen produced is greater than the most recent expected carbon intensity that was used to determine a clean hydrogen tax credit in respect of the project, there shall be added to the taxpayer√¢¬Ä¬ôs tax otherwise payable under this Part for the taxation year an amount equal to the total of all amounts, each of which is determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the specified percentage that was applied to the capital cost of the eligible clean hydrogen property forming part of the project in determining a clean hydrogen tax credit of the taxpayer;
is the specified percentage that would have applied to the capital cost of the property if the expected carbon intensity were equal to the average actual carbon intensity of the project;
is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (a)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the property is part of a pyrolysis reactor system and subsection (10.1) has applied to limit the capital cost of any property forming part of the system, the capital cost amount in respect of the property determined under paragraph (22.1)(a), and
in any other case, the capital cost of the property on which the clean hydrogen tax credit was deducted; and

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total of all amounts, each of which can reasonably be considered to be the portion of any amount payable by the taxpayer because of subsection (22) in respect of the property.

Subsection.Text: The description of B in subsection 127.48(22) of the Act is replaced by the following:

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total of all amounts, each of which can reasonably be considered to be the portion of any amount payable by the taxpayer because of subsection (18) in respect of the property;

Subsection.Text: Section 127.48 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (22):
For the purposes of subsections (10.3), (18), (21) and (22), if subsection (10.1) has applied to limit the capital cost of any property forming part of a pyrolysis reactor system,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the capital cost of each property that is part of the system is deemed to be the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount determined in respect of the system under paragraph (10.1)(a),
is the capital cost of the property determined without reference to subsection (10.1) and this subsection, and
is the aggregate capital cost of all property forming part of the system, determined without reference to subsection (10.1) and this subsection; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the clean hydrogen tax credit in respect of the property referred to in paragraph (a) is deemed to be the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the deemed capital cost amount determined under paragraph (a), and
is the specified percentage in respect of the property.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 127.48(25) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subject to section 127.47, if subsection (12) has at any time applied to add an amount in computing the clean hydrogen tax credit of a current or former member of a partnership, subsections (10.3) and (17.1) to (23) apply to determine amounts in respect of the partnership as if the partnership were a taxable Canadian corporation, its fiscal period were its taxation year and it had deducted all of the clean hydrogen tax credits that were previously added in computing the clean hydrogen tax credit of any member of the partnership because of the application of subsection (12) in respect of its partnership interest.
Subsection 127.48(30) of the Act is replaced by the following:
For the purpose of applying subsection (2) in respect of a property acquired after the compliance period of a qualified clean hydrogen project of the taxpayer,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the expected carbon intensity of the project is deemed to be the greater of the expected carbon intensity otherwise determined and the average actual carbon intensity for the compliance period of the project; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the project produces hydrogen from the pyrolysis of eligible hydrocarbons, the expected hydrogen use percentage of the project is deemed to be equal to the actual hydrogen use percentage of the project.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 127.48(32) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Authority of the Minister of Natural Resources
Subsection.Text: For the purposes of determining whether a property is an eligible clean hydrogen property and whether a property is part of a pyrolysis reactor system, the Clean Hydrogen Investment Tax Credit √¢¬Ä¬ì Technical and Equipment Guidance Document published by the Department of Natural Resources is to apply conclusively with respect to engineering and scientific matters.
Subsections (3), (6), (8), (13), (15), (17), (18), (21), (27), (30), (32), (36) and (38) to (44) are deemed to have come into force on December 16, 2024.
Subsection (34) is deemed to have come into force on May 4, 2026.
Paragraph 127.49(5)(a) of the Act is amended by striking out √¢¬Ä¬úor√¢¬Ä¬ù at the end of subparagraph (ii.1) and by adding the following after that subparagraph:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii.2)
Subparagraph.Text: that is a qualified expenditure (as defined in subsection 127(9)) in respect of which an investment tax credit was deducted by any person, or

Subsection.Text: Subsections 127.49(13) and (14) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsections (11) and (12) do not apply to a taxpayer that is a taxable Canadian corporation (in this subsection and subsection (14) referred to as the √¢¬Ä¬útransferor√¢¬Ä¬ù) that disposes of a property to another taxable Canadian corporation (in this subsection and subsection (14) referred to as the √¢¬Ä¬úpurchaser√¢¬Ä¬ù) related to the transferor, if the purchaser acquired the property in circumstances where the property would be CTM property to the purchaser (but for paragraph (b) of the definition CTM property in subsection (1)) and is used by the purchaser for a CTM use.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Certain non-arm√¢¬Ä¬ôs length transfers √¢¬Ä¬î recapture deferred
Subsection.Text: If a property is disposed of to a purchaser in circumstances described in subsection (13),

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subsections (11) and (12) do not apply to the transferor in respect of the property after the time of the disposition; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for the purposes of subsections (11) and (12),

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the purchaser is deemed to have acquired the property, as a CTM property, on the date the property was acquired by the transferor,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the capital cost to the purchaser of the property on which a CTM investment tax credit was deducted is deemed to be the amount that was the capital cost to the transferor of the property on which the CTM investment tax credit was deducted, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the purchaser is deemed to have become entitled to a CTM investment tax credit in respect of the capital cost referred to in subparagraph (ii) equal to the amount of the transferor√¢¬Ä¬ôs CTM investment tax credit in respect of the property.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on May 4, 2026.
Subsection (2) applies in respect of property that is acquired and becomes available for use on or after May 4, 2026.
