Subsection.Text: Paragraph 127.491(9)(a) of the Act is amended by adding the following after subparagraph (ii):

Subparagraph.Label: (ii.1)
Subparagraph.Text: that is a qualified expenditure (as defined in subsection 127(9)) in respect of which an investment tax credit was deducted by any person,

Subsection.Text: Section 127.491 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (19):

If more than one person is required by this section to file any documentation or information with the Minister or the Minister of Natural Resources in respect of a qualified natural gas energy system (including, but not limited to, a system plan or a compliance report described in subsection (19)), the filing with full and accurate disclosure by any one of such persons of the documentation or information is deemed to have been made by each person to whom the relevant requirement applies.
Subsections 127.491(22) and (23) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsections (16) and (17) do not apply to a qualifying entity (in this subsection and subsection (23) referred to as the √¢¬Ä¬útransferor√¢¬Ä¬ù) that disposes of a property to another qualifying entity (in this subsection and subsection (23) referred to as the √¢¬Ä¬úpurchaser√¢¬Ä¬ù) that is related to the transferor if the purchaser acquired the property in circumstances where the property would be clean electricity property to the purchaser but for paragraph (c) of the definition clean electricity property in subsection (1).

Subsection.MarginalNote: Certain related party transfers √¢¬Ä¬î recapture deferred
Subsection.Text: If a property is disposed of to a purchaser in circumstances described in subsection (22),

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subsections (16) and (17) do not apply to the transferor in respect of the property after the time of the disposition; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for the purposes of subsections (16) and (17),

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the purchaser is deemed to have acquired the property, as a clean electricity property, on the date the property was acquired by the transferor,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the capital cost to the purchaser of the property on which a clean electricity investment tax credit was deducted is deemed to be the amount that was, before that time, the capital cost to the transferor of the property on which the clean electricity investment tax credit was deducted,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the purchaser is deemed to have become entitled to a clean electricity investment tax credit in respect of the capital cost referred to in subparagraph (ii) equal to the amount of the transferor√¢¬Ä¬ôs clean electricity investment tax credit in respect of the property, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: the purchaser is deemed to have paid any amount previously paid by the transferor because of subsection (18) in respect of the property.

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1), (4) and (6) are deemed to have come into force on April 16, 2024.
Subsections (2), (3) and (7) apply in respect of property that is acquired and becomes available for use on or after May 4, 2026.
Subsection (5) is deemed to have come into force on May 4, 2026.
Subsection 128(1) of the Act is amended by adding √¢¬Ä¬úand√¢¬Ä¬ù at the end of paragraph (e), by striking out √¢¬Ä¬úand√¢¬Ä¬ù at the end of paragraph (f) and by repealing paragraph (g).
Subsection (1) applies in respect of bankruptcy proceedings that are commenced on or after April 16, 2024.

Section.Text: Section 128.1 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (8):

Subsection.Text: Despite subsections 152(4) to (5), any assessment, reassessment or additional assessment of the tax payable by the individual in respect of the year that includes the particular time referred to in subsection (8) may be made by the Minister as is necessary to take into account the amount deducted from the proceeds of disposition of the property under subsection (8).
Subsection 129(1.2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Dividends deemed not to be taxable dividends
Subsection.Text: Where a dividend is paid on a share of the capital stock of a corporation and the share (or another share for which the share was substituted) was acquired by its holder in a transaction or as part of a series of transactions one of the main purposes of which was to enable the corporation (or another corporation affiliated with the corporation) to obtain a dividend refund, the dividend shall, for the purposes of subsections (1) and (1.3) to (1.32), be deemed not to be a taxable dividend.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Staggered year ends √¢¬Ä¬î dividend refund suspension
Subsection.Text: For the purpose of subsection (1) and subject to subsection (1.31), the amount of a dividend paid by a corporation (in this subsection and subsections (1.31) and (1.32) referred to as the √¢¬Ä¬úpayer corporation√¢¬Ä¬ù) √¢¬Ä¬î other than a dividend to which paragraph 55(3)(a) or (b) applies in the course of a reorganization √¢¬Ä¬î is deemed not to be a taxable dividend (in this section referred to as a √¢¬Ä¬úsuspended dividend√¢¬Ä¬ù) to the extent that the dividend is received, directly or indirectly through one or more trusts or partnerships, by another corporation (in subsections (1.31) and (1.32) referred to as a √¢¬Ä¬úpayee corporation√¢¬Ä¬ù) that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is affiliated with the payer corporation immediately before the time the dividend is paid;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is a private corporation or a subject corporation (as defined in subsection 186(3)); and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: received the dividend in a taxation year that ends after the taxation year of the payer corporation in which the dividend was paid.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1.3) does not apply to a dividend if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the following conditions are met:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: in the taxation year of the payee corporation in which the dividend was received, the payee corporation paid, on or before the balance-due day of the taxation year of the payer corporation in which the dividend was paid (in this subsection referred to as the √¢¬Ä¬úpayer-due day√¢¬Ä¬ù), one or more taxable dividends (in this subsection referred to as the √¢¬Ä¬úpayee dividend√¢¬Ä¬ù) that have the same character as the dividend, the total of which is greater than or equal to the product obtained (in this section referred to as the √¢¬Ä¬úsuspended portion√¢¬Ä¬ù) when the portion of the dividend refund of the payer corporation that would otherwise be determined under paragraph (1)(a) if this Act were read without reference to subsection (1.3), in respect of the dividend, is multiplied by 2.6 (in this subsection, in the case where that total is greater than the suspended portion, the excess is referred to as the √¢¬Ä¬úsurplus payee dividend√¢¬Ä¬ù),

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if applicable, in the taxation year of each corporation (in this subsection referred to as a √¢¬Ä¬úgrandparent corporation√¢¬Ä¬ù) that is affiliated with the payer corporation immediately before the time the dividend is paid and that received, directly or indirectly through one or more trusts, partnerships or other grandparent corporations, all or a portion of the payee dividend, the grandparent corporation paid, on or before the payer-due day, one or more taxable dividends (in this subsection referred to as the √¢¬Ä¬úgrandparent dividend√¢¬Ä¬ù) that have the same character as the dividend, the total of which is greater than or equal to that proportion of the suspended portion that the amount of the dividend received, directly or indirectly, by the grandparent corporation is of the payee dividend (in this subsection, in the case where that total is greater than that proportion of the suspended portion, the excess is referred to as the √¢¬Ä¬úsurplus grandparent dividend√¢¬Ä¬ù), and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: no portion of the payee dividend or the grandparent dividend in excess of the surplus payee dividend or the surplus grandparent dividend, as applicable, is otherwise relied on by any taxpayer to avoid the application of subsection (1.3); or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the payer corporation was subject to a loss restriction event

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: within 30 days after the payer corporation paid the dividend that, but for this paragraph, would be subject to subsection (1.3), or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: within 12 months after the payer corporation paid the dividend that, but for this paragraph, would be subject to subsection (1.3) and the dividend was paid in contemplation of the loss restriction event.

Subsection.Text: For the purpose of subsection (1), a payer corporation is deemed to have paid at the end of a particular taxation year a taxable dividend that is of the same character as a suspended dividend paid in a taxation year of the payer corporation preceding the particular taxation year and that is equal to the suspended portion if the following conditions are met:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: during the period beginning on the day on which the suspended dividend was paid and ending on the day on which the particular taxation year ends

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the payer corporation was not subject to a loss restriction event,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the payee corporation and, if applicable, each grandparent corporation (as referred to in subparagraph (1.31)(a)(ii) at the time subsection (1.3) applied to the payer corporation in respect of the suspended dividend) paid one or more taxable dividends that have the same character as the suspended dividend and the dividends (in this subsection referred to as the √¢¬Ä¬úparticular dividends√¢¬Ä¬ù) were received, directly or indirectly through one or more trusts, partnerships or grandparent corporations, by a taxpayer that was not

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: a corporation that was affiliated with the payer corporation immediately before the time the suspended dividend was paid, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: a private corporation or a subject corporation as defined in subsection 186(3) (in this subsection referred to as the √¢¬Ä¬úconnected corporation√¢¬Ä¬ù) that was connected, within the meaning assigned by subsection 186(4), with the payee corporation or a grandparent corporation at the time that the payee corporation or the grandparent corporation paid the taxable dividend unless

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: in the taxation year of the connected corporation in which the dividend was received, the connected corporation paid, by the end of the particular taxation year, one or more taxable dividends (in this subsection referred to as the √¢¬Ä¬úconnected dividend√¢¬Ä¬ù) that have the same character as the suspended dividend, the total of which is greater than or equal to that proportion of the suspended portion that the amount of the dividend received, directly or indirectly, by the connected corporation is of the dividend paid by the payee corporation or grandparent corporation (in this subsection referred to as the √¢¬Ä¬úconnected portion√¢¬Ä¬ù), and

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: if applicable, in the taxation year of each corporation (in this subsection referred to as a √¢¬Ä¬úconnected parent or grandparent corporation√¢¬Ä¬ù) that is connected, within the meaning assigned by subsection 186(4), with the connected corporation immediately before the time the connected dividend is paid and that received, directly or indirectly through one or more trusts, partnerships or connected parent or grandparent corporations, all or a portion of the connected dividend, the connected parent or grandparent corporation paid, by the end of the particular taxation year, one or more taxable dividends that have the same character as the suspended dividend, the total of which is greater than or equal to that proportion of the connected portion that the amount of the connected dividend received, directly or indirectly, by the connected parent or grandparent corporation is of the connected dividend, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the total amount of the particular dividends are greater than or equal to the suspended portion (with the total amount of any excess being referred to in this subsection as the √¢¬Ä¬úsurplus dividends√¢¬Ä¬ù) except in the case of particular dividends received by the connected corporation, the amount of the particular dividends is determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount of the connected dividend paid by the connected corporation to a taxpayer other than a taxpayer described in clause (ii)(A) or subclause (ii)(B)(II) in respect of the connected corporation, and

is the amount of a taxable dividend paid by the connected parent or grandparent corporation to a taxpayer other than a taxpayer described in clause (ii)(A) or clause (ii)(B) in respect of the connected parent or grandparent corporation;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: no portion of the taxable dividends described in subparagraph (a)(ii) exceeding the amount determined in subparagraph (a)(iii) in respect of the particular dividends in excess of the surplus dividends or exceeding the amount of the connected dividend in excess of the applicable connected portion (or in the case of a taxable dividend paid by a connected parent or grandparent corporation, exceeding the amount of the dividend paid in excess of their share of the applicable connected portion) is relied on by any taxpayer (other than the payer corporation in respect of the suspended dividend) to

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: obtain a dividend refund under subsection (1), or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: avoid the application of subsection (1.3); and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: this subsection has not previously applied in respect of the suspended dividend.

Subsection.Text: Despite subsections 152(4) to (5), such assessments, reassessments, determinations and redeterminations may be made as are necessary to give effect to subsections (1.3) to (1.32).
Subsection (1) applies to dividends paid in taxation years that begin on or after November 4, 2025.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 146.1(2.3) of the Act is amended by striking out √¢¬Ä¬úand√¢¬Ä¬ù at the end of paragraph (a), by adding √¢¬Ä¬úand√¢¬Ä¬ù at the end of paragraph (b) and by adding the following after paragraph (b):

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a designation of the beneficiary under the plan if the subscriber is a designated subscriber.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 146.1(5) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

No tax is payable under this Part by a trust that is governed by a RESP on its taxable income for a taxation year, except that, if at any time in the taxation year, it holds a property that is a non-qualified investment (as defined in subsection 207.01(1)) for the trust, tax is payable under this Part by the trust on the amount that would be its taxable income for the taxation year if it had no income or losses from sources other than non-qualified investments, and no capital gains or capital losses other than from dispositions of non-qualified investments, and for that purpose,
Section 146.1 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (7.2):
If an education savings plan is entered into between a promoter and a designated subscriber, the registration conditions in respect of the plan are modified as follows:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case where a subscriber acquires the rights of the designated subscriber under the plan, the Social Insurance Number of the beneficiary must be provided to the promoter before educational assistance payments may be made to or for the beneficiary; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: throughout the period during which the subscriber is a designated subscriber

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: paragraph (2)(d.1) does not apply to an accumulated income payment to the designated subscriber,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: paragraph (2)(l) does not apply to the plan,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the plan does not allow contributions on behalf of the beneficiary, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: the plan does not allow for the payment of educational assistance payments to or for the beneficiary.

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (7) come into force on January 1, 2027.

Subsection.Text: If a trust that is governed by a RRIF holds, at any time in a taxation year, a property that is a non-qualified investment (as defined in subsection 207.01(1)),

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 146.3(14)(b) of the Act is amended by striking out √¢¬Ä¬úor√¢¬Ä¬ù at the end of subparagraph (i), by adding √¢¬Ä¬úor√¢¬Ä¬ù at the end of subparagraph (ii) and by adding the following after subparagraph (ii):

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)

Subsection.Text: Subsection 146.3(14.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:
An amount is transferred from a registered retirement income fund of an annuitant in accordance with this subsection if the amount is transferred at the direction of the annuitant directly to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an account of the annuitant under a pooled registered pension plan;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a registered pension plan of which, at any time before the transfer, the annuitant was a member (as defined in subsection 147.1(1)) or to a specified pension plan and is allocated to the annuitant under a money purchase provision (as defined in subsection 147.1(1)) of the plan;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a licensed annuities provider to acquire an advanced life deferred annuity for the benefit of the annuitant; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a registered pension plan, not exceeding the amount necessary to fund additional benefits becoming provided to the annuitant under a defined benefit provision (as defined in subsection 147.1(1)) of the plan, as a consequence of a past service event (as defined in subsection 147.1(1)).

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) to (3), (5), (6) and (8) to (10) come into force on January 1, 2027.
Subsection (7) is deemed to have come into force on August 4, 2023.
Subsection (12) is deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2025.

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) to (5) come into force on January 1, 2027.
Subparagraph (c)(ii) of the definition advanced life deferred annuity in subsection 146.5(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: are payable for the life of the annuitant or for the lives, jointly, of the annuitant and spouse or common-law partner or former spouse or common-law partner of the annuitant;

Clause.Label: (C)

Clause.Text: are increased on an actuarially equivalent basis in lieu of payments that would have otherwise been payable to the current or former spouse or common-law partner if the spouse or common-law partner is no longer entitled to an amount under the contract;

Subsection.Text: Section 146.5 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (4):

The amount of a payment described in clause (g.1)(ii)(A) or (B) of the definition advanced life deferred annuity in subsection (1) that is paid to an individual shall be included in the income of the individual for the year of the payment.

If an amount is refunded or paid in circumstances described in clause (g)(ii)(B) or (g.1)(ii)(C) of the definition advanced life deferred annuity in subsection (1),

Section.Text: The portion of subsection 147.1(12) of the Act after paragraph (b) is replaced by the following:

Provision.Text: give notice (in this subsection and subsection 147.1(13) referred to as a √¢¬Ä¬únotice of revocation√¢¬Ä¬ù) by registered mail to the plan administrator that the registration of the plan is revoked as of the date specified in the notice of revocation, which date may not be earlier than the date specified in the notice of intent or, if there is no notice of intent, the administrator√¢¬Ä¬ôs application.

Subsection.Text: Section 147.2 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (8):
Subsection (10) applies to a member under a defined benefit provision of a registered pension plan if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the member was an employee of a participating employer under the provision;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the participating employer has permanently ceased to make contributions under the provision;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the member is not an employee of one of the other participating employers under the provision; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the plan is not an individual pension plan (as defined in subsection 8300(1) of the Income Tax Regulations).

Subsection.Text: If this subsection applies to a member of a defined benefit provision of a registered pension plan, then for the purposes of this section and any regulations made under subsection 147.1(18) in respect of eligible contributions, the member is deemed to be a former employee of each participating employer under the provision.
Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on August 15, 2025.
The definition member in subsection 147.5(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: member, of a pooled pension plan, means an individual (other than a trust) who is a member of the plan under the Pooled Registered Pension Plans Act or a similar law of a province.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(participant)

Subsection.Text: Section 147.5 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (13):

If at any time in a taxation year a particular amount in respect of a member√¢¬Ä¬ôs account under a pooled registered pension plan that is a spousal or common-law partner plan (as defined in subsection 146(1)) in relation to a taxpayer is required to be included in the income of the taxpayer√¢¬Ä¬ôs spouse or common-law partner and the taxpayer is not living separate and apart from the taxpayer√¢¬Ä¬ôs spouse or common-law partner at that time by reason of the breakdown of their marriage or common-law partnership, there shall be included at that time in computing the taxpayer√¢¬Ä¬ôs income for the year an amount equal to the lesser of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the total of all amounts each of which is a premium (as defined in subsection 146(1)) paid by the taxpayer in the year or in one of the two immediately preceding taxation years to a registered retirement savings plan under which the taxpayer√¢¬Ä¬ôs spouse or common-law partner was the annuitant (as defined in subsection 146(1)) at the time the premium was paid, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the particular amount.

Text.Ins: Subsections 146(8.5) and (8.6) and paragraphs 146(8.7)(a) and (b) apply to a premium described in subsection (13.1) except that the references in subsections 146(8.5) to (8.7) to √¢¬Ä¬úsubsection 146(8.3)√¢¬Ä¬ù shall be read as references to √¢¬Ä¬úsubsection 147.5(13.1)√¢¬Ä¬ù.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (2) is deemed to have come into force on August 15, 2025.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 149.1(1.1)(d) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: expenditures on administration, management and fundraising of the charity.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 149.1(6.3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Minister may, by notice sent by registered mail, or electronically if authorized in accordance with subsection 244(14.3), to a registered charity, on the Minister√¢¬Ä¬ôs own initiative or on application made to the Minister in prescribed form, designate the charity to be a charitable organization, private foundation or public foundation and the charity shall be deemed to be registered as a charitable organization, private foundation or public foundation, as the case may be, for taxation years commencing after the day of mailing or sending of the notice unless and until it is otherwise designated under this subsection or its registration is revoked under subsection (2), (3), (4) or (4.1) or 168(2).
Section 149.1 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (14.1):

Every registered foreign charity must, within six months after the end of each taxation year of the charity and without notice or demand, file with the Minister a public information return for the year in prescribed form and containing prescribed information.
Paragraph 149.1(15)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the information contained in a public information return referred to in subsection (14), (14.1) or (14.2), and the filing status of information returns required by that subsection, must be communicated or otherwise made available to the public by the Minister in such manner as the Minister considers appropriate;

Subsection.Text: Subsections 149.1(22) and (23) of the Act are replaced by the following:

The Minister may by mail, or electronically if authorized in accordance with subsection 244(14.3), give notice to a person that the application of the person for registration as a qualified donee referred to in subparagraph (a)(i) or (iii) or any of paragraphs (b) to (c) of the definition qualified donee in subsection (1) is refused.

The Minister may by registered mail, or electronically if authorized in accordance with subsection 244(14.3), give notice to a person that the registration of the person as a registered charity is annulled and deemed not to have been so registered, if the person was so registered by the Minister in error or the person has, solely as a result of a change in law, ceased to be a charity.
The portion of subsection 149.1(26) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

For the purposes of subparagraph (a)(v) of the definition qualified donee in subsection (1), the Minister may register, in consultation with the Minister of Finance, a foreign charity for a 36-month period that includes the time at which His Majesty in right of Canada has made a gift to the foreign charity, if
Subsections (1) and (4) are deemed to have come into force on April 17, 2024 and apply to taxation years that begin on or after that day.
Subsection (7) applies to registrations after April 16, 2024.
Paragraph 150(1.1)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the taxpayer was a registered charity throughout the year; or

Subsection.Text: Section 150 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1.4):

The Minister may file a return of income for a taxation year of an individual (other than a trust or a deceased individual) resident in Canada throughout the year if the Minister is satisfied at the time of filing that the following conditions are met:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the individual has not notified the Minister not to file a return of income for the year on the individual√¢¬Ä¬ôs behalf;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the individual has not filed a return of income for at least one of the three immediate preceding taxation years;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the individual has not filed a return of income for the year on or before the day that is 45 days after the filing-due date for the year;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: all of the individual√¢¬Ä¬ôs income for the year, if any, was reported in an information return filed with the Minister for the year;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the individual√¢¬Ä¬ôs taxable income for the year does not exceed the lesser of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount determined under subsection 118(1.1) for the year,
is the amount, if any, that may be deducted by the individual for the year under subsection 118(2), and
is the amount, if any, that may be deducted by the individual for the year under subsection 118.3(1), and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is, in the case of an individual resident at the end of the year, in

FormulaParagraph.Label: (A)
FormulaParagraph.Text: Quebec, the amount determined for A in subparagraph (i), or

FormulaParagraph.Label: (B)
FormulaParagraph.Text: a province other than Quebec, the amount, if any, determined for the year under provincial income tax law similar in substance to subsection 118(1.1),

FormulaDefinition.Text: is, in the case of an individual resident at the end of the year, in

FormulaParagraph.Label: (A)
FormulaParagraph.Text: Quebec, the amount determined for B in subparagraph (i), or

FormulaParagraph.Label: (B)
FormulaParagraph.Text: a province other than Quebec, the amount, if any, determined for the year under provincial income tax law similar in substance to subsection 118(2), and

FormulaDefinition.Text: is, in the case of an individual resident at the end of the year, in

FormulaParagraph.Label: (A)
FormulaParagraph.Text: Quebec, the amount determined for C in subparagraph (i), or

FormulaParagraph.Label: (B)
FormulaParagraph.Text: a province other than Quebec, the amount, if any, determined for the year under provincial income tax law similar in substance to subsection 118.3(1);

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister has notified the individual of the information on file with the Minister for the year and the individual has not, within 90 days after the day on which the notice is mailed or sent electronically by the Minister,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: otherwise filed a return of income for the year, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: notified the Minister of corrections to be made to the information on file with the Minister that would cause any of the other conditions in this subsection not to be satisfied;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: the individual has not become a bankrupt in the year; and

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: the individual meets such other conditions as designated by the Minister, if any.

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (1.5), for the purposes of this Act

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a return of income for a taxation year of an individual is deemed to have been filed by the individual at the time of its filing by the Minister in accordance with subsection (1.5); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for greater certainty, any erroneous statement or omission in the return that is attributable to the individual√¢¬Ä¬ôs failure to notify the Minister, within the 90-day period described in paragraph (1.5)(f) or at any other time prior to the filing of the return, of any correction to be made to the information on file with the Minister is a misrepresentation made by the individual in filing the return or in supplying any information under this Act.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (2) applies to the 2025 and subsequent taxation years.
Subsection 150.1(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If a return of income of a taxpayer for a taxation year is filed by way of electronic filing by a particular person (in this subsection referred to as the √¢¬Ä¬úfiler√¢¬Ä¬ù) other than the person who is required to file the return, or the Minister if the return is filed pursuant to subsection 150(1.5), the person who is required to file the return shall make an information return in prescribed form containing prescribed information, retain a copy of it and provide the filer with the information return, and that return and the copy shall be deemed to be a record referred to in section 230 in respect of the filer and the other person.
Subsection (1) applies to the 2025 and subsequent taxation years.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 160(5) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

For the purposes of this section, if a person (referred to in this section as the √¢¬Ä¬útransferor√¢¬Ä¬ù) has transferred property either directly or indirectly, by means of a trust or by any other means whatever to another person (referred to in this section as the √¢¬Ä¬útransferee√¢¬Ä¬ù) in a transaction or as part of a series of transactions
Section 160 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (5):
Subsection (7) applies in respect of a transaction or series of transactions if, as part of the transaction or series of transactions,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a person (in this subsection referred to as the √¢¬Ä¬úplanner√¢¬Ä¬ù) has transferred property, either directly or indirectly, by means of a trust or by any other means whatever, to a person (in this subsection referred to as the √¢¬Ä¬útransferee√¢¬Ä¬ù) or a person not dealing at arm√¢¬Ä¬ôs length with the transferee, in accordance with the direction of, or with the concurrence of the transferee;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: another person (in this subsection referred to as the √¢¬Ä¬útransferor√¢¬Ä¬ù) has transferred a property (in this subsection referred to as the √¢¬Ä¬úparticular property√¢¬Ä¬ù), either directly or indirectly, by means of a trust or by any other means whatever, to the planner or any other person; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: it is reasonable to conclude that one of the purposes for undertaking or arranging the transaction or series of transactions is to avoid joint and several, or solidary, liability of the transferee and transferor for an amount payable under this Act.

Subsection.Text: If this subsection applies in respect of a transaction or series of transactions, for the purposes of this section, the transferor (within the meaning of subsection (6)) is deemed to have transferred the particular property to the transferee (within the meaning of subsection (6)) as part of the transaction or series of transactions.

If a transaction or series of transactions is a section 160 avoidance transaction (as defined in subsection 160.01(1)), in determining the amount the transferee and transferor are jointly and severally, or solidarily, liable to pay under this section, the fair market value of the consideration given, if any, by the transferee for any transferred property is deemed to be nil if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the transaction or series of transactions is described in paragraph (a) or (c) of the definition section 160 avoidance transaction in subsection 160.01(1); or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: it is reasonable to conclude that one of the purposes for undertaking or arranging the transaction or series of transactions is to avoid joint and several, or solidary, liability of the transferee and transferor for an amount payable under this Act.

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (2) apply in respect of a transaction or series of transactions that occurs on or after April 16, 2024.

transferor√¢¬Ä¬Çhas the meaning assigned by subsections 160(1), (5) and (7).√¢¬Ä¬Ç(auteur du transfert)

Subsection.Text: The Minister may by registered mail, or electronically if authorized in accordance with subsection 244(14.3), give notice to a person described in any of paragraphs (a) to (c) of the definition qualified donee in subsection 149.1(1) that the Minister proposes to revoke its registration if the person

Subsection.Text: Subsection 168(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:
If the Minister gives notice under subsection (1) to a registered charity, a registered Canadian amateur athletic association or a registered journalism organization,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if it has applied to the Minister in writing for the revocation of its registration, the Minister shall, forthwith after the mailing or sending of the notice, publish a copy of the notice on an internet webpage of the Government of Canada, and on that publication of a copy of the notice, the registration is revoked;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in any other case, the Minister may, after the expiration of 30 days from the day of mailing or sending of the notice, or after the expiration of such extended period from the day of mailing or sending of the notice as the Federal Court of Appeal or a judge of that Court, on application made at any time before the determination of any appeal pursuant to subsection 172(3) from the giving of the notice, may fix or allow, publish a copy of the notice on an internet webpage of the Government of Canada, and on that publication of a copy of the notice, the registration is revoked; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister shall maintain a permanent record of the notice and make the notice available to the public in such manner as the Minister considers appropriate.

A person may, on or before the day that is 90 days after the day on which the notice was mailed or sent, serve on the Minister a written notice of objection in the manner authorized by the Minister, setting out the reasons for the objection and all the relevant facts, and the provisions of subsections 165(1), (1.1) and (3) to (7) and sections 166, 166.1 and 166.2 apply, with any modifications that the circumstances require, as if the notice were a notice of assessment made under section 152, if

Definition.Text: qualified investment√¢¬Ä¬Çfor a trust governed by a registered plan means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: money (other than money the fair market value of which exceeds its stated value as legal tender in the country of issuance or money that is held for its numismatic value) and deposits (within the meaning assigned by the Canada Deposit Insurance Corporation Act or with a branch in Canada of a bank) of such money standing to the credit of the trust;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a guaranteed investment certificate issued by a trust company incorporated under the laws of Canada or of a province;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a bond, debenture, note or similar obligation

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: described in paragraph (a) of the definition fully exempt interest in subsection 212(3),

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: issued by a corporation, mutual fund trust or limited partnership the shares or units of which are listed on a designated stock exchange in Canada,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: issued by a corporation the shares of which are listed on a designated stock exchange outside Canada,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: issued by an authorized foreign bank and payable at a branch in Canada of the bank,

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: that was issued as part of a single issue of debt of at least $25 million and has an investment grade rating from a prescribed credit rating agency (or had such a rating at the time it was acquired by the trust),

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: that was issued on a continuous basis under a debt issuance program, provided that at least $25 million of debt under the program had been issued and is outstanding and it has an investment grade rating from a prescribed credit rating agency (or had such a rating at the time it was acquired by the trust),

Subparagraph.Label: (vii)
Subparagraph.Text: that was acquired by the trust in exchange for a bond, debenture, note or similar obligation that satisfied the condition in subparagraph (v) or (vi) as part of a proposal or an arrangement that has been approved by a court under the Bankruptcy and Insolvency Act or the Companies√¢¬Ä¬ô Creditors Arrangement Act, or

Subparagraph.Label: (viii)
Subparagraph.Text: issued by a public corporation (other than a mortgage investment corporation);

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a security (other than a future contract or other derivative instrument in respect of which the holder√¢¬Ä¬ôs risk of loss may exceed the holder√¢¬Ä¬ôs cost) that is listed on a designated stock exchange;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: a share of the capital stock of a public corporation (other than a mortgage investment corporation);

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: an investment contract described in subparagraph (b)(ii) of the definition retirement savings plan in subsection 146(1) and issued by a corporation approved by the Governor in Council for the purposes of that subparagraph;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: a contract for an annuity issued by a licensed annuities provider if

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the trust is the only person that, disregarding a subsequent transfer of the contract by the trust, is or may become entitled to an annuity payment under the contract, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the holder of the contract has a right to surrender the contract at any time for an amount that would, if reasonable sales and administration charges were ignored, approximate the value of funds that could otherwise be applied to fund future periodic payments under the contract;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: if the registered plan is a RESP, an investment that was acquired by the trust before October 28, 1998;

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: if the registered plan is a RDSP, a contract for an annuity issued by a licensed annuities provider if

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: annual or more frequent periodic payments are or may be made under the contract to the holder of the contract,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the trust is the only person who, disregarding any subsequent transfer of the contract by the trust, is or may become entitled to any annuity payments under the contract,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: neither the time nor the amount of any payment under the contract may vary because of the length of any life, other than the life of the beneficiary under the RDSP (in this paragraph referred to as the √¢¬Ä¬úRDSP beneficiary√¢¬Ä¬ù),

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: the day on which the periodic payments began or are to begin is not later than the end of the later of

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the year in which the RDSP beneficiary attains the age of 60 years, and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the year following the year in which the contract was acquired by the trust,

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: the periodic payments are payable for the life of the RDSP beneficiary and either there is no guaranteed period under the contract or there is a guaranteed period that does not exceed 15 years,

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: the periodic payments

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: are equal, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: are not equal solely because of one or more adjustments that would, if the contract were an annuity under a retirement savings plan, be in accordance with subparagraphs 146(3)(b)(iii) to (v) or that arise because of a uniform reduction in the entitlement to the periodic payments as a consequence of a partial surrender of rights to the periodic payments, and

Subparagraph.Label: (vii)
Subparagraph.Text: the contract requires that, in the event the plan must be terminated in accordance with paragraph 146.4(4)(p), any amounts that would otherwise be payable after the termination be commuted into a single payment;

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: if the registered plan is a RRIF, a contract for an annuity issued by a licensed annuities provider if

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: annual or more frequent periodic payments are or may be made under the contract to the holder of the contract,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the trust is the only person who, disregarding any subsequent transfer of the contract by the trust, is or may become entitled to any annuity payments under the contract,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: neither the time nor the amount of any payment under the contract may vary because of the length of any life, other than

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: if the annuitant under the RRIF (in this paragraph referred to as the √¢¬Ä¬úRRIF annuitant√¢¬Ä¬ù) has made the election referred to in the definition retirement income fund in subsection 146.3(1) in respect of the fund and a spouse or common-law partner, the life of the RRIF annuitant or the life of the spouse or common-law partner, and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: in any other case, the life of the RRIF annuitant,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: the day on which the periodic payments began or are to begin (in this paragraph referred to as the √¢¬Ä¬ústart date√¢¬Ä¬ù) is not later than the end of the year following the year in which the contract was acquired by the trust,

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: either

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the periodic payments are payable for the life of the RRIF annuitant or the joint lives of the RRIF annuitant and the RRIF annuitant√¢¬Ä¬ôs spouse or common-law partner and either there is no guaranteed period under the contract or there is a guaranteed period that begins at the start date and does not exceed a term equal to 90 years minus the lesser of

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: the age in whole years at the start date of the RRIF annuitant (determined on the assumption that the RRIF annuitant is alive at the start date), and

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: the age in whole years at the start date of a spouse or common-law partner of the RRIF annuitant (determined on the assumption that a spouse or common-law partner of the RRIF annuitant at the time the contract was acquired is a spouse or common-law partner of the RRIF annuitant at the start date), or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the periodic payments are payable for a term equal to

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: 90 years minus the age described in subclause (A)(I), or

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: 90 years minus the age described in subclause (A)(II), and

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: the periodic payments

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: are equal, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: are not equal solely because of one or more adjustments that would, if the contract were an annuity under a retirement savings plan, be in accordance with subparagraphs 146(3)(b)(iii) to (v) or that arise because of a uniform reduction in the entitlement to the periodic payments as a consequence of a partial surrender of rights to the periodic payments;

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: if the registered plan is a RRSP, a contract for an annuity issued by a licensed annuities provider if it

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: is described in the definition retirement income in subsection 146(1) in respect of the annuitant under the RRSP (in this paragraph referred to as the √¢¬Ä¬úRRSP annuitant√¢¬Ä¬ù), or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: meets the following conditions:

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: annual or more frequent periodic payments are or may be made under the contract to the holder of the contract,

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the trust is the only person who, disregarding any subsequent transfer of the contract by the trust, is or may become entitled to any annuity payments under the contract,

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: neither the time nor the amount of any payment under the contract may vary because of the length of any life, other than the life of the RRSP annuitant,

Clause.Label: (D)
Clause.Text: the day on which the periodic payments began or are to begin (in this subparagraph referred to as the √¢¬Ä¬ústart date√¢¬Ä¬ù) is not later than the end of the year in which the RRSP annuitant attains 72 years of age,

Clause.Label: (E)
Clause.Text: either

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: the periodic payments are payable for the life of the RRSP annuitant and either there is no guaranteed period under the contract or there is a guaranteed period that begins at the start date and does not exceed a term equal to 90 years minus the lesser of

Subsubclause.Text: the age in whole years at the start date of the RRSP annuitant (determined on the assumption that the RRSP annuitant is alive at the start date), and

the age in whole years at the start date of a spouse or common-law partner of the RRSP annuitant (determined on the assumption that a spouse or common-law partner of the RRSP annuitant at the time the contract was acquired is a spouse or common-law partner of the RRSP annuitant at the start date), or

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (c) of the definition controlling individual in subsection 207.01(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs (a) and (b) of the definition excluded property in subsection 207.01(1) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: property described in subparagraph 5001(h)(ii) of the Income Tax Regulations;

Paragraph.Text: an equity of a mutual fund corporation or mutual fund trust if

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: either

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the equity is equity of a mutual fund corporation or mutual fund trust that derives all or substantially all its value from one or more mutual funds that are subject to, and substantially comply with, the requirements of National Instrument 81-102 Investment Funds, as amended from time to time, of the Canadian Securities Administrators, or

Clause.Label: (B)

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the time is

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: during the 24-month period that begins on the day on which the first taxation year of the corporation or trust begins,

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: during the 24-month period that ends on the day on which the last taxation year of the corporation or trust ends, or

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: where the equity is a share of the capital stock of a mutual fund corporation and the share derives all or substantially all its value from a particular mutual fund,

Subclause.Label: (I)

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: during the 24-month period that ends on the day on which the particular mutual fund is terminated,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: it is reasonable to conclude that none of the main purposes of the structure of the corporation or trust, or of the terms and conditions of the equity, is to accommodate transactions or events that could affect the fair market value of the property held by the trust governed by the registered plan in a manner that would not occur in a normal commercial or investment context in which parties deal with each other at arm√¢¬Ä¬ôs length and act prudently, knowledgeably and willingly, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)

If an approved share of the capital stock of a registered labour-sponsored venture capital corporation or a revoked corporation is, before the first discontinuation of its venture capital business, redeemed, acquired or cancelled by the corporation less than eight years after the day on which the share was issued (other than in circumstances described in subclause 204.81(1)(c)(v)(A)(I) or (III) or clause 204.81(1)(c)(v)(B) or (D) or other than if the share is a Class A share of the capital stock of the corporation that is exchanged for another Class A share of the capital stock of the corporation as part of a qualifying exchange) or any other share that was issued by any other prescribed labour-sponsored venture capital corporation is disposed of, the person who was the shareholder immediately before the redemption, acquisition, cancellation or disposition shall pay a tax under this Part equal to the lesser of

Section.Text: Subsection 212(17.1) of the Act is repealed.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 220(3.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Minister may, on or before the day that is 10 calendar years after the end of a taxation year of a taxpayer (or in the case of a partnership, a fiscal period of the partnership) or on application by the taxpayer or partnership on or before that day, waive or cancel all or any portion of any penalty or interest otherwise payable under this Act (other than a penalty under section 231.7 or any interest payable in respect of that penalty) by the taxpayer or partnership in respect of that taxation year or fiscal period, and notwithstanding subsections 152(4) to (5), any assessment of the interest and penalties payable by the taxpayer or partnership shall be made that is necessary to take into account the cancellation of the penalty or interest.
The portion of subsection 220(3.3) of the French version of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Date pr√É¬©sum√É¬©e d√¢¬Ä¬ôun choix modifi√É¬©, annul√É¬© ou produit en retard
Subsection.Text: Lorsque le ministre, en vertu du paragraphe (3.2), proroge le d√É¬©lai pour faire un choix ou permet qu√¢¬Ä¬ôun choix soit modifi√É¬© ou annul√É¬©, les pr√É¬©somptions suivantes s√¢¬Ä¬ôappliquent√¢¬Ä¬Ø:

Section.Text: The portion of section 231 of the Act before the first definition is replaced by the following:
In sections 231.1 to 231.9,

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 231.1(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

An authorized person may, at all reasonable times, for any purpose related to the administration or enforcement of this Act (including the collection of any amount payable under this Act by any person), of a listed international agreement or, for greater certainty, of a tax treaty with another country,

Subsection 231.1(1) of the Act is amended by striking out √¢¬Ä¬úand√¢¬Ä¬ù at the end of paragraph (d), by adding √¢¬Ä¬úand√¢¬Ä¬ù at the end of paragraph (e) and by adding the following after paragraph (e):

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: subject to subsection (4), require a taxpayer or any other person to provide and deliver, in a reasonable manner, within a reasonable period of time,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: any information or additional information, including a return of income or a supplementary return, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: any document.

Subsection.Text: Section 231.1 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (3):

An authorized person shall not impose on a taxpayer or any other person a requirement under paragraph (1)(f) to provide information or any document relating to one or more unnamed persons for which an application under subsection 231.2(3) would be required if the information or document were sought under a notice of requirement under section 231.2.
The portion of subsection 231.2(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Requirement to provide documents or information

Subsection.Text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this Act, the Minister may, subject to subsection (2), for any purpose related to the administration or enforcement of this Act (including the collection of any amount payable under this Act by any person), of a listed international agreement or, for greater certainty, of a tax treaty with another country, by notice sent or served in accordance with subsection (1.1), require that any person provide, within such reasonable time and in such reasonable manner as is stipulated in the notice,
Paragraph 231.2(3)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the requirement is made

Subparagraph.Text: to verify compliance by the person or persons in the group with any duty or obligation under this Act, or

Text.Ins: for a purpose related to the administration of a listed international agreement or, for greater certainty, of a tax treaty with another country.

Section.Text: Section 231.5 of the Act is replaced by the following:

If any document is seized, inspected, audited, examined or provided under any of sections 231.1 to 231.4 and 231.6, the person by whom it is seized, inspected, audited or examined or to whom it is provided or any officer of the Canada Revenue Agency may make, or cause to be made, one or more copies thereof and, in the case of an electronic document, make or cause to be made, a print-out of the electronic document, and any document purporting to be certified by the Minister or an authorized person to be a copy of the document, or to be a print-out of an electronic document, made under this section is evidence of the nature and content of the original document and has the same probative force as the original document would have if it were proven in the ordinary way.

No person shall, physically or otherwise, interfere with, hinder or molest an official (as defined in subsection 241(10)) doing anything that the official is authorized to do under this Act or attempt to interfere with, hinder or molest any official doing or prevent or attempt to prevent an official from doing, anything that the official is authorized to do under this Act, and every person shall, unless the person is unable to do so, do everything that the person is required to do by or under sections 231.1 to 231.6.

Subsection.Text: Subsections 231.6(1) and (2) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Definition of foreign-based information or document

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this section, foreign-based information or document means any information or document that is available or located outside Canada and that may be relevant to the administration or enforcement of this Act (including the collection of any amount payable under this Act by any person), of a listed international agreement or, for greater certainty, of a tax treaty with another country.

Notwithstanding any other provision of this Act, the Minister may, by notice sent or served in accordance with subsection (3.1), require that a person resident in Canada or a non-resident person carrying on business in Canada provide and deliver, within such reasonable time and in such reasonable manner as is stipulated in the notice, any foreign-based information or document.
Paragraph 231.6(3)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a reasonable period of time of not less than 90 days after the notice is sent or served for the production of the information or document;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 231.6(3)(c) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: c)
Paragraph.Text: pr√É¬©ciser les cons√É¬©quences pr√É¬©vues au paragraphe (8) du d√É¬©faut de fournir les renseignements ou documents √É¬©trangers recherch√É¬©s dans le d√É¬©lai pr√É¬©vu dans l√¢¬Ä¬ôavis.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 231.6(5) of the Act is amended by adding √¢¬Ä¬úor√¢¬Ä¬ù at the end of paragraph (a) and by replacing paragraphs (b) and (c) with the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: vary or set aside the requirement if the judge determines that the requirement is unreasonable.

Subsection.Text: Subsections 231.6(6) and (7) of the Act are replaced by the following:

For the purposes of paragraph (5)(b), the requirement to provide the information or document shall not be considered to be unreasonable because the information or document is under the control of or available to a non-resident person that is not controlled by the person who is sent or served with the notice of the requirement under subsection (2) if that person is related to the non-resident person.
Subsection 231.6(8) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If a person fails to comply substantially with a notice sent or served under subsection (2) and if the notice is not set aside by a judge under subsection (5), any court having jurisdiction in a civil proceeding relating to the administration or enforcement of this Act, of a listed international agreement or, for greater certainty, of a tax treaty with another country, shall, on motion of the Minister, prohibit the introduction by that person of any foreign-based information or document covered by that notice.
Subsection 231.7(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

On summary application by the Minister, a judge may, notwithstanding subsection 238(2), order a person to provide any access, assistance, information or document sought by the Minister under section 231.1, 231.2 or 231.6, and answer all questions either orally or in writing as required by paragraph 231.1(1)(d), if the judge is satisfied that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the person was required under

Subparagraph.Text: section 231.1, 231.2 or 231.6 to provide the access, assistance, information or document and did not do so, or
paragraph 231.1(1)(d) to answer questions orally or in writing and the person did not do so; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of information, a document or an answer to a question, the information, document or answer is not protected from disclosure by solicitor-client privilege.

Subsection.Text: Section 231.7 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (5):

If an order under subsection (1) is made in respect of a taxpayer√¢¬Ä¬ôs failure to comply with a requirement under section 231.1, 231.2 or 231.6 in respect of a taxation year of the taxpayer, the taxpayer is, in addition to any penalty otherwise provided, liable to a penalty equal to 10% of the aggregate amount of tax payable by the taxpayer under this Act for each taxation year of the taxpayer to which the order relates.
Subsection (6) does not apply

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: to an order made in respect of a taxpayer√¢¬Ä¬ôs failure to comply with a requirement to provide information or documents or to answer questions, if one of the reasons for not complying with the requirement was the taxpayer√¢¬Ä¬ôs reasonable belief that the information, documents or answers were protected from disclosure by solicitor-client privilege; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the amount of tax payable by the taxpayer under this Act for each taxation year to which the order under subsection (1) relates is less than $50,000.

Subsection.Text: The Minister may apply for a compliance order under subsection (1) before or after sending a notice described under subsection 231.9(1).

The Minister may at any time assess any amount payable under subsection (6) by any person and, if the Minister makes such an assessment, the provisions of Divisions I and J of Part I apply, with such modifications as the circumstances require, in respect of the assessment as though it had been made under section 152.

MarginalNote.Ins: Requirement to vacate or vary assessment

Subsection.Text: If the Minister determines that a penalty under subsection (6) is disproportionate or unfair, the Minister shall waive or cancel all or part of the penalty (and any related interest) that the Minister considers is appropriate having regard to all the circumstances and, despite subsections 152(4) to (5), any assessment of the penalty and interest payable shall be made that is necessary to take into account the cancellation of all or part of the penalty or interest.

Section.Text: Section 231.8 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The following periods of time shall not be counted in the computation of the period of time within which an assessment may be made for a taxation year of a taxpayer under subsection 152(4):

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the taxpayer, or a person that does not deal at arm√¢¬Ä¬ôs length with the taxpayer, is required to do something under subsection 231.1(1) in respect of the taxation year of the taxpayer, the period of time between the day on which an application for judicial review in respect of the requirement is made and the day on which the application is finally disposed of;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the taxpayer, or a person that does not deal at arm√¢¬Ä¬ôs length with the taxpayer, is sent or served with a notice of a requirement under subsection 231.2(1) in respect of the taxation year of the taxpayer, the period of time between the day on which an application for judicial review in respect of the requirement is made and the day on which the application is finally disposed of;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if the taxpayer, or a person that does not deal at arm√¢¬Ä¬ôs length with the taxpayer, is sent or served with a notice of requirement under subsection 231.6(2) in respect of the taxation year of the taxpayer, the period of time between the day on which the taxpayer or the non-arm√¢¬Ä¬ôs length person applies to a judge for review under subsection 231.6(4) and the day on which the application is finally disposed of;

if an application is commenced by the Minister under subsection 231.7(1) to order the taxpayer, or a person that does not deal at arm√¢¬Ä¬ôs length with the taxpayer, to provide any access, assistance, information or document in respect of the taxation year of the taxpayer, the period of time between the day on which the taxpayer or the non-arm√¢¬Ä¬ôs length person files a notice of appearance, or otherwise opposes the application, and the day on which the application is finally disposed of;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: if the taxpayer, or a person that does not deal at arm√¢¬Ä¬ôs length with the taxpayer, is sent or served with a notice of non-compliance under subsection 231.9(1) in respect of the taxation year of the taxpayer, the period of time that the notice of non-compliance is outstanding; and

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: if, under subsection 231.9(10), a judge has vacated a notice of non-compliance sent to, or served on, the taxpayer, or a person that does not deal at arm√¢¬Ä¬ôs length with the taxpayer in respect of the taxation year of the taxpayer, the period of time between the day on which the taxpayer or the non-arm√¢¬Ä¬ôs length person applies to a judge for review under subsection 231.9(9) and the day on which the application is finally disposed of.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of subsection (1), an application is finally disposed of when the application is disposed of and the time to appeal the application has expired and, in the case of an appeal, when the appeal and any further appeal is disposed of or the time for filing any further appeal has expired.

Subject to subsection (2), the Minister may, at any time, send to a person or serve on a person, in accordance with subsection (3), a notice of non-compliance if the Minister determines that the person has not complied in full or in part with

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a requirement under paragraph 231.1(1)(d) or (f);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a requirement under paragraph 231.1(1)(e) to give an authorized person all reasonable assistance necessary to allow the authorized person to do anything the authorized person is authorized to do under paragraphs 231.1(1)(a) to (c); or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a notice sent or served under subsection 231.2(1) or 231.6(2).

Subsection.Text: The Minister must not send or serve on a person (referred to in this subsection as the √¢¬Ä¬úthird party√¢¬Ä¬ù) a notice described under subsection (1) in respect of a requirement or notice (in this subsection referred to as the √¢¬Ä¬úunrelated person requirement√¢¬Ä¬ù) if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the unrelated person requirement

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: requires the third party to provide information or any document relating to one or more other persons that are not related to the third party, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: is a requirement described in subsection 231.2(2); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an order has not been made under subsection 231.7(1) in respect of the unrelated person requirement.

Subsection.Text: A notice of non-compliance under subsection (1) shall set out, in respect of each taxation year of the taxpayer under review, the manner in which the person that has been sent or served with the notice of non-compliance has failed to comply with a requirement or notice described in any of paragraphs (1)(a) to (c).
A notice of non-compliance referred to in subsection (1) may be

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: served personally;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: sent by registered or certified mail; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: sent electronically to a bank or credit union that has provided written consent to receive notices of non-compliance under subsection (1) electronically.

Subsection.Text: A person that is sent or served with a notice of non-compliance under subsection (1) may, within 90 days after the day on which the notice of non-compliance is sent or served, request in writing to the Minister that the notice of non-compliance be reviewed and make a representation or submission to the Minister in that regard.
Within 180 days from the date of receipt by the Minister of a request by a person under subsection (5), the Minister must

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: confirm, vary or vacate the notice of non-compliance sent or served under subsection (1); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: notify the person in writing of the Minister√¢¬Ä¬ôs decision.

Subsection.Text: A notice of non-compliance shall be vacated under subsection (6) if the Minister determines that it was unreasonable to issue the notice of non-compliance, or that the person had, prior to the issuance of the notice of non-compliance, done everything reasonably necessary to comply with each requirement or notice in respect of which the notice of non-compliance was issued.

If a person has made a request under subsection (5), the notice of non-compliance sent or served under subsection (1) is deemed to be vacated under subsection (6) if the Minister has not notified the person in writing of the Minister√¢¬Ä¬ôs decision to confirm, vary or vacate that notice of non-compliance within 180 days after the day on which the Minister received the request.
A person may, within 90 days after the day on which the person is notified of the Minister√¢¬Ä¬ôs decision under subsection (6), apply to a judge for a review of that decision.
On hearing an application under subsection (9) in respect of a decision by the Minister, a judge may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: confirm the decision; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: vary or vacate the notice of non-compliance if the judge determines that the Minister√¢¬Ä¬ôs decision was not reasonable.

Subsection.Text: Except for the purpose of paragraph 231.8(1)(f), if a notice of non-compliance is vacated under subsection (6), (8) or (10), it is deemed to have never been sent or served.

For the purposes of subsection (13) and paragraph 231.8(1)(e), a notice of non-compliance is outstanding beginning on the day on which it is sent to, or served on, a person until the day on which the person has, to the satisfaction of the Minister, complied, or demonstrated that they have done everything reasonably necessary to comply, with each requirement or notice in respect of which the notice of non-compliance was issued.
A person sent or served a notice of non-compliance under subsection (1) is liable to a penalty of $50 for each day the notice of non-compliance is outstanding, to a maximum of $25,000.

Subsection (13) does not apply to a notice of non-compliance sent or served in respect of a person√¢¬Ä¬ôs non-compliance with a requirement to provide information or documents or to answer questions, if one of the reasons for the person not complying with the requirement was the person√¢¬Ä¬ôs reasonable belief that the information, documents or answers were protected from disclosure by solicitor-client privilege.

The Minister may at any time assess any amount payable under subsection (13) by any person and, if the Minister makes such an assessment, the provisions of Divisions I and J of Part I apply, with such modifications as the circumstances require, in respect of the assessment as though it had been made under section 152.
Clauses 233.2(4)(c)(ii)(E) and (F) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Clause.Label: (F)
Clause.Text: a trust in which all persons beneficially interested are persons described in clauses (A) to (D), or

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) comes into force on January 1, 2027.

Paragraph (a) of the definition specified Canadian entity in subsection 233.3(1) of the Act is amended by adding √¢¬Ä¬úor√¢¬Ä¬ù at the end of subparagraph (vi) and by replacing subparagraphs (vii) and (viii) with the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (viii)
Subparagraph.Text: a trust in which all persons beneficially interested are persons described in subparagraphs (i) to (vi); and

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) comes into force on January 1, 2027.
Subparagraph 241(4)(d)(vii.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (vii.1)
Subparagraph.Text: to an official solely for the purpose of the administration or enforcement of the Canada Education Savings Act or a designated provincial program as defined in subsection 146.1(1) or for the purposes of the evaluation or formulation of policy for the Canada Education Savings Act,

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 241(4)(d)(vii.5) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (vii.5)
Subparagraph.Text: to an official solely for the purposes of the administration or enforcement of the Canada Disability Savings Act or a designated provincial program as defined in subsection 146.4(1) or for the purposes of the evaluation or formulation of policy for the Canada Disability Savings Act,

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 241(4)(l)(ii) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a by-law of a municipality in Canada or a law of an Aboriginal government;

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph 241(4)(u) of the Act before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (u)

Section.Text: Section 244 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (14.2):

Subsection.Text: Despite subsection (14.2), a notice issued under any of subsections 149.1(6.3), (22) or (23), subsection 168(1) or subsections 188.2(1), (2) or (2.1) that is made available in electronic format such that it can be read or perceived by an individual or a computer system or other similar device, and that refers to the business number, trust account number or registration number of a person, is presumed to be sent to the person and received by the person on the date that it is posted by the Minister in the secure electronic account in respect of a business number, trust account number or registration number of the person, if the person has authorized that notices may be made available in that manner and has not at least 30 days before that date revoked that authorization in a manner specified by the Minister.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 248(36) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If a taxpayer acquired a property, otherwise than by reason of the death of an individual, that is the subject of a gift to which subsection (35) applies because of subparagraph (35)(b)(i) or (ii) and the property was, at any time within the 3-year or 10-year period, respectively, that ends when the gift was made, acquired by a person or partnership with whom the taxpayer does not deal at arm√¢¬Ä¬ôs length, for the purpose of applying subsection (35) to the taxpayer, the cost or in the case of capital property, the adjusted cost base, of the property √¢¬Ä¬ì or, in the case of a life insurance policy in respect of which the taxpayer is a policyholder, the adjusted cost basis (as defined in subsection 148(9)) √¢¬Ä¬ì to the taxpayer immediately before the gift is made is deemed to be equal to the lowest amount that is the cost or in the case of capital property, the adjusted cost base √¢¬Ä¬ì or, in the case of a life insurance policy in respect of which the taxpayer is a policyholder, the adjusted cost basis (as defined in subsection 148(9)) √¢¬Ä¬ì to the taxpayer or any of those persons or partnerships immediately before the property was disposed of by that person or partnership.
Subsection (1) comes into force on January 1, 2027.
