The definitions depository account, depository institution, high value account, investment entity, lower value account and new account in subsection 270(1) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: depository account√¢¬Ä¬Çincludes

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a commercial, chequing, savings, time or thrift account, or an account that is evidenced by a certificate of deposit, thrift certificate, investment certificate, certificate of indebtedness or other similar instrument maintained by a depository institution;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an amount held by an insurance company under a guaranteed investment contract or similar agreement to pay or credit interest on the contract or agreement;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: an account or notional account that represents all specified electronic money products held for the benefit of a customer; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: an account that holds one or more central bank digital currencies for the benefit of a customer.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(compte de d√É¬©p√É¬¥t)

Definition.Text: depository institution√¢¬Ä¬Çmeans an entity that

Paragraph.Text: accepts deposits in the ordinary course of a banking or similar business; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: holds specified electronic money products or central bank digital currencies for the benefit of customers.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(√É¬©tablissement de d√É¬©p√É¬¥t)

Definition.Text: high value account√¢¬Ä¬Çmeans a preexisting individual account with an aggregate balance or value that exceeds 1 million USD

Text.Ins: if the account is an ACRS account, on December 31, 2026 or on December 31 of any subsequent year; and

Paragraph.Text: in any other case, on June 30, 2017 or on December 31 of any subsequent year.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(compte de valeur √É¬©lev√É¬©e)

Definition.Text: investment entity√¢¬Ä¬Çmeans any entity (other than an entity that is an active NFE because of any of paragraphs (d) to (g) of that definition)

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that primarily carries on as a business one or more of the following activities or operations for or on behalf of a customer:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: trading in money market instruments (such as cheques, bills, certificates of deposit and derivatives), foreign exchange, transferable securities, commodity futures or exchange, interest rate and index instruments,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: individual and collective portfolio management, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: otherwise investing, administering or managing financial assets, money or relevant crypto-assets on behalf of other persons, excluding through the provision of services effectuating exchange transactions for or on behalf of customers; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the gross income of which is primarily attributable to investing, reinvesting or trading in financial assets or relevant crypto-assets, if the entity is managed by another entity that is a depository institution, a custodial institution, a specified insurance company or an investment entity described in paragraph (a).√¢¬Ä¬Ç(entit√É¬© d√¢¬Ä¬ôinvestissement)

Definition.Text: lower value account√¢¬Ä¬Çmeans a preexisting individual account with an aggregate balance or value that does not exceed 1 million USD

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the account is an ACRS account, as of December 31, 2026; and
in any other case, as of June 30, 2017.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(compte de faible valeur)

Definition.Text: new account√¢¬Ä¬Çmeans a financial account maintained by a reporting financial institution opened

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the account is an ACRS account, after 2026; and
in any other case, after June 2017.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(nouveau compte)

Subsection.Text: The definition proc√É¬©dures de connaissance de la client√É¬®le et de lutte contre le blanchiment d√¢¬Ä¬ôargent or proc√É¬©dures AML/KYC in subsection 270(1) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: proc√É¬©dures de connaissance de la client√É¬®le et de lutte contre le blanchiment d√¢¬Ä¬ôargent ou proc√É¬©dures AML/KYC√¢¬Ä¬ÇObligations de tenue de documents, de v√É¬©rification d√¢¬Ä¬ôidentit√É¬©s, de d√É¬©claration des op√É¬©rations douteuses et d√¢¬Ä¬ôinscription qu√¢¬Ä¬ôune institution financi√É¬®re d√É¬©clarante est tenue d√¢¬Ä¬ôobserver en vertu de la Loi sur le recyclage des produits de la criminalit√É¬© et le financement des activit√É¬©s terroristes.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(anti-money laundering and know your customer procedures or AML/KYC procedures)

Subsection.Text: The portion of the definition controlling persons in subsection 270(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: controlling persons, in respect of an entity, means the natural persons who exercise control over the entity (interpreted in a manner consistent with the 2012 FATF recommendations) and includes

Subsection.Text: The portion of the definition excluded account in subsection 270(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: excluded account, at any time, means

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (e) of the definition excluded account in subsection 270(1) of the Act is amended by striking out √¢¬Ä¬úor√¢¬Ä¬ù at the end of subparagraph (iii), by adding √¢¬Ä¬úor√¢¬Ä¬ù at the end of subparagraph (iv) and by adding the following after that subparagraph:

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: a contribution of capital to a corporation or its incorporation, if the account meets the following conditions:

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the account is used exclusively to deposit amounts that are to be used for the purpose of the incorporation of or the making of contributions of capital to a corporation, in accordance with applicable law,

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: any amounts held in the account are blocked until the reporting financial institution obtains an independent confirmation regarding the incorporation or contribution of capital,

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: the account is closed or transformed into an account in the name of the corporation after the incorporation or contribution of capital,

Clause.Label: (D)
Clause.Text: any repayments resulting from the failed incorporation or contribution of capital, net of service provider and similar fees, are made solely to the persons who contributed the amounts, and

Clause.Label: (E)
Clause.Text: the account was not established more than 12 months before that time;

Subsection.Text: The definition excluded account in subsection 270(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (e):

Paragraph.Label: (e.1)
Paragraph.Text: a depository account that only includes all specified electronic money products held for the benefit of a customer, if the rolling average 90-day end-of-day aggregate account balance or value during any period of 90 consecutive days did not exceed 10,000 USD on any day during the calendar year that includes that time;

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph (a)(vi) of the definition financial asset in subsection 270(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: any interest (including a futures or forward contract or option) in a security, relevant crypto-asset, partnership interest, commodity, swap, insurance contract or annuity contract; and

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (b) of the definition non-reporting financial institution in subsection 270(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a governmental entity or international organization, other than with respect to

Subparagraph.Text: a payment that is derived from an obligation held in connection with a commercial financial activity of a type engaged in by a specified insurance company, custodial institution or depository institution, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the activity of maintaining central bank digital currencies for account holders which are not financial institutions, governmental entities, international organizations or central banks;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (a) of the definition preexisting account in subsection 270(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a financial account maintained by a reporting financial institution on June 30, 2017 or, if the account is an ACRS account, on December 31, 2026; and

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs (a) and (b) of the definition reportable person in subsection 270(1) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an entity the stock of which is regularly traded on one or more established securities markets;

Paragraph.Label: (b)

Subsection.Text: Subsection 270(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: 2012 FATF recommendations√¢¬Ä¬Çmeans the Financial Action Task Force Recommendations √¢¬Ä¬î International Standards on Combating Money Laundering and the Financing of Terrorism and Proliferation, adopted in February 2012 and as amended from time to time.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(recommandations de 2012 du GAFI)

ACRS account√¢¬Ä¬Çmeans an account that is treated as a financial account solely because of the amendments to this Part relating to the implementation of the 2023 amendments to the Common Reporting Standard set out in the Standard for Automatic Exchange of Financial Account Information in Tax Matters modified by the Council of the Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development in June 2023.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(compte NCDA)
central bank digital currency√¢¬Ä¬Çmeans a digital fiat currency issued by a central bank.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(monnaie num√É¬©rique de banque centrale)
crypto-asset√¢¬Ä¬Çhas the same meaning as in subsection 296(1).√¢¬Ä¬Ç(crypto-actif)
exchange transaction√¢¬Ä¬Çhas the same meaning as in subsection 296(1).√¢¬Ä¬Ç(transaction d√¢¬Ä¬ô√É¬©change)
fiat currency√¢¬Ä¬Çmeans

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the official currency of a jurisdiction, issued by a jurisdiction or by a jurisdiction√¢¬Ä¬ôs designated central bank or monetary authority, as represented by physical bank notes or coins or by money in digital forms, including bank reserves and central bank digital currencies; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: commercial bank money and electronic money products, including specified electronic money products.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(monnaie fiduciaire)

Definition.Text: relevant crypto-asset√¢¬Ä¬Çhas the same meaning as in subsection 296(1).√¢¬Ä¬Ç(crypto-actif concern√É¬©)

specified electronic money product√¢¬Ä¬Çmeans a product (not including a product that can only be used for facilitating the transfer of funds from a customer to another person pursuant to instructions of the customer) that can be used for making payment transactions and that is

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a digital representation of a single fiat currency;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: issued on receipt of funds;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: represented by a claim on the issuer denominated in the fiat currency described in paragraph (a);

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: accepted in payment by a person other than the issuer; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: by virtue of regulatory requirements to which the issuer is subject, redeemable at any time and at par value for the same fiat currency upon request of the holder of the product.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(produit de monnaie √É¬©lectronique sp√É¬©cifique)

Subsection.Text: Subsection 270(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

This Part relates to the implementation of the Common Reporting Standard set out in the Standard for Automatic Exchange of Financial Account Information in Tax Matters approved by the Council of the Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development and, unless the context otherwise requires, the provisions in this Part are to be interpreted consistently with the Common Reporting Standard and its official commentary, as amended from time to time.
The portion of subsection 270(3) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

For the purposes of the definition investment entity in subsection (1), an entity is considered to be primarily carrying on as a business one or more of the activities described in paragraph (a) of that definition, or an entity√¢¬Ä¬ôs gross income is primarily attributable to investing, reinvesting or trading in financial assets or relevant crypto-assets for the purposes of paragraph (b) of that definition, if the entity√¢¬Ä¬ôs gross income attributable to the relevant activities equals or exceeds 50% of the entity√¢¬Ä¬ôs gross income during the shorter of
Section 270 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (4):

For the purposes of the definition specified electronic money product in subsection (1), a product is not a product that can only be used for facilitating the transfer of funds if, in the ordinary course of business of the transferring entity, either the funds connected with such product are held longer than 60 days after receipt of instructions to facilitate the transfer, or, if no instructions are received, the funds connected with such product are held longer than 60 days after receipt of the funds.
Subsections (1) to (11), (13) and (14) apply to the 2027 and subsequent calendar years.
The portion of subsection 271(1) of the Act before paragraph (b) is replaced by the following:
Subject to subsections (3) to (6), each reporting financial institution must report the following information to the Minister with respect to each of its reportable accounts:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the name, address, jurisdiction of residence, TIN and date of birth (in the case of a natural person) of each reportable person that is an account holder of the account and whether the account holder has provided a valid self-certification;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 271(1)(b) of the Act is amended by striking out √¢¬Ä¬úand√¢¬Ä¬ù at the end of subparagraph (i), by adding √¢¬Ä¬úand√¢¬Ä¬ù at the end of subparagraph (ii) and by adding the following after subparagraph (ii):

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the role by virtue of which each reportable person is a controlling person of the entity and whether a valid self-certification has been provided for each reportable person;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 271(1)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the account number (or functional equivalent in the absence of an account number) of the account, the type of account and whether the account is a preexisting account or a new account;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 271(1) of the Act is amended by striking out √¢¬Ä¬úand√¢¬Ä¬ù at the end of paragraph (g) and by adding the following after paragraph (h):

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: whether the account is a joint account and, if so, the number of joint account holders; and

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of any equity interest held in an investment entity that is a legal arrangement, the role by virtue of which the reportable person is an equity interest holder.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 271(3)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a reporting financial institution is required to use reasonable efforts to obtain the TIN and the date of birth with respect to a preexisting account by the end of the second calendar year following the year in which the preexisting account is identified as a reportable account and whenever it is required to update the information relating to the preexisting account pursuant to AML/KYC procedures.

Subsection.Text: Section 271 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (4):

Despite subparagraph (1)(f)(ii), and unless the reporting financial institution elects otherwise with respect to any clearly identified group of accounts, the gross proceeds from the sale or redemption of a financial asset are not required to be reported to the extent that the gross proceeds are reported by the reporting financial institution under Part XXI.

With respect to each reportable account that is maintained by a reporting financial institution as of January 1, 2027 and for reporting periods ending before 2029, each reporting financial institution must only report the role by virtue of which each reportable person is a controlling person of an entity or an equity interest holder in respect of an investment entity that is a legal arrangement if such information is available in the electronically searchable data maintained by the reporting financial institution.
Subsections (1) to (6) apply to the 2027 and subsequent calendar years.

Section.Text: Paragraphs 273(4)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the account is an ACRS account that is

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a high value account, 2028, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a lower value account, 2029; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in any other case, if the account is

Subparagraph.Text: a high value account, 2019, or
a lower value account, 2020.

Subsection.Text: Subsections 275(1) and (2) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Unless the reporting financial institution elects otherwise √¢¬Ä¬î either with respect to all preexisting entity accounts or, separately, with respect to any clearly identified group of those accounts √¢¬Ä¬î a preexisting entity account is not required to be reviewed, identified or reported as a reportable account

Paragraph.Label: (a)

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in any other case, if the aggregate account balance or value does not exceed 250,000 USD on June 30, 2017, until the end of the calendar year following the first year after 2017 in which the aggregate account balance or value exceeds 250,000 USD.

Subsection.Text: The review procedures set forth in subsection (4) apply to a preexisting entity account if it has an aggregate account balance or value that exceeds 250,000 USD on

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the account is an ACRS account, December 31, 2026 or the last day of a subsequent calendar year; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in any other case, June 30, 2017 or the last day of a subsequent calendar year.

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 275(5)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Text.Ins: if the account is an ACRS account and

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: has an aggregate account balance or value that exceeds 250,000 USD on December 31, 2026, 2028, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in any other case,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: if the account has an aggregate account balance or value that exceeds 250,000 USD on June 30, 2017, 2020, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if subparagraph (i) does not apply, the end of the calendar year following the first year in which the aggregate account balance or value exceeds 250,000 USD on December 31.

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 276(b)(ii) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: determining the controlling persons of an account holder, a reporting financial institution may rely on information collected and maintained in accordance with AML/KYC procedures, provided that such procedures are consistent with the 2012 FATF recommendations, or, if the reporting financial institution is not legally required to apply AML/KYC procedures that are consistent with the 2012 FATF recommendations, it must apply substantially similar procedures for the purpose of determining the controlling persons, and

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to the 2027 and subsequent calendar years.
Section 277 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Special due diligence rules √¢¬Ä¬î new account
Subsection.Text: In exceptional circumstances where a self-certification cannot be obtained by a reporting financial institution in respect of a new account in time to meet its due diligence and reporting obligations with respect to the reporting period during which the account was opened, the reporting financial institution must apply the due diligence procedures for preexisting accounts, until such self-certification is obtained and validated.
Subsection (1) applies to the 2027 and subsequent calendar years.
Section 280 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: If an individual or an entity enters into an arrangement or engages in a practice, the primary purpose of which can reasonably be considered to be to avoid an obligation under this Part, this Part applies as if the individual or entity had not entered into the arrangement or engaged in the practice.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to the 2027 and subsequent calendar years.
Subsection 282(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

This Part relates to the implementation of the Model Rules set out in the Model Rules for Reporting by Platform Operators with respect to Sellers in the Sharing and Gig Economy approved by the Council of the Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development and, unless the context otherwise requires, the provisions in this Part are to be interpreted consistently with the Model Rules and their official commentary, as amended from time to time.
Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2024.
The Act is amended by adding the following after Part XX:
The following definitions apply in this Part.

Definition.Text: active entity, at any time, means an entity that meets any of the following criteria:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: less than 50% of the entity√¢¬Ä¬ôs gross income for the preceding calendar year is passive income and less than 50% of the assets held by the entity during the preceding calendar year are assets that produce or are held for the production of passive income;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: it is the case that

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: all or substantially all of the activities of the entity consist of holding (in whole or in part) the outstanding stock of, or providing financing and services to, one or more subsidiaries that engage in trades or businesses other than the business of a financial institution, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the entity does not function as, and is not represented or promoted to the public as, an investment fund, including

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: a private equity fund,

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: a venture capital fund,

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: a leveraged buyout fund, and

Clause.Label: (D)
Clause.Text: an investment vehicle whose purpose is to acquire or fund companies and then hold interests in those companies as capital assets for investment purposes;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the entity

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: is not yet operating a business,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: has no prior operating history,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: is investing capital into assets with the intent to operate a business other than that of a financial institution, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: was initially organized no more than 24 months prior to that time;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the entity has not been a financial institution in any of the last five years and is in the process of liquidating its assets or is reorganizing with the intent to continue or recommence operations in a business other than that of a financial institution;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the entity primarily engages in financing and hedging transactions with, or for, related entities that are not financial institutions, and does not provide financing or hedging services to any entity that is not a related entity, provided that the group of those related entities is primarily engaged in a business other than that of a financial institution;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the entity meets all of the following requirements:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: it is established and operated in

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: its jurisdiction of residence exclusively for religious, charitable, scientific, artistic, cultural, athletic or educational purposes, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: its jurisdiction of residence and it is a professional organization, business league, chamber of commerce, labour organization, agricultural or horticultural organization, civic league or an organization operated exclusively for the promotion of social welfare,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: it is exempt from income tax in its jurisdiction of residence,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: it has no shareholders or members who have a proprietary or beneficial interest in its income or assets,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: the applicable laws of the entity√¢¬Ä¬ôs jurisdiction of residence or the entity√¢¬Ä¬ôs formation documents

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: do not permit any income or assets of the entity to be distributed to, or applied for the benefit of, a private person or non-charitable entity other than pursuant to the conduct of the entity√¢¬Ä¬ôs charitable activities, or as payment of reasonable compensation for services rendered, or as payment representing the fair market value of property which the entity has purchased, and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: require that, upon the entity√¢¬Ä¬ôs liquidation or dissolution, all of its assets be distributed to a governmental entity or other non-profit organization, or escheat to the government of the entity√¢¬Ä¬ôs jurisdiction of residence or any of its political subdivisions. (entit√É¬© active)

Definition.Text: anti-money laundering and know your customer procedures or AML/KYC procedures√¢¬Ä¬Çmeans the record keeping, verification of identity, reporting of suspicious transactions and registration requirements required of a reporting crypto-asset service provider under the Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering) and Terrorist Financing Act.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(proc√É¬©dures de connaissance de la client√É¬®le et de lutte contre le blanchiment d√¢¬Ä¬ôargent ou proc√É¬©dures AML/KYC)

branch√¢¬Ä¬Çmeans a unit, business or office of a reporting crypto-asset service provider that is treated as a branch under the regulatory regime of a jurisdiction or that is otherwise regulated under the laws of a jurisdiction as separate from other offices, units or branches of the reporting crypto-asset service provider.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(succursale)
controlling persons√¢¬Ä¬Çhas the same meaning as in subsection 270(1).√¢¬Ä¬Ç(personnes d√É¬©tenant le contr√É¬¥le)

crypto-asset√¢¬Ä¬Çmeans a digital representation of value that relies on a cryptographically secured distributed ledger or a similar technology to validate and secure transactions.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(crypto-actif)
crypto-asset user√¢¬Ä¬Çmeans

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an individual or entity that is a customer of a reporting crypto-asset service provider for the purpose of carrying out relevant transactions, other than an individual or entity that

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: is not a financial institution or a reporting crypto-asset service provider, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: is acting as a customer of a reporting crypto-asset service provider for the purpose of carrying out relevant transactions for the benefit of, or on behalf of, another individual or entity as agent, custodian, nominee, signatory, investment advisor or intermediary;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the individual or entity referred to in subparagraph (a)(ii) for whose benefit, or on whose behalf, relevant transactions are carried out; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: with respect to a reportable retail payment transaction, the customer that is the counterparty to the merchant for the transaction, if

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a reporting crypto-asset service provider provides a service effectuating reportable retail payment transactions for or on behalf of the merchant, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the reporting crypto-asset service provider is required to verify the identity of the customer under the Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering) and Terrorist Financing Act.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(utilisateur de crypto-actifs)

Definition.Text: custodial institution√¢¬Ä¬Çhas the same meaning as in subsection 270(1).√¢¬Ä¬Ç(√É¬©tablissement de garde de valeurs)
depository institution√¢¬Ä¬Çhas the same meaning as in subsection 270(1).√¢¬Ä¬Ç(√É¬©tablissement de d√É¬©p√É¬¥t)
entity√¢¬Ä¬Çhas the same meaning as in subsection 270(1).√¢¬Ä¬Ç(entit√É¬©)
entity crypto-asset user√¢¬Ä¬Çmeans a crypto-asset user that is an entity.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(entit√É¬© utilisatrice de crypto-actifs)
exchange transaction√¢¬Ä¬Çmeans an exchange between relevant crypto-assets and fiat currencies or an exchange between one or more forms of relevant crypto-assets.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(transaction d√¢¬Ä¬ô√É¬©change)
excluded person√¢¬Ä¬Çmeans

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an entity, the stock of which is regularly traded on one or more established securities markets (as defined in subsection 270(1));

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an entity that is a related entity of an entity described in paragraph (a);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a governmental entity;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: an international organization (as defined in subsection 270(1));

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: a central bank (as defined in subsection 270(1)); or

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: a financial institution, other than an investment entity described in paragraph (b) of the definition investment entity.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(personne exclue)

Definition.Text: fiat currency√¢¬Ä¬Çhas the same meaning as in subsection 270(1).√¢¬Ä¬Ç(monnaie fiduciaire)
financial asset√¢¬Ä¬Çhas the same meaning as in subsection 270(1).√¢¬Ä¬Ç(actif financier)
financial institution√¢¬Ä¬Çmeans a custodial institution, depository institution, investment entity or specified insurance company.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(institution financi√É¬®re)
governmental entity√¢¬Ä¬Çhas the same meaning as in subsection 270(1).√¢¬Ä¬Ç(entit√É¬© gouvernementale)
individual crypto-asset user√¢¬Ä¬Çmeans a crypto-asset user that is an individual (other than a trust).√¢¬Ä¬Ç(utilisateur individuel de crypto-actifs)
investment entity√¢¬Ä¬Çmeans an entity, other than an entity that is an active entity because of any of paragraphs (b) to (e) of that definition,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that primarily carries on as a business one or more of the following activities or operations for or on behalf of a customer:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: trading in money market instruments (such as cheques, bills, certificates of deposit and derivatives), foreign exchange, transferable securities, commodity futures or exchange, interest rate and index instruments,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: individual and collective portfolio management, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: otherwise investing, administering or managing financial assets, money, or relevant crypto-assets on behalf of other persons, other than through the provision of services effectuating exchange transactions for or on behalf of customers; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the gross income of which is primarily attributable to investing, reinvesting or trading in financial assets or relevant crypto-assets, if the entity is managed by another entity that is a depository institution, custodial institution, specified insurance company or entity described in paragraph (a).√¢¬Ä¬Ç(entit√É¬© d√¢¬Ä¬ôinvestissement)

Definition.Text: partner jurisdiction√¢¬Ä¬Çmeans each jurisdiction identified as a partner jurisdiction by the Minister on the Internet webpage of the Canada Revenue Agency or by any other means that the Minister considers appropriate.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(juridiction partenaire)

preexisting crypto-asset user√¢¬Ä¬Çmeans a crypto-asset user that has established a relationship with the reporting crypto-asset service provider as of December 31, 2026.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(utilisateur de crypto-actifs pr√É¬©existant)

related entity√¢¬Ä¬Çin respect of an entity, means an entity if either entity controls the other entity or the two entities are controlled by the same entity or individual, and, for this purpose, control includes direct or indirect ownership of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a corporation, shares of the capital stock of a corporation that

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: give their holders more than 50% of the votes that could be cast at the annual meeting of the shareholders of the corporation, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: have a fair market value of more than 50% of the fair market value of all the issued and outstanding shares of the capital stock of the corporation;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a partnership, an interest as a member of the partnership that entitles the member to more than 50% of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the income or loss of the partnership, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the assets (net of liabilities) of the partnership if it were to cease to exist; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a trust, an interest as a beneficiary under the trust with a fair market value that is greater than 50% of the fair market value of all interests as a beneficiary under the trust.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(entit√É¬© li√É¬©e)

Definition.Text: relevant crypto-asset√¢¬Ä¬Çmeans any crypto-asset that is not a central bank digital currency, a specified electronic money product (as those terms are defined in subsection 270(1)) or any crypto-asset for which the reporting crypto-asset service provider has adequately determined that it cannot be used for payment or investment purposes.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(crypto-actif concern√É¬©)
relevant transaction√¢¬Ä¬Çmeans an exchange transaction or a transfer of a relevant crypto-asset.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(transaction concern√É¬©e)
reportable jurisdiction√¢¬Ä¬Çmeans any jurisdiction, including Canada.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(juridiction soumise √É¬Ý d√É¬©claration)

reportable person√¢¬Ä¬Çmeans a natural person (as defined in subsection 270(1)) or an entity, other than an excluded person, that is resident in a reportable jurisdiction under the tax laws of that jurisdiction, or an estate of an individual who was a resident of a reportable jurisdiction under the tax laws of that jurisdiction immediately before death, and, for this purpose, an entity that has no residence for tax purposes is deemed to be resident in the jurisdiction in which its place of effective management is situated.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(personne devant faire l√¢¬Ä¬ôobjet d√¢¬Ä¬ôune d√É¬©claration)

reportable retail payment transaction√¢¬Ä¬Çmeans a transfer of a relevant crypto-asset in consideration for goods or services for a value exceeding 50,000 USD.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(op√É¬©ration de paiement au d√É¬©tail d√É¬©clarable)
reportable user√¢¬Ä¬Çmeans a crypto-asset user that is a reportable person.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(utilisateur soumis √É¬Ý d√É¬©claration)

reporting crypto-asset service provider√¢¬Ä¬Çmeans any individual or entity that, as a business, provides a service effectuating exchange transactions for or on behalf of customers, including by acting as a counterparty or as an intermediary to the exchange transactions, or by making available a trading platform.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(prestataire de services sur crypto-actifs d√É¬©clarant)
specified insurance company√¢¬Ä¬Çhas the same meaning as in subsection 270(1).√¢¬Ä¬Ç(compagnie d√¢¬Ä¬ôassurance particuli√É¬®re)
TIN√¢¬Ä¬Çhas the same meaning as in subsection 270(1).√¢¬Ä¬Ç(NIF)

transfer√¢¬Ä¬Çmeans a transaction that moves a relevant crypto-asset from or to the crypto-asset address or account of one crypto-asset user, other than one maintained by the reporting crypto-asset service provider on behalf of the same crypto-asset user, where, based on the knowledge available to the reporting crypto-asset service provider at the time of transaction, the reporting crypto-asset service provider cannot determine that the transaction is an exchange transaction.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(transfert)
USD√¢¬Ä¬Çmeans dollars of the United States of America.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(USD)

Subsection.Text: This Part relates to the implementation of the Crypto-Asset Reporting Framework set out in the International Standards for Automatic Exchange of Information in Tax Matters approved by the Council of the Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development and, unless the context otherwise requires, the provisions in this Part are to be interpreted consistently with the Crypto-Asset Reporting Framework and its official commentary, as amended from time to time.

For the purposes of the definition investment entity in subsection (1), an entity is considered to be primarily carrying on as a business one or more of the activities described in paragraph (a) of that definition, or an entity√¢¬Ä¬ôs gross income is primarily attributable to investing, reinvesting or trading in financial assets or relevant crypto-assets for the purposes of paragraph (b) of that definition, if the entity√¢¬Ä¬ôs gross income attributable to the relevant activities equals or exceeds 50% of the entity√¢¬Ä¬ôs gross income during the shorter of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the three-year period that ends at the end of the entity√¢¬Ä¬ôs last taxation year; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the period during which the entity has been in existence.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of the definition branch in subsection (1), all units, businesses and offices of a reporting crypto-asset service provider in a single jurisdiction must be treated as a single branch.
Subject to subsections (3) to (7), sections 298 and 299 apply to a reporting crypto-asset service provider for a calendar year if it is

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an entity or individual resident in Canada;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an entity that

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: is organized under the laws of Canada or a province, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: has an obligation to file tax returns or tax information returns in Canada;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a partnership managed from Canada; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: an entity or individual that carries on a business in Canada.

Subsection.Text: Sections 298 and 299 apply to a reporting crypto-asset service provider described in paragraph (1)(d) for a calendar year with respect to relevant transactions effectuated through a branch in Canada if those sections would otherwise not apply to the reporting crypto-asset service provider because of any of subsections (3) to (5) and (7).

A reporting crypto-asset service provider is not required to complete the reporting and due diligence requirements in sections 298 and 299 because of paragraphs (1)(b) to (d) if equivalent requirements are completed by the reporting crypto-asset service provider in a partner jurisdiction by virtue of it being resident in the partner jurisdiction.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Exception √¢¬Ä¬î jurisdiction of incorporation or organization
Subsection.Text: A reporting crypto-asset service provider that is an entity is not required to complete the reporting and due diligence requirements in sections 298 and 299 because of any of paragraphs (1)(c) to (d) if equivalent requirements are completed by the reporting crypto-asset service provider in a partner jurisdiction by virtue of it being an entity that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is incorporated or organized under the laws of the partner jurisdiction; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: either has legal personality in the partner jurisdiction or has an obligation to file tax returns or tax information returns to the tax authorities in the partner jurisdiction with respect to the income of the entity.

Subsection.Text: A reporting crypto-asset service provider that is an entity is not required to complete the reporting and due diligence requirements in sections 298 and 299 because of paragraph (1)(d), if equivalent requirements are completed by the reporting crypto-asset service provider in a partner jurisdiction by virtue of it being managed from the partner jurisdiction.

A reporting crypto-asset service provider is not required to complete the reporting and due diligence requirements in sections 298 and 299 with respect to relevant transactions it effectuates through a branch in a partner jurisdiction, if equivalent requirements are completed by the branch in the partner jurisdiction.

A reporting crypto-asset service provider is not required to complete the reporting and due diligence requirements in sections 298 and 299 because of subsection (1), if it has notified the Minister, in the manner specified by the Minister, that the reporting crypto-asset service provider completed equivalent requirements under the rules of a partner jurisdiction because of a nexus that is substantially similar to one of those described in paragraphs (1)(a) to (d) to which the reporting crypto-asset service provider is subject to in Canada.

Subject to subsections (2) and (3) and section 299, if this section applies in respect of a reporting crypto-asset service provider for a calendar year, the reporting crypto-asset service provider must report the following information with respect to each of its crypto-asset users that are reportable users or that have controlling persons that are reportable persons at any time during the year:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the name, address, jurisdiction of residence, TIN and date of birth of each reportable user;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of any entity that has one or more controlling persons that is a reportable person

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the name, address, jurisdiction of residence and TIN of the entity,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the name, address, jurisdiction of residence, TIN and date of birth of each reportable person, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the role by virtue of which each reportable person is a controlling person of the entity;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the name, address and TIN or other identifying number of the reporting crypto-asset service provider;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: for each crypto-asset user and for each type of relevant crypto-asset with respect to which it has effectuated relevant transactions during the calendar year

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the full name of the type of relevant crypto-asset,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the aggregate gross amount paid, aggregate number of units and number of relevant transactions in respect of acquisitions against fiat currency,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the aggregate gross amount received, aggregate number of units and number of relevant transactions in respect of dispositions against fiat currency,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: the aggregate fair market value, aggregate number of units and number of relevant transactions in respect of acquisitions against other relevant crypto-assets,

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: the aggregate fair market value, aggregate number of units and number of relevant transactions in respect of dispositions against other relevant crypto-assets,

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: the aggregate fair market value, aggregate number of units and number of reportable retail payment transactions,

Subparagraph.Label: (vii)
Subparagraph.Text: the aggregate fair market value, aggregate number of units and number of relevant transactions, and subdivided by transfer type where known by the reporting crypto-asset service provider, in respect of transfers to the crypto-asset user not covered by subparagraphs (ii) and (iv),

Subparagraph.Label: (viii)
Subparagraph.Text: the aggregate fair market value, aggregate number of units and number of relevant transactions, and subdivided by transfer type where known by the reporting crypto-asset service provider, in respect of transfers by the crypto-asset user not covered by subparagraphs (iii), (v) and (vi), and

Subparagraph.Label: (ix)
Subparagraph.Text: the aggregate fair market value and aggregate number of units in respect of transfers by the crypto-asset user effectuated by the reporting crypto-asset service provider to wallet addresses not known by the reporting crypto-asset service provider to be associated with a virtual asset service provider or financial institution.

Subsection.Text: Despite subsection (1), a TIN is not required to be reported if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: it is not issued by the relevant reportable jurisdiction; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the domestic law of the relevant reportable jurisdiction does not require the collection of the TIN.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of subparagraphs (1)(d)(ii) and (iii), the amount paid or received must be reported in the fiat currency in which it was paid or received, except that if the amount was paid or received in multiple fiat currencies, the amount must be reported in Canadian dollars, converted at the time of each relevant transaction in a manner that is consistently applied by the reporting crypto-asset service provider.

For the purposes of subparagraphs (1)(d)(iv) to (ix), fair market value must be determined and reported in Canadian dollars, valued at the time of each relevant transaction in a manner that is consistently applied by the reporting crypto-asset service provider.
A crypto-asset user is a reportable user as of the date it is identified (or should have been identified) as a reportable user under the due diligence procedures set out in this section.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Due diligence procedures √¢¬Ä¬î individual crypto-asset users
Subsection.Text: A reporting crypto-asset service provider must apply the following due diligence procedures to determine whether an individual crypto-asset user is a reportable user:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: obtain a valid self-certification that allows it to determine the individual crypto-asset user√¢¬Ä¬ôs residence for tax purposes, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: confirm the reasonableness of such self-certification based on the information obtained by the reporting crypto-asset service provider, including any documentation collected under AML/KYC procedures.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Due diligence procedures √¢¬Ä¬î entity crypto-asset users
Subsection.Text: A reporting crypto-asset service provider must apply the following due diligence procedures to determine whether an entity crypto-asset user is a reportable user or is an entity, other than an excluded person or an active entity, with one or more controlling persons who are reportable persons:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: obtain a valid self-certification that allows it to determine the entity crypto-asset user√¢¬Ä¬ôs residence for tax purposes and whether it is an active entity;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: confirm the reasonableness of such self-certification based on the information obtained by the reporting crypto-asset service provider, including any documentation collected under AML/KYC procedures;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if the entity crypto-asset user certifies that it has no residence for tax purposes, rely on the place of effective management or on the address of the principal office to determine the residence of the entity crypto-asset user; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: if the self-certification indicates that the entity crypto-asset user is resident in a reportable jurisdiction, treat the entity crypto-asset user as a reportable user, unless it reasonably determines based on the self-certification or on information in its possession or that is publicly available, that the entity crypto-asset user is an excluded person.

Subsection.Text: A reporting crypto-asset service provider must apply the following due diligence procedures with respect to an entity crypto-asset user, other than an excluded person or an active entity, to determine whether it has one or more controlling persons who are reportable persons:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: rely on a valid self-certification from the entity crypto-asset user or such controlling person that allows the reporting crypto-asset service provider to determine the controlling person√¢¬Ä¬ôs residence for tax purposes; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: confirm the reasonableness of the self-certification based on the information obtained by the reporting crypto-asset service provider, including any documentation collected under AML/KYC procedures.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of determining a controlling person of the entity crypto-asset user, as required under subsection (4), a reporting crypto-asset service provider may rely on information collected and maintained under AML/KYC procedures, provided that such procedures are consistent with the 2012 FATF recommendations (as defined in subsection 270(1)) or, if it is not legally required to apply such procedures, substantially similar procedures.

A reporting crypto-asset service provider must complete the due diligence procedures set out in this section when establishing a relationship with a crypto-asset user or, with respect to preexisting crypto-asset users, before 2028.

If at any point there is a change of circumstances of a crypto-asset user or its controlling person that causes the reporting crypto-asset service provider to know, or have reason to know, that the original self-certification is incorrect or unreliable, the reporting crypto-asset service provider cannot rely on the original self-certification and must obtain a valid self-certification, or a reasonable explanation and documentation supporting the validity of the original self-certification.
A self-certification provided by an individual crypto-asset user or controlling person is valid only if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: it is signed or positively affirmed by the individual crypto-asset user or controlling person on or before the date of its receipt by the reporting crypto-asset service provider; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: it contains the following information with respect to the individual crypto-asset user or controlling person:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: first and last name,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: address of residence,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: jurisdiction of residence for tax purposes,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: TIN with respect to each reportable jurisdiction, and

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: date of birth.

Subsection.Text: A self-certification provided by an entity crypto-asset user is valid only if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: it is signed or positively affirmed by the crypto-asset user on or before the date of its receipt by the reporting crypto-asset service provider; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: it contains the following information with respect to the entity crypto-asset user:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: legal name,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: address,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: jurisdiction of residence for tax purposes,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: TIN with respect to each reportable jurisdiction,

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: if it is not an active entity or an excluded person, the information described in subsection (8) with respect to each controlling person of the entity crypto-asset user, unless such controlling person has provided a self-certification in accordance with that subsection, as well as the role by virtue of which each reportable person is a controlling person of the entity, if not already determined on the basis of AML/KYC procedures, and

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: if applicable, information as to the criteria it meets to be an active entity or excluded person.

Subsection.Text: Despite subsections (8) and (9), a TIN of a reportable person is not required to be collected if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the jurisdiction of residence of the reportable person does not issue a TIN to the reportable person; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the domestic law of the relevant reportable jurisdiction does not require the collection of the TIN issued by the reportable jurisdiction.

Subsection.Text: A reporting crypto-asset service provider that is also a financial institution (as defined in subsection 270(1)) may rely on the due diligence procedures completed under sections 274 and 276 for the purposes of the due diligence procedures in this section.

A reporting crypto-asset service provider may rely on a self-certification already collected for other tax purposes for the purposes of the due diligence procedures in this section, provided the self-certification meets the requirements of subsection (8) or (9).

A reporting crypto-asset service provider may rely on a third party to fulfil the due diligence obligations set out in this section, but such obligations remain the responsibility of the reporting crypto-asset service provider.

Every reporting crypto-asset service provider to which section 298 applies must file with the Minister, before May 2 of each calendar year, an information return in prescribed form containing the information that is required to be reported in respect of the preceding calendar year under this Part.
The information return required under subsection (1) must be filed by way of electronic filing.

Every reporting crypto-asset service provider must keep, at the service provider√¢¬Ä¬ôs place of business or at such other place as may be designated by the Minister, records that the service provider obtains or creates for the purpose of complying with this Part, including self-certifications and records of documentary evidence.

Every reporting crypto-asset service provider required by this Part to keep records that does so electronically must retain them in an electronically readable format for the retention period referred to in subsection (3).

Every reporting crypto-asset service provider that is required to keep, obtain or create records under this Part must retain those records for a period of at least six years following the end of the last calendar year in respect of which the record is relevant.

Section.Text: If an individual or an entity enters into an arrangement or engages in a practice, the primary purpose of which can reasonably be considered to be to avoid an obligation under this Part, this Part applies as if the individual or entity had not entered into the arrangement or engaged in the practice.

Subsection.Text: Every reportable person and every crypto-asset user that has a controlling person that is a reportable person must provide their TIN and the TIN of each of their controlling persons, if applicable, at the request of a reporting crypto-asset service provider that is required under this Part to make an information return requiring the TIN.

A person required to make an information return referred to in subsection (1) must not knowingly use, communicate or allow to be communicated, other than as required or authorized under this Act or a regulation, the TIN without the written consent of the person to whom the TIN relates.

Every reportable person and every crypto-asset user that has a controlling person that is a reportable person who fails to provide on request their TIN (or that of a controlling person that is a reportable person) to a reporting crypto-asset service provider that is required under this Part to make an information return requiring the TIN is liable to a penalty of $500 for each such failure, unless

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an application for the assignment of the TIN is made to the relevant reportable jurisdiction not later than 90 days after the request was made and the TIN is provided to the reporting crypto-asset service provider that requested it within 15 days after the reportable person or entity to whom the TIN relates received it; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the reportable person or the crypto-asset user that has a controlling person that is a reportable person is not eligible to obtain a TIN from the relevant reportable jurisdiction (including because the relevant reportable jurisdiction does not issue TINs).

Subsection.Text: The Minister may at any time assess any amount payable under subsection (3) by any person and, if the Minister sends a notice of assessment to the person, sections 150 to 163, subsections 164(1) and (1.4) to (7), sections 165 to 167 and Division J of Part I apply with such modifications as the circumstances require.
Subsection (1) applies to the 2027 and subsequent calendar years.

The definition section 325 avoidance transaction in subsection 285.03(1) of the Excise Tax Act is amended by striking out √¢¬Ä¬úor√¢¬Ä¬ù at the end of paragraph (a), by adding √¢¬Ä¬úor√¢¬Ä¬ù at the end of paragraph (b) and by adding the following after paragraph (b):

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: property is deemed under subsection 325(7) to have been transferred as part of the transaction or series of transactions. (op√É¬©ration d√¢¬Ä¬ô√É¬©vitement de l√¢¬Ä¬ôarticle 325)

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on April 16, 2024.

Section.Text: Paragraph 298(3)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: with the consent in writing of the person, to dispose of an appeal of the person or another person; or

Subsection.Text: Section 325 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (5):
Subsection (7) applies in respect of a transaction or series of transactions if, as part of the transaction or series of transactions, the following conditions are satisfied:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a person (in this subsection referred to as the √¢¬Ä¬úplanner√¢¬Ä¬ù) transfers property, either directly or indirectly, by means of a trust or by any other means, to a person (in this subsection and subsection (7) referred to as the √¢¬Ä¬úacquirer√¢¬Ä¬ù), or a person not dealing at arm√¢¬Ä¬ôs length with the acquirer, pursuant to the direction of or with the concurrence of the acquirer;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a person (in this subsection and subsection (7) referred to as the √¢¬Ä¬úconveyor√¢¬Ä¬ù) transfers property, either directly or indirectly, by means of a trust or by any other means, to the planner or any other person; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: it is reasonable to conclude that one of the purposes of undertaking or arranging the transaction or series of transactions is to avoid joint and several, or solidary, liability of the acquirer and the conveyor under this section for an amount payable or remittable under this Part.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this section, if the conditions set out in subsection (6) are satisfied in respect of a transaction or series of transactions, the following rules apply:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the conveyor is deemed to have transferred the property referred to in paragraph (6)(b) to the acquirer as part of the transaction or series of transactions; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the conveyor is deemed to be a transferor, and the acquirer is deemed to be a transferee, in respect of the transaction or series of transactions.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (9) applies in respect of a transaction or series of transactions if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the transaction or series of transactions is a section 325 avoidance transaction (as defined in subsection 285.03(1)) that is described in paragraph (a) or (c) of that definition; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the transaction or series of transactions is a section 325 avoidance transaction (as defined in subsection 285.03(1)) that is described in paragraph (b) of that definition and it is reasonable to conclude that one of the purposes of undertaking or arranging the transaction or series of transactions is to avoid joint and several, or solidary, liability of the transferee and the transferor under this section for an amount payable or remittable under this Part.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this section, if the conditions set out in paragraph (8)(a) or (b) are satisfied in respect of a transaction or series of transactions, in determining the amount that the transferee and the transferor are jointly and severally, or solidarily, liable to pay under this section, the fair market value of the consideration given by the transferee for any property transferred as part of the transaction or series of transactions is deemed to be nil.
Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on April 16, 2024.
The Income Tax Conventions Implementation Act, 1996 is amended by adding the following after section 7:

Despite sections 4 and 7, the Agreement is deemed to be suspended and of no force or effect throughout the period beginning on November 18, 2024 and ending on the day before the recommencement date, to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council made on the recommendation of the Minister of Finance after consultation with the Minister of Foreign Affairs, when the Agreement is no longer suspended and recommences to be of force and effect.
An order made under subsection (1) may, if it so provides, have retroactive effect.
Throughout the period referred to in subsection (1),

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: all obligations and liabilities of His Majesty in right of Canada and all rights and privileges of anyone under the Agreement, or arising in connection with the Agreement, are deemed to be extinguished; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any provision in sections 2 to 7 that has the effect of providing for, or permitting, anything that, in substance, is contrary to this section is of no force or effect.

Subsection.Text: No one is entitled to any compensation from His Majesty in right of Canada in connection with the coming into force of this section.
Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on November 18, 2024.

Subsection.Text: Section 297 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (6):
Subsection (8) applies in respect of a transaction or series of transactions if, as part of the transaction or series of transactions, the following conditions are satisfied:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a person (in this subsection referred to as the √¢¬Ä¬úplanner√¢¬Ä¬ù) transfers property, either directly or indirectly, by means of a trust or by any other means, to a person (in this subsection and subsection (8) referred to as the √¢¬Ä¬úacquirer√¢¬Ä¬ù), or a person not dealing at arm√¢¬Ä¬ôs length with the acquirer, pursuant to the direction of or with the concurrence of the acquirer;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a person (in this subsection and subsection (8) referred to as the √¢¬Ä¬úconveyor√¢¬Ä¬ù) transfers property, either directly or indirectly, by means of a trust or by any other means, to the planner or any other person; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: it is reasonable to conclude that one of the purposes of undertaking or arranging the transaction or series of transactions is to avoid joint and several, or solidary, liability of the acquirer and the conveyor under this section for an amount payable under this Act.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this section, if the conditions set out in subsection (7) are satisfied in respect of a transaction or series of transactions, the following rules apply:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the conveyor is deemed to have transferred the property referred to in paragraph (7)(b) to the acquirer as part of the transaction or series of transactions; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the conveyor is deemed to be a transferor, and the acquirer is deemed to be a transferee, in respect of the transaction or series of transactions.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (10) applies in respect of a transaction or series of transactions if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the transaction or series of transactions is an avoidance transaction that is described in paragraph (a) or (c) of the definition avoidance transaction in subsection (0.1); or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the transaction or series of transactions is an avoidance transaction that is described in paragraph (b) of the definition avoidance transaction in subsection (0.1) and it is reasonable to conclude that one of the purposes of undertaking or arranging the transaction or series of transactions is to avoid joint and several, or solidary, liability of the transferee and the transferor under this section for an amount payable under this Act.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this section, if the conditions set out in paragraph (9)(a) or (b) are satisfied in respect of a transaction or series of transactions, in determining the amount that the transferee and the transferor are jointly and severally, or solidarily, liable to pay under this section, the fair market value of the consideration given by the transferee for any property transferred as part of the transaction or series of transactions is deemed to be nil.
Subsections (1) and (2) are deemed to have come into force on April 16, 2024.

Section 1102 of the Regulations is amended by adding the following after subsection (25):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Acquisition cost of eligible manufacturing buildings
Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this Part and Schedule II, if a building of a taxpayer was under construction on November 4, 2025 and the building would be an eligible manufacturing building (as defined in subsection 1104(2)) if that definition were read without reference to its paragraph (b), the portion, if any, of the capital cost of the building that was incurred by the taxpayer before November 4, 2025 and has not been deducted under paragraph 20(1)(a) of the Act is deemed to have been incurred by the taxpayer on November 4, 2025 unless the taxpayer elects (in the taxpayer√¢¬Ä¬ôs return of income under Part I of the Act for the taxation year in which the building was acquired) that this subsection not apply to that cost.
Subsections (1) to (3) are deemed to have come into force on November 4, 2025.

Subparagraph.Text: the trust satisfies the conditions in subparagraphs (b)(i) to (vi) of the definition investment fund in subsection 251.2(1) of the Act, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the investments of the trust are managed by a person that is registered as an investment fund manager as described in National Instrument 31-103 Registration Requirements, Exemptions and Ongoing Registrant Obligations, as amended from time to time, of the Canadian Securities Administrators;

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on November 4, 2025.
Part XLIX of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Heading.Label: PART L
Heading.TitleText: Qualified and Prohibited Investments for Registered Plans

Section.Text: The following definitions apply in this Part.

Definition.Text: connected person, under a registered plan (as defined in subsection 207.01(1) of the Act) governing a trust, means a person that is a controlling individual (as defined in subsection 207.01(1) of the Act) of the plan or a beneficiary of the trust and any other person or partnership that does not deal at arm√¢¬Ä¬ôs length with that person.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(personne rattach√É¬©e)
specified cooperative corporation√¢¬Ä¬Çmeans

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a cooperative corporation as defined in subsection 136(2) of the Act; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a corporation that would be a cooperative corporation as defined in subsection 136(2) of the Act if the purpose described in that subsection were the purpose of providing employment to the corporation√¢¬Ä¬ôs members or customers.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(coop√É¬©rative d√É¬©termin√É¬©e)

Definition.Text: specified small business corporation, at any time, means a corporation (other than a cooperative corporation as defined in subsection 136(2) of the Act) that would, at that time or at the end of the last taxation year of the corporation that ended before that time, be a small business corporation if the reference to √¢¬Ä¬úCanadian-controlled private corporation√¢¬Ä¬ù in the definition small business corporation in subsection 248(1) of the Act were read as a reference to √¢¬Ä¬úCanadian corporation (other than a corporation controlled at that time, directly or indirectly in any manner whatever, by one or more non-resident persons)√¢¬Ä¬ù.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(soci√É¬©t√É¬© d√É¬©termin√É¬©e exploitant une petite entreprise)

Section.Text: For the purposes of paragraph (l) of the definition qualified investment in subsection 207.01(1) of the Act, each of the following investments is prescribed as a qualified investment for a trust governed by a registered plan at a particular time if at that time it is:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an indebtedness of a Canadian corporation represented by a bankers√¢¬Ä¬ô acceptance;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a bond, debenture, note or similar obligation of a Canadian corporation if

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: payment of the principal amount of the obligation and the interest on the principal amount is guaranteed by a corporation or a mutual fund trust whose shares or units, as the case may be, are listed on a designated stock exchange in Canada,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the corporation is controlled directly or indirectly by one or more corporations or mutual fund trusts described in subparagraph (i), or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: payment of the principal amount of which is guaranteed by His Majesty in right of a province under the Community Development Bonds Act, C.C.S.M., c. C160;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a bond, debenture, note or similar obligation issued by, or a deposit with, a credit union;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a bond, debenture, note or similar obligation issued by a cooperative corporation (as defined in 136(2) of the Act) if

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: throughout the taxation year of the corporation immediately preceding the year in which the obligation was acquired by the trust, the corporation had at least 100 shareholders or, if all its shareholders were corporations, 50 shareholders, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: no more than 5% of the fair market value of all obligations of the corporation are held by one or more trusts that share the same controlling individual (as defined in subsection 207.01(1) of the Act) or beneficiary;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: a bond, debenture, note or similar obligation of a Canadian corporation if, at the time the obligation is acquired by the trust, the corporation that issued the obligation is a corporation that

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: is, or is controlled by, a corporation that has issued and outstanding share capital carried in its books at not less than $25 million, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: has issued and outstanding bonds, debentures, notes or similar obligations having in the aggregate a principal amount of at least $10 million

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: that are held by at least 300 different persons,

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: for which a prospectus, registration statement or similar document was filed with and, where required by law, accepted for filing by a public authority in Canada in accordance with the laws of Canada or a province, and

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: for which there was a lawful distribution to the public in accordance with the document described in clause (B);

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: a debt issued by a Canadian corporation (other than a corporation with share capital) if the taxable income of the corporation is exempt from tax under Part I of the Act because of paragraph 149(1)(l) of the Act and

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: before the particular time and after 1995, the corporation

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: acquired, for a total consideration of not less than $25 million, property from His Majesty in right of Canada or a province, and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: put that property to a use that is the same as or similar to the use to which the property was put before the acquisition described in clause (A), or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: at the time of the acquisition of the debt by the trust, it was reasonable to expect that subparagraph (i) would apply in respect of the debt no later than one year after the time of the acquisition;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: a debt issued by a Canadian corporation (other than a corporation with share capital) if the taxable income of the corporation is exempt from tax under Part I of the Act because of paragraph 149(1)(l) of the Act and

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the debt is issued by the corporation as part of an issue of debt by the corporation for an amount of at least $25 million, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: at the time of the acquisition of the debt by the trust, the corporation had issued debt as part of a single issue for an amount of at least $25 million;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: a debt obligation of a debtor, or an interest (or for civil law a right) in that debt obligation, if it is

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: fully secured by a mortgage, charge, hypothec or similar instrument in respect of real or immovable property situated in Canada, or would be fully secured were it not for a decline in the fair market value of the property after the debt obligation was issued, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: secured by a mortgage, charge, hypothec or similar instrument in respect of real or immovable property situated in Canada and is

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: administered by an approved lender under the National Housing Act or a qualified mortgage lender under the Protection of Residential Mortgage or Hypothecary Insurance Act, and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: insured under the National Housing Act or the Protection of Residential Mortgage or Hypothecary Insurance Act; or

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: a certificate evidencing an undivided interest, or for civil law an undivided right, in one or more properties if

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: all or substantially all of the fair market value of the certificate is attributable to property that is, or is incidental to, a debt obligation secured by

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: a mortgage, charge, hypothec or similar instrument in respect of real or immovable property situated in Canada, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: property described in paragraph (a) or subparagraph (c)(i) of the definition qualified investment in subsection 207.01(1) of the Act that was substituted for the security referred to in clause (A) under the terms of the debt obligation,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the certificate, at the time of its acquisition by the trust, has an investment-grade rating with a credit rating agency referred to in section 5005, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the certificate is issued as part of an issue of certificates by the issuer for a total amount of at least $25 million.

Section.Text: For the purposes of paragraph (l) of the definition qualified investment in subsection 207.01(1) of the Act, each of the following investments is prescribed as a qualified investment for a trust governed by a registered plan at a particular time if at that time it is:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a share of the capital stock of a mortgage investment corporation;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a share of, or similar interest in, a credit union;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: an American Depositary Receipt where the property represented by the receipt is listed on a designated stock exchange;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a share of the capital stock of a specified small business corporation;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: a share of the capital stock of a venture capital corporation described in any of sections 6700 to 6700.2; or

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: a share of the capital or capital stock of a specified cooperative corporation.

Section.Text: For the purposes of paragraph (l) of the definition qualified investment in subsection 207.01(1) of the Act, each of the following investments is prescribed as a qualified investment for a trust governed by a registered plan at a particular time if at that time it is a unit of a trust that:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is subject to, and substantially complies with, the requirements of National Instrument 81-102 Investment Funds, as amended from time to time, of the Canadian Securities Administrators;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: meets the following conditions:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the trust has a class of units outstanding that

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: has been lawfully distributed in a province to the public and a prospectus, registration statement or similar document was not, under the laws of the province, required to be filed in respect of the distribution, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: is qualified for distribution to the public,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the trust satisfies the conditions in subparagraphs (b)(i) to (vi) of the definition investment fund in subsection 251.2(1) of the Act, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the investments of the trust are managed by a person that is registered as an investment fund manager as described in National Instrument 31-103 Registration Requirements, Exemptions and Ongoing Registrant Obligations, as amended from time to time, of the Canadian Securities Administrators;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: is a mutual fund trust (or would be a mutual fund trust if it had been created after 1999); or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: is a small business investment trust (within the meaning assigned by subsection 5103(1)) that

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: was acquired by the trust before January 1, 2027,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: was a qualified investment for the trust at the time it was acquired, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: would, at the particular time, be a qualified investment for the trust under these Regulations as they read on December 31, 2026.

Section.Text: For the purposes of paragraph (l) of the definition qualified investment in subsection 207.01(1) of the Act, each of the following investments is prescribed as a qualified investment for a trust governed by a registered plan at a particular time if at that time it is:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an option, a warrant or a similar right (each of which is, in this paragraph, referred to as the √¢¬Ä¬úsecurity√¢¬Ä¬ù) issued by a person or partnership (in this paragraph referred to as the √¢¬Ä¬úissuer√¢¬Ä¬ù) that gives the holder the right to acquire, either immediately or in the future, property all of which is a qualified investment for the plan trust or to receive a cash settlement in lieu of delivery of that property, where the property is

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
