Subparagraph.Text: a share of the capital stock of, a unit of, or a debt issued by, the issuer or another person or partnership that does not, when the security is issued, deal at arm√¢¬Ä¬ôs length with the issuer; or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a warrant issued by the issuer or another person or partnership that does not, when the security is issued, deal at arm√¢¬Ä¬ôs length with the issuer and that gives the holder the right to acquire a share or unit described in subparagraph (i);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: gold (with a minimum fineness of 995 parts per 1000) or silver (with a minimum fineness of 999 parts per 1000), in the form of or represented by

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a legal tender bullion coin produced by the Royal Canadian Mint that

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: has a fair market value at the particular time not exceeding 110 per cent of the fair market value of the coin√¢¬Ä¬ôs gold or silver content, and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: is acquired by the trust directly from the Royal Canadian Mint or from a bank, a trust company, a credit union, an insurance corporation or a registered securities dealer that is resident in Canada and subject by law to the supervision of a regulating authority that is the Superintendent of Financial Institutions or a similar authority of a province,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a bullion bar, ingot or wafer produced by a refiner on the London Bullion Market Association√¢¬Ä¬ôs good delivery list for gold or silver, as the case may be, that is

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: stamped with the fineness, weight and the refiner√¢¬Ä¬ôs hallmark, and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: acquired directly from the refiner or a person described in clause (i)(B), or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: a certificate issued by a person described in clause (i)(B) representing a claim of the holder of the certificate to property held by the issuer of the certificate if

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the property would be property described in subparagraph (i) or (ii) if those subparagraphs were read without regard to their clause (B), and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the certificate is acquired by the trust directly from the issuer of the certificate or a person described in clause (i)(B); or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: an interest of a limited partner in a small business investment limited partnership (within the meaning assigned by subsection 5102(1)) that

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: was acquired by the trust before January 1, 2027,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: was a qualified investment for the trust at the time it was acquired, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: would, at the particular time, be a qualified investment for the trust under these Regulations as they read on December 31, 2026.

Section.Text: For the purposes of subparagraphs (c)(v) and (vi) of the definition qualified investment in subsection 207.01(1) of the Act, each of the following is a prescribed credit rating agency:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: A.M. Best Company, Inc.;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: DBRS Limited;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: Fitch Ratings, Inc.;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: Moody√¢¬Ä¬ôs Investors Service, Inc.;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: Standard & Poor√¢¬Ä¬ôs Financial Services LLC; and

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: a subsidiary or affiliate of a company listed in any of paragraphs (a) to (e), to the extent that it provides credit rating services outside of Canada on behalf of the company in respect of which it is the subsidiary or affiliate.

Section.Text: For the purposes of paragraph (d) of the definition prohibited investment in subsection 207.01(1) of the Act, each of the following is a prescribed property for a trust at a particular time if at that time it is:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a share of the capital stock of a mortgage investment corporation that holds any indebtedness, whether by way of mortgage or otherwise, of a person who is a connected person under the registered plan governing the trust;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an option, a warrant or a similar right that is issued by a connected person under the registered plan governing the trust;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a bond, debenture, note or similar obligation issued by, or a deposit with, a credit union that granted any benefit or privilege as a result of the trust holding a share or obligation of, or a deposit with, the credit union to a person who is a connected person under the registered plan governing the trust, if the benefit was granted during the calendar year in which the particular time occurs or, where the person continues to enjoy the benefit or privilege, in a prior year;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a bond, debenture, note or similar obligation issued by a cooperative corporation (as defined in subsection 136(2) of the Act) that granted any benefit or privilege as a result of the trust holding a share or obligation of the corporation to a person who is a connected person under the registered plan governing the trust, if the benefit was granted during the calendar year in which the particular time occurs or, where the person continues to enjoy the benefit or privilege, in a prior year;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: a bankers√¢¬Ä¬ô acceptance that represents an indebtedness of a Canadian corporation that is a connected person under the registered plan governing the trust; and

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: a share of the capital or capital stock of a specified cooperative corporation if

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: ownership of the share or a share identical to the share is a condition of membership in the corporation, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a connected person under the registered plan governing the trust

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: has received a payment from the corporation because of an allocation in proportion to patronage (as defined in subsection 135(4) of the Act) in respect of consumer goods or services (as defined in subsection 135(4) of the Act), or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: can reasonably be expected to receive a payment described in clause (A), after the acquisition of the share by the plan trust.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) comes into force on January 1, 2027.
The heading of Part LI of the Regulations is replaced by the following:
Subsection (1) comes into force on January 1, 2027.
The definition specified property in subsection 5100(1) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: specified property√¢¬Ä¬Çmeans property described in paragraph (a) or (b), any of subparagraphs (c)(i) to (iv) or paragraph (f) of the definition qualified investment in subsection 207.01(1) of the Act.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(bien d√É¬©termin√É¬©)

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) comes into force on January 1, 2027.

Section 5600 of the Regulations is amended by striking out √¢¬Ä¬úand√¢¬Ä¬ù at the end of paragraph (l), by adding √¢¬Ä¬úand√¢¬Ä¬ù at the end paragraph (m) and by adding the following after paragraph (m):

Paragraph.Label: (n)
Paragraph.Text: the distribution by Novartis AG, on October 4, 2023 to its common shareholders, of common shares of Sandoz Group AG.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on October 4, 2023.
Paragraph (a) of the definition exempt deficit in subsection 5907(1) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the total of all amounts each of which is an amount determined at that time under any of subparagraphs (i) to (vii) of the description of B in the definition exempt surplus

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph (vi) of the description of A in the definition exempt surplus in subsection 5907(1) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

FormulaParagraph.Label: (vi)
FormulaParagraph.Text: an amount added to the exempt surplus of the subject affiliate or deducted from its exempt deficit in the period and before the particular time under subsection (1.092), (1.1), (1.14), (1.17) or (1.2),

Subsection.Text: The description of B in the definition exempt surplus in subsection 5907(1) of the Regulations is amended by striking out √¢¬Ä¬úor√¢¬Ä¬ù at the end of subparagraph (v) and by replacing subparagraph (vi) with the following:

FormulaParagraph.Label: (vi)
FormulaParagraph.Text: an amount, in the period and before the particular time, deducted from the exempt surplus of the subject affiliate or added to its exempt deficit under subsection (1.092), (1.1), (1.14), (1.17) or (1.2), or

FormulaParagraph.Label: (vii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the portion of any income or profits tax paid, in the period and before the particular time, to the government of a country by the subject affiliate under a domestic minimum top-up tax regime if that portion can reasonably be considered to be in respect of income or profits, as determined under that tax regime, of the subject affiliate that are derived from an activity the income, profit or gains from which would be included in computing the subject affiliate√¢¬Ä¬ôs exempt earnings; (surplus exon√É¬©r√É¬©)

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (a) of the definition hybrid deficit in subsection 5907(1) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the total of all amounts each of which is an amount determined at that time under any of subparagraphs (i) to (viii) of the description of B in the definition hybrid surplus

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph (v) of the description of A in the definition hybrid surplus in subsection 5907(1) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

FormulaParagraph.Label: (v)
FormulaParagraph.Text: an amount added to the hybrid surplus of the subject affiliate or deducted from its hybrid deficit in the period and before the particular time under subsection (1.092), (1.1), (1.14), (1.17) or (1.2), and

Subsection.Text: The description of B in the definition hybrid surplus in subsection 5907(1) of the Regulations is amended by striking out √¢¬Ä¬úor√¢¬Ä¬ù at the end of subparagraph (vi) and by replacing subparagraph (vii) with the following:

FormulaParagraph.Label: (vii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: an amount deducted from the hybrid surplus of the subject affiliate or added to its hybrid deficit in the period and before the particular time under subsection (1.092), (1.1), (1.14), (1.17) or (1.2), or

FormulaParagraph.Label: (viii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the portion of any income or profits tax paid, in the period and before the particular time, to the government of a country by the subject affiliate under a domestic minimum top-up tax regime if that portion can reasonably be considered to be in respect of income or profits, as determined under that tax regime, of the subject affiliate that are derived from an activity the income, profit or gains from which would be included in computing the subject affiliate√¢¬Ä¬ôs hybrid surplus; (surplus hybride)

Subsection.Text: The description of A in the definition hybrid underlying tax in subsection 5907(1) of the Regulations is amended by striking out √¢¬Ä¬úor√¢¬Ä¬ù at the end of subparagraph (iii) and by replacing subparagraph (iv) with the following:

FormulaParagraph.Label: (iv)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the amount by which the subject affiliate√¢¬Ä¬ôs hybrid underlying tax is required to be increased in the period and before the particular time under subsection (1.092), (1.1), (1.17) or (1.2), or

FormulaParagraph.Label: (v)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the portion of any income or profits tax referred to in subparagraph (viii) of the description of B in the definition hybrid surplus,

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph (iv) of the description of B in the definition hybrid underlying tax in subsection 5907(1) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

FormulaParagraph.Label: (iv)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the amount by which the subject affiliate√¢¬Ä¬ôs hybrid underlying tax is required to be decreased in the period and before the particular time under subsection (1.092), (1.1), (1.17) or (1.2);√¢¬Ä¬Ç(montant intrins√É¬®que d√¢¬Ä¬ôimp√É¬¥t hybride)

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (a) of the definition taxable deficit in subsection 5907(1) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the total of all amounts each of which is an amount determined at that time under any of subparagraphs (i) to (vii) of the description of B in the definition taxable surplus

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph (iv) of the description of A in the definition taxable surplus in subsection 5907(1) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

FormulaParagraph.Label: (iv)
FormulaParagraph.Text: an amount added to the taxable surplus of the subject affiliate or deducted from its taxable deficit in the period and before the particular time under subsection (1.092), (1.1), (1.14), (1.17) or (1.2),

Subsection.Text: The description of B in the definition taxable surplus in subsection 5907(1) of the Regulations is amended by striking out √¢¬Ä¬úor√¢¬Ä¬ù at the end of subparagraph (v) and by replacing subparagraph (vi) with the following:

FormulaParagraph.Label: (vi)
FormulaParagraph.Text: an amount, in the period and before the particular time, deducted from the taxable surplus of the subject affiliate or added to its taxable deficit under subsection (1.092), (1.1), (1.14), (1.17) or (1.2), or

FormulaParagraph.Label: (vii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the portion of any income or profits tax paid, in the period and before the particular time, to the government of a country by the subject affiliate under a domestic minimum top-up tax regime if that portion can reasonably be considered to be in respect of income or profits, as determined under that tax regime, of the subject affiliate that are derived from an activity the income, profit or gains from which would be included in computing the subject affiliate√¢¬Ä¬ôs taxable earnings; (surplus imposable)

Subsection.Text: The description of A in the definition underlying foreign tax in subsection 5907(1) of the Regulations is amended by striking out √¢¬Ä¬úor√¢¬Ä¬ù at the end of subparagraph (iv) and by replacing subparagraph (v) with the following:

FormulaParagraph.Label: (v)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the amount by which the subject affiliate√¢¬Ä¬ôs underlying foreign tax is required to be increased in the period and before the particular time under subsection (1.092), (1.1), (1.17) or (1.2), or

FormulaParagraph.Label: (vi)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the portion of any income or profits tax referred to in subparagraph (vii) of the description of B in the definition taxable surplus,

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph (iv) of the description of B in the definition underlying foreign tax in subsection 5907(1) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

FormulaParagraph.Label: (iv)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the amount by which the subject affiliate√¢¬Ä¬ôs underlying foreign tax is required to be decreased in the period and before the particular time under subsection (1.092), (1.1), (1.17) or (1.2);√¢¬Ä¬Ç(montant intrins√É¬®que d√¢¬Ä¬ôimp√É¬¥t √É¬©tranger)

Subsection.Text: Subsection 5907(1) of the Regulations is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: DMTT group, for a fiscal year, means one or more entities that are, for the fiscal year, members of the same MNE group (as defined in subsection 10(1) of the Global Minimum Tax Act) and subject to a country√¢¬Ä¬ôs domestic minimum top-up tax regime;√¢¬Ä¬Ç(groupe d√¢¬Ä¬òICMN)

domestic minimum top-up amount, for a fiscal year, of a foreign affiliate of a corporation resident in Canada that is subject to a domestic minimum top-up tax regime of a country, means the total of all amounts, each of which is a portion of the income or profits tax paid to the government of the country under that tax regime for the fiscal year that can reasonably be considered to be in respect of income or profits, as determined under that regime, of the affiliate that are derived from an activity the income, profit or gains from which would be included in computing the affiliate√¢¬Ä¬ôs exempt earnings, hybrid surplus or taxable earnings, as the case may be;√¢¬Ä¬Ç(montant compl√É¬©mentaire minimum national)

domestic minimum top-up tax regime√¢¬Ä¬Çmeans any provisions under the tax laws of a country that can reasonably be considered to have been enacted or otherwise brought into effect by the country with the intention of implementing a √¢¬Ä¬úQualified Domestic Minimum Top-up Tax√¢¬Ä¬ù within the meaning of the Tax Challenges Arising from the Digitalisation of the Economy √¢¬Ä¬î Global Anti-Base Erosion Model Rules (Pillar Two), published by the Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development;√¢¬Ä¬Ç(r√É¬©gime d√¢¬Ä¬ôimp√É¬¥t compl√É¬©mentaire minimum national)

fiscal year, in respect of income or profits tax paid under a domestic minimum top-up tax regime, means the accounting period for which the income or profits subject to tax under that regime are determined;√¢¬Ä¬Ç(ann√É¬©e financi√É¬®re)

Subsection.Text: Section 5907 of the Regulations is amended by adding the following after subsection (1.01):

Except as otherwise expressly provided in this Part, no income or profits tax paid under a domestic minimum top-up tax regime is to be taken into account in determining any amounts referred to in the definitions exempt earnings, exempt surplus, hybrid surplus, hybrid underlying tax, net earnings, taxable earnings, taxable surplus and underlying foreign tax in subsection (1).
Section 5907 of the Regulations is amended by adding the following after subsection (1.13):

For the purposes of this Part, if a foreign affiliate (referred to in this subsection as the √¢¬Ä¬úprimary affiliate√¢¬Ä¬ù) of a corporation resident in Canada pays income or profits tax to the government of a country under a domestic minimum top-up tax regime for a fiscal year on behalf of itself and other foreign affiliates (referred to in this subsection as the √¢¬Ä¬úsecondary affiliates√¢¬Ä¬ù) of the corporation resident in Canada that are, together with the primary affiliate, members of a DMTT group, the following rules apply:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of the primary affiliate,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: any of the income or profits tax paid by the primary affiliate for the fiscal year that exceeds the primary affiliate√¢¬Ä¬ôs domestic minimum top-up amount for the fiscal year is deemed, other than for the purposes of this subsection, subsection (1.16) and (1.17) and paragraph (1.18)(b), not to have been paid by the primary affiliate, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the domestic minimum top-up amount of a secondary affiliate is, at the end of the fiscal year,

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: to the extent that the income or profits tax would be, if it were paid to the government by the secondary affiliate, taken into account in determining the secondary affiliate√¢¬Ä¬ôs exempt surplus or exempt deficit because of subparagraph (vii) of the description of B in the definition exempt surplus in subsection (1), to be deducted from the exempt surplus or added to the exempt deficit, as the case may be, of the primary affiliate,

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: to the extent that the income or profits tax would be, if it were paid to the government by the secondary affiliate, taken into account in determining the secondary affiliate√¢¬Ä¬ôs hybrid surplus or hybrid deficit because of subparagraph (viii) of the description of B in the definition hybrid surplus in subsection (1), to be deducted from the hybrid surplus or added to the hybrid deficit, as the case may be, of the primary affiliate, and

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: to the extent that the income or profits tax would be, if it were paid to the government by the secondary affiliate, taken into account in determining the secondary affiliate√¢¬Ä¬ôs taxable surplus or taxable deficit because of subparagraph (vii) of the description of B in the definition taxable surplus in subsection (1), to be deducted from the taxable surplus or added to the taxable deficit, as the case may be, of the primary affiliate; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if, because the primary affiliate pays income or profits tax under the domestic minimum top-up tax regime for the fiscal year on behalf of the secondary affiliates, an amount is paid to the primary affiliate by a secondary affiliate in respect of the domestic minimum top-up amount of the secondary affiliate for the fiscal year,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: in respect of the secondary affiliate, the amount so paid is deemed to be a payment of that income or profits tax to the government of a country by the secondary affiliate under that tax regime for the fiscal year, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in respect of the primary affiliate,

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the portion of the amount so paid that is taken into account in determining the secondary affiliate√¢¬Ä¬ôs exempt surplus or exempt deficit because of subparagraph (vii) of the description of B in the definition exempt surplus in subsection (1) is, at the end of the fiscal year, to be added to the exempt surplus or deducted from the exempt deficit, as the case may be, of the primary affiliate,

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the portion of the amount so paid that is taken into account in determining the secondary affiliate√¢¬Ä¬ôs hybrid surplus or hybrid deficit because of subparagraph (viii) of the description of B in the definition hybrid surplus in subsection (1) is, at the end of the fiscal year, to be added to the hybrid surplus or deducted from the hybrid deficit, as the case may be, of the primary affiliate, and

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: the portion of the amount so paid that is taken into account in determining the secondary affiliate√¢¬Ä¬ôs taxable surplus or taxable deficit because of subparagraph (vii) of the description of B in the definition taxable surplus in subsection (1) is, at the end of the fiscal year, to be added to the taxable surplus or deducted from the taxable deficit, as the case may be, of the primary affiliate.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this subsection and subsections (1.14) and (1.16) to (1.192),

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a non-resident corporation is deemed to be, at any time, a foreign affiliate (referred to in this subsection as a √¢¬Ä¬údeemed affiliate√¢¬Ä¬ù) of a particular corporation resident in Canada if at that time

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the deemed affiliate and the particular corporation are both members of the same MNE group (as defined in subsection 10(1) of the Global Minimum Tax Act), and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the deemed affiliate is a member of the same DMTT group referred to in subsection (1.14) as a foreign affiliate (other than a deemed affiliate) of the particular corporation; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for any taxation year, any activities of a deemed affiliate that, in the absence of paragraph (a), would not be a foreign affiliate of a corporation resident in Canada are deemed to be activities the income, profit or gains from which would be included in computing the deemed affiliate√¢¬Ä¬ôs exempt earnings.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1.17) applies in respect of income or profits tax paid under a domestic minimum top-up tax regime of a country by a foreign affiliate (in this subsection and subsection (1.17) referred to as the √¢¬Ä¬úshareholder affiliate√¢¬Ä¬ù) of a corporation resident in Canada for a fiscal year of the shareholder affiliate if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the shareholder affiliate pays the income or profits tax in respect of its income or profits, as determined under that domestic minimum top-up tax regime, for the fiscal year;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the income or profits tax is paid to the government of that country;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the shareholder affiliate has an equity percentage in another foreign affiliate (in this subsection and subsection (1.17) referred to as the √¢¬Ä¬útransparent affiliate√¢¬Ä¬ù) of the taxpayer; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a portion of the financial accounting income (as defined in subsection 17(1) of the Global Minimum Tax Act) of the transparent affiliate for the fiscal year is taken into account in determining the shareholder affiliate√¢¬Ä¬ôs income or profits, as determined under that tax regime.

Subsection.Text: If this subsection applies in respect of income or profits tax paid by a shareholder affiliate for a fiscal year

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of the shareholder affiliate,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: despite subparagraph (1.14)(b)(i), any of the income or profits tax paid by the shareholder affiliate that exceeds the shareholder affiliate√¢¬Ä¬ôs domestic minimum top-up amount for the fiscal year is deemed not to have been paid by the shareholder affiliate, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the portion of the income or profits tax deemed not to have been paid by the shareholder affiliate under subparagraph (i) for the fiscal year is, at the end of the fiscal year,

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: to the extent that the portion can reasonably be considered to be in respect of income or profits, as determined under the tax regime referred to in subsection (1.16), that are derived from an activity the income, profit or gains from which would be included in computing the transparent affiliate√¢¬Ä¬ôs exempt earnings, to be deducted from the exempt surplus or added to the exempt deficit, as the case may be, of the shareholder affiliate,

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: to the extent that the portion can reasonably be considered to be in respect of income or profits, as determined under the tax regime referred to in subsection (1.16), that are derived from an activity the income, profit or gains from which would be included in computing the transparent affiliate√¢¬Ä¬ôs hybrid surplus,

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: to be deducted from the hybrid surplus or added to the hybrid deficit, as the case may be, of the shareholder affiliate, and

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: to be added to the hybrid underlying tax of the shareholder affiliate, and

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: to the extent that the portion can reasonably be considered to be in respect of income or profits, as determined under the tax regime referred to in subsection (1.16), that are derived from an activity the income, profit or gains from which would be included in computing the transparent affiliate√¢¬Ä¬ôs taxable earnings,

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: to be deducted from the taxable surplus or added to the taxable deficit, as the case may be, of the shareholder affiliate, and

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: to be added to the underlying foreign tax of the shareholder affiliate; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: where an amount is paid to the shareholder affiliate by the transparent affiliate in respect of the portion of income or profits tax referred to in subparagraph (a)(i), as a result of the shareholder affiliate paying income or profits tax under the tax regime referred to in subsection (1.16) for the fiscal year because a portion of the financial accounting income (as defined in subsection 17(1) of the Global Minimum Tax Act) of the transparent affiliate for the fiscal year is taken into account in determining the shareholder affiliate√¢¬Ä¬ôs income or profits, as determined under that tax regime,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: in respect of the transparent affiliate, the amount so paid is deemed to be a payment of income or profits tax to the government of a country by the transparent affiliate under the tax regime referred to in subsection (1.16) for the fiscal year, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in respect of the shareholder affiliate,

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the portion of the amount so paid that is taken into account in determining the transparent affiliate√¢¬Ä¬ôs exempt surplus or exempt deficit because of subparagraph (vii) of the description of B in the definition exempt surplus in subsection (1) is, at the end of the fiscal year, to be added to the exempt surplus or deducted from the exempt deficit, as the case may be, of the shareholder affiliate,

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the portion of the amount so paid that is taken into account in determining the transparent affiliate√¢¬Ä¬ôs hybrid surplus or hybrid deficit because of subparagraph (viii) of the description of B in the definition hybrid surplus in subsection (1) is, at the end of the fiscal year,

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: to be added to the hybrid surplus or deducted from the hybrid deficit, as the case may be, of the shareholder affiliate, and

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: to be deducted from the hybrid underlying tax of the shareholder affiliate, and

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: the portion of the amount so paid that is taken into account in determining the transparent affiliate√¢¬Ä¬ôs taxable surplus or taxable deficit because of subparagraph (vii) of the description of B in the definition taxable surplus in subsection (1) is, at the end of the fiscal year,

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: to be added to the taxable surplus or deducted from the taxable deficit, as the case may be, of the shareholder affiliate, and

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: to be deducted from the underlying foreign tax of the shareholder affiliate.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1.19) applies in respect of a particular foreign affiliate of a corporation resident in Canada that is a secondary affiliate (within the meaning assigned by subsection (1.14)) and in respect of a foreign affiliate of the corporation that is the primary affiliate (within the meaning assigned by subsection (1.14)) in respect of the particular affiliate if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the particular affiliate has an equity percentage in another foreign affiliate (in this subsection and subsection (1.19) referred to as the √¢¬Ä¬útransparent affiliate√¢¬Ä¬ù);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the primary affiliate pays income or profits tax under the domestic minimum top-up tax regime referred to in subsection (1.14) for the fiscal year on behalf of the particular affiliate; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a portion of the financial accounting income (as defined in subsection 17(1) of the Global Minimum Tax Act) of the transparent affiliate for the fiscal year is taken into account in determining the particular affiliate√¢¬Ä¬ôs income or profits, as determined under that tax regime.

Subsection.Text: If this subsection applies in respect of a particular foreign affiliate and a primary affiliate (within the meaning assigned by subsection (1.14)), the following rules apply:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: for the purposes of subparagraphs (1.14)(a)(ii) and (b)(i), any portion (referred to in this subsection as the √¢¬Ä¬úrelevant portion√¢¬Ä¬ù) of the income or profits tax paid for the fiscal year under the domestic minimum top-up tax regime referred to in subsection (1.18) in respect of the particular affiliate√¢¬Ä¬ôs income or profits, as determined under that tax regime, that are derived from an activity the income, profit or gains from which would be included in computing the transparent affiliate√¢¬Ä¬ôs exempt earnings, hybrid surplus or taxable earnings is deemed to be included in the particular affiliate√¢¬Ä¬ôs domestic minimum top-up amount for the fiscal year;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: to the extent that the relevant portion is in respect of an activity the income, profit or gains from which would be included in computing the transparent affiliate√¢¬Ä¬ôs exempt earnings, it is deemed to be an amount that

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: for the purpose of subparagraph (1.14)(a)(ii), would, if it were paid by the particular affiliate, be taken into account in determining the particular affiliate√¢¬Ä¬ôs exempt surplus or exempt deficit, as the case may be, because of subparagraph (vii) of the description of B in the definition exempt surplus in subsection (1), and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: for the purpose of subparagraph (1.14)(b)(ii), is taken into account in determining the particular affiliate√¢¬Ä¬ôs exempt surplus or exempt deficit, as the case may be, because of subparagraph (vii) of the description of B in the definition exempt surplus in subsection (1);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: to the extent that the relevant portion is in respect of an activity the income, profit or gains from which would be included in computing the transparent affiliate√¢¬Ä¬ôs hybrid surplus, it is deemed to be an amount that

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: for the purpose of subparagraph (1.14)(a)(ii), would, if it were paid by the particular affiliate, be taken into account in determining the particular affiliate√¢¬Ä¬ôs hybrid surplus or hybrid deficit, as the case may be, because of subparagraph (viii) of the description of B in the definition hybrid surplus in subsection (1), and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: for the purpose of subparagraph (1.14)(b)(ii), is taken into account in determining the particular affiliate√¢¬Ä¬ôs hybrid surplus or hybrid deficit, as the case may be, because of subparagraph (viii) of the description of B in the definition hybrid surplus in subsection (1); and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: to the extent that the relevant portion is in respect of an activity the income, profit or gains from which would be included in computing the transparent affiliate√¢¬Ä¬ôs taxable earnings, it is deemed to be an amount that

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: for the purpose of subparagraph (1.14)(a)(ii), would, if it were paid by the particular affiliate, be taken into account in determining the particular affiliate√¢¬Ä¬ôs taxable surplus or taxable deficit, as the case may be, because of subparagraph (vii) of the description of B in the definition taxable surplus in subsection (1), and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: for the purpose of subparagraph (1.14)(b)(ii), is taken into account in determining the particular affiliate√¢¬Ä¬ôs taxable surplus or taxable deficit, as the case may be, because of subparagraph (vii) of the description of B in the definition taxable surplus in subsection (1).

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of subparagraph (vii) of the description of B in the definition exempt surplus in subsection (1), subparagraph (viii) of the description of B in the definition hybrid surplus in subsection (1), subparagraph (vii) of the description of B in the definition taxable surplus in subsection (1), subparagraph (v) of the description of A in the definition hybrid underlying tax in subsection (1) and subparagraph (vi) of the description of A in the definition underlying foreign tax in subsection (1), the income or profits, as determined under a domestic minimum top-up tax regime, for a fiscal year of a foreign affiliate of a corporation resident in Canada includes the income or profits, as determined under that tax regime, for the fiscal year of another entity that is not a foreign affiliate of the corporation to the extent that the income or profits, as determined under that tax regime, of the other entity are derived from an activity the income, profit or gains from which would be included in computing the affiliate√¢¬Ä¬ôs exempt earnings, hybrid surplus or taxable earnings, as the case may be.

For the purposes of this Part, the income or profits tax payable under a domestic minimum top-up tax regime for a fiscal year that can reasonably be considered to be in respect of the income or profits, as determined under that tax regime, of a foreign affiliate of a taxpayer that is a member of the DMTT group for the fiscal year is the amount determined under that tax regime to be the income or profits tax payable for the fiscal year in respect of those income or profits.

Despite any other provision of these Regulations, if a particular amount of income or profits tax payable under a domestic minimum top-up tax regime of a country other than Canada was determined taking into account any tax imposed under the Act (other than any tax imposed under Part XIII of the Act), no amount paid in respect of that particular amount is to be included in determining any amounts referred to in the definitions hybrid underlying tax and underlying foreign tax in subsection (1).

Subsection 5907(1.3) of the Regulations is amended by striking out √¢¬Ä¬úand√¢¬Ä¬ù at the end of paragraph (a), by adding √¢¬Ä¬úand√¢¬Ä¬ù at the end of paragraph (b) and by adding the following after paragraph (b):

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if paragraph (1.14)(b) applies (or would apply, if the taxpayer were a corporation resident in Canada) in respect of an amount paid by the particular affiliate, or a shareholder affiliate of the particular affiliate, to a primary affiliate (within the meaning assigned by subsection (1.14)), then the amount paid by the particular affiliate, or the shareholder affiliate, to the primary affiliate is prescribed to be foreign accrual tax applicable to a particular amount that is included under subsection 91(1) of the Act in computing the taxpayer√¢¬Ä¬ôs income for a taxation year of the taxpayer to the extent that the amount can reasonably be considered to be in respect of income or profits, as determined under the domestic minimum top-up tax regime referred to in subsection (1.14), of the particular affiliate or the shareholder affiliate, as the case may be, that are derived from an activity the income, profit or gains from which are included in the particular affiliate√¢¬Ä¬ôs foreign accrual property income that is included in the particular amount.

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) to (17) are deemed to have come into force on December 31, 2023.

Section.Text: Section 6400 of the Regulations and the heading √¢¬Ä¬úChild Tax Credits√¢¬Ä¬ù before it are repealed.
The portion of section 6701 of the Regulations before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

For the purposes of paragraph 6702(b) of these Regulations and paragraph 40(2)(i), clause 53(2)(k)(i)(C), the definition public corporation in subsection 89(1), the definition specified investment business in subsection 125(7), the definition approved share in subsection 127.4(1), subsections 131(8) and (11), section 186.1, the definition financial intermediary corporation in subsection 191(1), the definition eligible investment in subsection 204.8(1), and subsections 204.81(8.3) and 211.8(1) of the Act, prescribed labour-sponsored venture capital corporation means, at any particular time,
Paragraph 6702(b) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a tax credit provided in respect of, or for the acquisition of, a share of a prescribed labour-sponsored venture capital corporation; and

Section.Text: Section 6803 of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

For the purposes of the definition foreign retirement arrangement in subsection 248(1) of the Act, a prescribed plan or arrangement is a plan or arrangement to which subsection 401(k) or 408(a), (b) or (h) of the United States√¢¬Ä¬ô Internal Revenue Code of 1986, as amended from time to time, applies.

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 8303(6)(a)(i) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: in accordance with any of subsections 146(16), 146.3(14) and (14.1), 147(19) and 147.3(2), (5) and (7) of the Act, or

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2025.
Subsection 8304(11) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Subsection (10) does not apply to a past service event in relation to an individual pension plan if the provisional PSPA of the member determined under subsections 8303(3) and 8304(5) would be nil if no qualifying transfers were made in connection with the past service event.
Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2024.
Paragraph 8308(5.2)(c) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the retroactive contribution replaces, in whole or in part, a contribution that would have been required to be made to the plan in the calendar year 2020 or 2021 but for an amendment made to the plan under section 8511 that reduced the contributions required to be made.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on August 12, 2024.

Section.Text: Subparagraph 8502(b)(iv) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: is transferred to the plan in accordance with any of subsections 146(16), 146.3(14) and (14.1), 147(19), 147.3(1) to (8) and 147.5(21) of the Act,

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 8503(2)(e)(i) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: no other benefits (other than benefits permissible under paragraph (g), (i), (j), (l.1) or (n)) are payable as a consequence of the member√¢¬Ä¬ôs death,

Subsection.Text: Subparagraphs 8503(2)(i)(i) and (ii) of the Regulations are replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: no retirement benefits are payable as a consequence of the member√¢¬Ä¬ôs death, other than survivor retirement benefits described in subparagraph (e)(iv) payable to an individual that does not receive an amount under this paragraph, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the aggregate of all amounts, each of which is such a single amount (other than its portion, if any, that can reasonably be considered to be interest, computed at a rate not exceeding a reasonable rate, in respect of the period from the day of death of the member to the day the single amount is paid), does not exceed the present value, immediately before the death of the member or at any other time allowed under the Pension Benefits Standards Act, 1985 or similar law of a province, of all benefits that have accrued under the provision with respect to the member to the day of the member√¢¬Ä¬ôs death less the present value of any survivor retirement benefits described in subparagraph (e)(iv);

Subsection.Text: The portion of subparagraph 8503(4)(a)(i) of the Regulations before clause (A) is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of a plan that is subject to a designated provision of the law of Canada or a province, the aggregate amount of current service contributions to be made by a member in respect of a calendar year, no part of which is a period of disability or an eligible period of reduced pay or temporary absence of the member, does not exceed the lesser of

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 8503(4)(a) of the Regulations is amended by adding the following after subparagraph (i):

Subparagraph.Label: (i.1)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of a plan that is not subject to a designated provision of the law of Canada or a province, the aggregate amount of current service contributions to be made by a member in respect of a calendar year, no part of which is a period of disability or an eligible period of reduced pay or temporary absence of the member, does not exceed the money purchase limit for the year,

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 8503(4)(f) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: if lifetime retirement benefits are provided under the provision to a member in respect of a period of disability of the member, the benefits, to the extent that they would not be in accordance with paragraph (3)(a) if that paragraph were read without reference to its subparagraph (iv), are not paid before the plan administrator has received from a medical doctor, a nurse practitioner or a psychologist, who is licensed to practise under the laws of a province or of the place where the member resides a written report providing the information on the medical condition of the member taken into account by the administrator in determining that the period is a period of disability.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 8503(5) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

The Minister may waive the condition in subparagraph (4)(a)(i) if member contributions under a defined benefit provision of a pension plan are determined in a manner acceptable to the Minister and it is reasonable to expect that, on a long-term basis, the aggregate of the regular current service contributions made under the provision by all members will not exceed 1/2 of the amount that is required to fund the aggregate benefits in respect of which those contributions are made.
Subsections (1) to (6) are deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2025.
Clause 8506(1)(e.2)(iii)(A) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: a benefit described in any of paragraphs (b) to (e), (i) and (j),

Subsection.Text: Subsection 8506(1) of the Regulations is amended by striking out √¢¬Ä¬úand√¢¬Ä¬ù at the end of paragraph (h), by adding √¢¬Ä¬úand√¢¬Ä¬ù at the end of paragraph (i) and by adding the following after paragraph (i):

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: the payment with respect to one or more beneficiaries of one or more single amounts after the death of the last annuitant if

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the payments are the last payments to be made under the annuity contract, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the aggregate amount to be paid does not exceed the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total of all amounts transferred to a licensed annuities provider to acquire the annuity,
is the amount of interest, if any, calculated in respect of the amount determined for A at a rate that

FormulaParagraph.Label: (A)
FormulaParagraph.Text: is required by the Pension Benefits Standards Act, 1985 or a similar law of a province, or

FormulaParagraph.Label: (B)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if clause (A) does not apply, does not exceed a reasonable rate, and

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total amount of all annuity payments made under the annuity contract.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 8506(13)(a) of the Regulations is amended by striking out √¢¬Ä¬úor√¢¬Ä¬ù at the end of subparagraph (i), by adding √¢¬Ä¬úor√¢¬Ä¬ù at the end of subparagraph (ii) and by adding the following after subparagraph (ii):

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: transferred to the arrangement from a VPLA fund under another registered pension plan (referred to in this subparagraph as a √¢¬Ä¬útransferor fund√¢¬Ä¬ù), at the discretion of the administrator of the transferor fund, to replace VPLA benefits of one or more of the participants of the transferor fund by benefits provided under the arrangement;

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (2) are deemed to have come into force on August 12, 2024.
Subsection (3) is deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2024.
Section 8512 of the Regulations and the heading before it are repealed.
Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on August 12, 2024.
Section 8513 of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: For the purposes of paragraph 8302(3)(m), subparagraph 8502(c)(iii), subsection 8503(4) and paragraph 8517(5)(f), designated provision of the law of Canada or a province means section 21 of the Pension Benefits Standards Act, 1985 and any provision of a law of a province that is similar to that section.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on August 12, 2024.

Subsection 8514(2) of the Regulations is amended by striking out √¢¬Ä¬úor√¢¬Ä¬ù at the end of paragraph (e), by adding √¢¬Ä¬úor√¢¬Ä¬ù at the end of paragraph (f) and by adding the following after paragraph (f):

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: except in the case of an individual pension plan, a share of the capital stock of, an interest in, or a debt (in this paragraph referred to as the √¢¬Ä¬úinvestment√¢¬Ä¬ù) of a person or partnership that does not deal at arm√¢¬Ä¬ôs length with a participating employer if

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the investment is a prohibited investment solely because a person described in any of paragraphs (a) to (d) of the definition restricted financial institution in subsection 248(1) of the Act, or a person affiliated with that person, participates in the registered pension plan, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: no person receives a benefit that it is reasonable to consider, having regard to all the circumstances, is attributable, directly or indirectly, to the registered pension plan holding the investment, if holding the investment

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: would not have occurred in a normal commercial or investment context in which parties deal with each other at arm√¢¬Ä¬ôs length and act prudently, knowledgeably and willingly, and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: had as one of its main purposes to enable a person or partnership to benefit from the exemption from tax under Part I of the Act of any amount in respect of the registered pension plan.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on August 15, 2025.
Subsection 8516(1) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:
For the purposes of subsection 147.2(2) of the Act, a contribution described in subsection (2), (3) or (4) is a prescribed contribution.
Section 8516 of the Regulations is amended by adding the following after subsection (3):
A contribution that is made by an employer to a registered pension plan is described in this subsection if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the plan is not an individual pension plan; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the contribution

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: complies with the conditions in subparagraphs 8516(3)(a)(ii) and (iii),

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: is made in connection with a settlement of a member√¢¬Ä¬ôs entitlement, or a beneficiary√¢¬Ä¬ôs entitlement, to benefits at a time when the transfer ratio of the provision is less than 1 (determined in accordance with the pension standards legislation that applies to the plan), and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: does not exceed the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the present value of the benefits that are transferred or paid on behalf of the member, and
is the transfer ratio of the defined benefit provision as determined in the last actuarial valuation report that was prepared before the transfer date.

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (2) are deemed to have come into force on August 15, 2025.
Paragraph 9005(a) of the Regulations is repealed.
Subsection (1) applies to the 2027 and subsequent calendar years.

Section 9006 of the Regulations is amended by striking out √¢¬Ä¬úand√¢¬Ä¬ù at the end of paragraph (k), by adding √¢¬Ä¬úand√¢¬Ä¬ù at the end of paragraph (l) and by adding the following after paragraph (l):

Paragraph.Label: (m)
Paragraph.Text: an account

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: that is an equity or debt interest in a prescribed labour-sponsored venture capital corporation, as defined in section 6701, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: unless that account is held in an account described in any of paragraphs (a) to (l), to which the total value of contributions in any calendar year does not exceed 50,000 USD.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to the 2027 and subsequent calendar years.
Subparagraph (a)(iii) of Class 57 in Schedule II to the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: generates or distributes electrical energy, heat energy or a combination of electrical and heat energy, that directly and solely supports a qualified CCUS project, excluding equipment that

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: supports the qualified CCUS project indirectly by way of an electrical utility grid,

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: expands the capacity of existing equipment that supports the qualified CCUS project and that distributes electrical energy, heat energy or a combination of electrical and heat energy, or
uses fossil fuels and emits carbon dioxide that is not subject to capture by a qualified CCUS project, unless the fossil fuels are used for one or a combination of the following activities:

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: starting up the equipment for no more than 120 hours per startup, and

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: fuelling the equipment for any purpose for no more than 72 hours per calendar year,

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2022.
Subsections (2) to (5) apply if Bill C-30, introduced in the 1st session of the 45th Parliament and entitled the Spring Economic Update 2026 Implementation Act, receives royal assent.

The portion of paragraph (a) of the description of A.1 in subsection 1100(2) of the Income Tax Regulations before subparagraph (i), as enacted by subsection 114(3) of this Act, is replaced by the following:

FormulaParagraph.Label: (a)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the property is not included in subparagraph (1)(a.1)(i), paragraph (1)(v) or (ze) or in any of Classes 12, 13, 14, 15, 43.1, 44, 46, 50, 53, 54, 55, 56 and 59 or in Class 43 in the circumstances described in paragraph (f),

Subsection.Text: Subsection 1100(3) of the Income Tax Regulations, as enacted by 114(4) of this Act, is replaced by the following:

Where a taxation year is less than 12 months, the amount allowed as a deduction under this section, other than under subsection (0.1) and any of subparagraph (1)(a.1)(i) and paragraphs (1)(c), (e), (f), (g), (m), (w), (x), (y), (ya) and (ze), shall not exceed that proportion of the maximum amount otherwise allowable that the number of days in the taxation year is of 365.
The portion of subsection 1102(20.1) of the Income Tax Regulations before paragraph (b), as enacted by subsection 116(1) of this Act, is replaced by the following:

For the purposes of subsections 1100(0.3), (2.02) and (2.021) and 1104(2), (3.1), (4) and (4.01), a particular person or partnership and another person or partnership shall be considered not to be dealing at arm√¢¬Ä¬ôs length with each other in respect of the acquisition or ownership of a property if, in the absence of this subsection, they would be considered to be dealing at arm√¢¬Ä¬ôs length with each other and it may reasonably be considered that the principal purpose of any transaction or event, or a series of transactions or events, is to cause

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the property to qualify as accelerated investment incentive property, reaccelerated investment incentive property, immediate expensing property, an eligible manufacturing building or an eligible greenhouse; or

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph (a) of the description of A.1 in subsection 1100(2) of the Income Tax Regulations before subparagraph (i), as enacted by subsection (2), is deemed to have come into force on November 4, 2025.
Subsection 1100(3) of the Income Tax Regulations, as enacted by subsection (3), is deemed to have come into force on November 4, 2025.
The portion of subsection 1102(20.1) of the Income Tax Regulations before paragraph (b), as enacted by subsection (4), is deemed to have come into force on November 4, 2025.

Heading.Label: PART 2
Heading.TitleText: Amendments to the Global Minimum Tax Act and Related Legislation

Subsection.Text: The definition reverse hybrid entity in subsection 2(1) of the Global Minimum Tax Act is repealed.
The definitions substitute loss carry-forward recapture amount and unclaimed accrual in subsection 2(1) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: substitute loss carry-forward recapture amount, of a constituent entity that is located in a particular jurisdiction, means an amount if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the amount is offset, in determining the constituent entity√¢¬Ä¬ôs taxable income for a taxation year, against another amount (referred to in this definition as the √¢¬Ä¬úincome amount√¢¬Ä¬ù) in respect of the income of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: another entity (referred to in this definition as the √¢¬Ä¬úforeign subsidiary√¢¬Ä¬ù) that is located in a jurisdiction other than the particular jurisdiction and in which the constituent entity holds, directly or indirectly, an ownership interest, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a permanent establishment in respect of which the constituent entity is the main entity;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the amount would be a domestic source tax loss for the taxation year of the constituent entity if it were not offset against the income amount or is a domestic source tax loss carry-forward claimed in the taxation year by the constituent entity (referred to in this definition as the √¢¬Ä¬úconsumed domestic loss√¢¬Ä¬ù); and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the income tax laws of the particular jurisdiction allow the consumed domestic loss to be recaptured by the constituent entity in subsequent taxation years, by recharacterizing amounts of domestic source income of the constituent entity as amounts in respect of income of the foreign subsidiary or permanent establishment, or as amounts of income from another foreign source, that are included in computing the constituent entity√¢¬Ä¬ôs taxable income in the particular jurisdiction.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(montant de r√É¬©cup√É¬©ration du report de pertes de remplacement)

Definition.Text: unclaimed accrual, of a constituent entity of an MNE group for a fiscal year, means an increase in a deferred tax liability, or aggregation of deferred tax liabilities, recorded in the financial accounts of the constituent entity for the fiscal year if

Subparagraph.Text: the increase is not expected to reverse, or to be considered to have reversed, on or before the last day of the fifth fiscal year following the fiscal year, and
the filing constituent entity of the MNE group elects to not include the increase in determining the constituent entity√¢¬Ä¬ôs total deferred tax adjustment amount for the fiscal year; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an election under subsection 25(7) applies to the increase.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(accumulation non r√É¬©clam√É¬©e)

Subsection.Text: The definitions RDIR and RPII in subsection 2(1) of the French version of the Act are replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: RDIR√¢¬Ä¬ÇS√¢¬Ä¬ôentend d√¢¬Ä¬ôune loi d√¢¬Ä¬ôune juridiction qui peut raisonnablement √É¬™tre consid√É¬©r√É¬©e comme ayant √É¬©t√É¬© √É¬©dict√É¬©e dans l√¢¬Ä¬ôintention de mettre en √Ö¬ìuvre, en tout ou en partie, les articles 2.1 √É¬Ý 2.3 des r√É¬®gles GloBE.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(IIR)

RPII√¢¬Ä¬ÇS√¢¬Ä¬ôentend d√¢¬Ä¬ôune loi d√¢¬Ä¬ôune juridiction qui peut raisonnablement √É¬™tre consid√É¬©r√É¬©e comme ayant √É¬©t√É¬© √É¬©dict√É¬©e dans l√¢¬Ä¬ôintention de mettre en √Ö¬ìuvre, en tout ou en partie, les articles 2.4 √É¬Ý 2.6 des r√É¬®gles GloBE.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(UTPR)

Subsection.Text: Subparagraphs (a)(ii) and (iii) of the definition filing constituent entity in subsection 2(1) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the designated local entity (as appointed under paragraph 60(3)(a)) for the fiscal year,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the qualifying foreign filing entity, as defined in subsection 55(1), for the fiscal year, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: the ultimate parent entity or the designated filing entity, as defined in subsection 55(1), in respect of the MNE group for the fiscal year that is located in a jurisdiction other than Canada and that files a complete or substantially complete GIR for the fiscal year, in the prescribed form and manner with the Minister, on or before the GIR due date, as defined in subsection 55(1); and

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (a) of the definition recapture exception accrual in subsection 2(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: cost recovery allowance in respect of a tangible asset;

Subsection.Text: The portion of the definition substitute loss carry-forward tax credit in subsection 2(1) of the Act before paragraph (c) is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: substitute loss carry-forward tax credit, of a constituent entity that is located in a particular jurisdiction, means all or a portion of a tax credit of the constituent entity that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: arises under the income tax laws of the particular jurisdiction in respect of tax paid to the government of a jurisdiction other than the particular jurisdiction by

Subparagraph.Text: another entity (referred to in this definition as the √¢¬Ä¬úforeign subsidiary√¢¬Ä¬ù) that is located in a jurisdiction other than the particular jurisdiction, in which the constituent entity holds, directly or indirectly, an ownership interest, on income (referred to in this definition as the √¢¬Ä¬úforeign subsidiary income√¢¬Ä¬ù) of the foreign subsidiary, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the constituent entity, on income (referred to in this definition as the √¢¬Ä¬úPE income√¢¬Ä¬ù) of a permanent establishment in respect of which the constituent entity is the main entity;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is not used in the particular taxation year in which it arises because the income tax laws of the particular jurisdiction require, in determining the constituent entity√¢¬Ä¬ôs taxable income, that an amount claimed in the particular taxation year in respect of a domestic source tax loss carry-forward, or an amount that would be a domestic source tax loss of the constituent entity in the absence of the foreign subsidiary income or PE income in the particular taxation year, be offset against the foreign subsidiary income or PE income in priority to the use of the tax credit, or portion of the tax credit, to reduce or eliminate any covered taxes for which the constituent entity would otherwise be liable in respect of that income under the income tax laws of the particular jurisdiction; and

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph (d) of the definition √É¬©tats financiers consolid√É¬©s in subsection 2(1) of the French version of the Act before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: d)
Paragraph.Text: si l√¢¬Ä¬ôentit√É¬© n√¢¬Ä¬ô√É¬©tablit pas d√¢¬Ä¬ô√É¬©tats financiers vis√É¬©s aux alin√É¬©as a) √É¬Ý c), des √É¬©tats financiers √¢¬Ä¬î dans lesquels les actifs, les passifs, les produits, les charges et les flux de tr√É¬©sorerie de cette entit√É¬© et d√¢¬Ä¬ôautres entit√É¬©s sont pr√É¬©sent√É¬©s comme les √É¬©l√É¬©ments d√¢¬Ä¬ôune seule entit√É¬© √É¬©conomique √¢¬Ä¬î qui auraient √É¬©t√É¬© √É¬©tablis en application d√¢¬Ä¬ôune norme de comptabilit√É¬© financi√É¬®re agr√É¬©√É¬©e qui, selon le cas√¢¬Ä¬Ø:

Subsection.Text: Subsection 2(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: private investment entity, for a fiscal year, means an entity

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that files a prescribed form with the Minister in respect of the fiscal year or any prior fiscal year on or before the GIR due date (as defined in subsection 55(1)) for the fiscal year or prior fiscal year, as the case may be;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: that is located in Canada;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: that does not have any of its ownership interests quoted on a securities market;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the controlling interest in which is not held, directly or indirectly, by an entity that has any of its ownership interests quoted on a securities market;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: that holds, directly or indirectly, a controlling interest in at least one corporation (referred to in this definition as a √¢¬Ä¬úsubsidiary public company√¢¬Ä¬ù) that is located in Canada and that has a class of shares of its capital stock listed on a designated stock exchange (as defined in subsection 248(1) of the Income Tax Act);

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: whose assets, liabilities, income, expenses and cash flows are included in an entity√¢¬Ä¬ôs consolidated financial statements that are described in paragraph (d) of the definition consolidated financial statements and that include the assets, liabilities, income, expenses and cash flows of a subsidiary public company; and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: that prepares √¢¬Ä¬î or the controlling interest in which is held, directly or indirectly, by an entity (referred to in this paragraph as the √¢¬Ä¬úcontrolling entity√¢¬Ä¬ù) that prepares √¢¬Ä¬î actual financial statements in accordance with the Accounting Standards for Private Enterprises established by the Accounting Standards Board (Canada) and that chooses √¢¬Ä¬î or the controlling entity in respect of which chooses √¢¬Ä¬î under paragraph 1591.24(b) of those standards, to not consolidate its subsidiaries.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(entit√É¬© d√¢¬Ä¬ôinvestissement priv√É¬©e)

Definition.Text: securitization entity√¢¬Ä¬Çmeans an entity that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is a participant in one or more arrangements, each of which

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: is implemented for the purpose of pooling and repackaging a portfolio of assets (or exposures to assets) for investors that are not constituent entities of the same MNE group as the entity in a manner that legally segregates one or more identified pools of assets, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: seeks through contractual agreements to limit the exposure of those investors to the risk of insolvency of an entity that holds the legally segregated assets by controlling the ability of identified creditors of that entity (or of another entity that is a participant in the arrangement) to make claims against an entity that is a participant in the arrangement through legally binding documentation entered into by those creditors; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: meets the following conditions:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the entity only carries out activities that facilitate one or more arrangements described in paragraph (a),

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the entity grants security over its assets in favour of its creditors (or the creditors of another securitization entity),

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the entity pays out all cash received from its assets to its creditors (or the creditors of another securitization entity) on an annual or more frequent basis, other than

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: cash retained to meet an amount of profit required by the documentation of the arrangement, for eventual distribution to equity holders (or equivalent), or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: cash reasonably required under the terms of the arrangement to

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: make provision for future payments which are required, or will likely be required, to be made by the entity under the terms of the arrangement, or

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: maintain or enhance the creditworthiness of the entity, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount of profit referred to in clause (iii)(A) for a fiscal year is negligible relative to the revenues of the entity for the fiscal year.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(entit√É¬© de titrisation)

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) to (8) apply to fiscal years of a qualifying MNE group that begin on or after December 31, 2023.
Subsection 3(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

This Part, Part 2, Part 2.1 and relevant provisions of Part 5 implement the GloBE Model Rules, the GloBE Commentary and the administrative guidance in respect of the GloBE Model Rules approved by the Inclusive Framework and published by the OECD from time to time and, unless the context otherwise requires, these Parts and provisions are to be interpreted consistently with those sources, as amended from time to time.
Subsection (1) applies to fiscal years of a qualifying MNE group that begin on or after December 31, 2025.
Paragraph 5(1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in any other case, in the jurisdiction where it was created or last continued.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to fiscal years of a qualifying MNE group that begin on or after December 31, 2023.
Subsection 7(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subject to subsection (2), if a particular amount is relevant to the determination of the top-up amount, or of any other amount or result relevant to the determination of the top-up amount, of a constituent entity of an MNE group for a fiscal year,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the currency in which the particular amount is to be denominated for use in that determination is the reporting currency of the consolidated financial statements of the ultimate parent entity of the MNE group for the fiscal year; and

in cases where the particular amount is denominated in a currency other than the reporting currency and is not converted to the reporting currency in the course of preparing the consolidated financial statements, it is to be converted, for use in that determination, to the reporting currency using the foreign currency translation principles of the financial accounting standard that would have been used to convert the amount to the reporting currency if that conversion were undertaken in the course of preparing the consolidated financial statements.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 7(3)(a) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: a)
Paragraph.Text: la monnaie dans laquelle cette somme doit √É¬™tre libell√É¬©e aux fins d√¢¬Ä¬ôutilisation dans le calcul est la monnaie de pr√É¬©sentation des √É¬©tats financiers consolid√É¬©s de l√¢¬Ä¬ôentit√É¬© m√É¬®re ultime du groupe d√¢¬Ä¬ôEMN;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 7(3)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in cases where that amount would not, in the absence of this paragraph, be denominated in the reporting currency, it is to be converted, for use in the determination or computation, to the reporting currency using the average for the fiscal year of the daily rates of exchange quoted by the Bank of Canada or, if there is no daily rate quoted by the Bank of Canada for a particular day, a daily rate of exchange acceptable to the Minister, in respect of the two currencies.

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) to (3) apply to fiscal years of a qualifying MNE group that begin on or after December 31, 2023.
Section 9 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

If a private investment entity for a fiscal year would, in the absence of this subsection, be a constituent entity of a qualifying MNE group (referred to in this subsection and subsection (2.2) as the √¢¬Ä¬úactual group√¢¬Ä¬ù) for the year, the following rules apply:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subject to paragraph (b), the private investment entity is deemed to not have, directly or indirectly, any controlling interest for the fiscal year in any entity that

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: would, in the absence of this paragraph, be a constituent entity of the actual group,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: is a corporation located in Canada, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: has a class of shares of its capital stock listed on a designated stock exchange (as defined in subsection 248(1) of the Income Tax Act);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph (a) does not apply for the purposes of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the definition private investment entity in subsection 2(1),

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: subsections 18(13), (14) and (18) in calculating the GloBE income or loss, and subsections 48(4) to (9) in calculating the adjusted covered taxes, of any entity (referred to in this paragraph as a √¢¬Ä¬úsubject entity√¢¬Ä¬ù) that would, in the absence of paragraph (a), be a constituent entity of the actual group, except in determining

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the effective tax rate referred to in clause 18(14)(b)(ii)(B), and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: whether a subject entity is a low-tax entity or a high-tax counterparty referred to in subsection 18(18),

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: paragraph 32(15)(d) in calculating the carrying value of an eligible tangible asset of a subject entity,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: determining if the condition in paragraph 33(1)(b) or (c) is met,

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: the definitions deduction/non-inclusion arrangement, duplicate loss arrangement and duplicate tax recognition arrangement in subsection 47(1) in calculating the qualifying income tax expense of a subject entity and the profit (loss) before income tax for the jurisdiction in which a subject entity is located, and

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: the de minimis threshold test in subsection 47(3);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if, because of paragraph (a), two or more entities (or a main entity and one or more of its permanent establishments) that would be constituent entities of the actual group form a different group, or an entity that would be a constituent entity of the actual group is not included in any group, that group or entity, as the case may be, is deemed to be a qualifying MNE group for the fiscal year; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: if, because of paragraph (c), a single entity (other than a main entity in respect of a permanent establishment) is deemed to be a qualifying MNE group,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: that entity is deemed to be the ultimate parent entity of the group for the fiscal year, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the consolidated financial statements of that ultimate parent entity for the fiscal year are the financial statements of that entity that are or would be prepared in accordance with an authorized financial accounting standard that is

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: an acceptable financial accounting standard, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: a financial accounting standard that is adjusted to prevent any material competitive distortions.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (2.1) does not apply in respect of an actual group if one of the main purposes of any transaction or event is to render that subsection applicable to the actual group.
Subsection (1) applies to fiscal years of a qualifying MNE group that begin on or after December 31, 2023.
The portion of subsection 15(1) of the Act before the formula is replaced by the following:
The amount of the tax a person must pay under this Part in respect of an MNE group for a fiscal year is equal to the total of all amounts, each of which is an amount determined by the formula
Paragraph (b) of the description of B in subsection 15(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

FormulaParagraph.Label: (b)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the allocable share, of the top-up amount of the constituent entity for the fiscal year, of a relevant parent entity of the MNE group √¢¬Ä¬î that is located in a jurisdiction where the constituent entity is subject to a qualified IIR √¢¬Ä¬î through which the person referred to in paragraph (a), or the relevant parent entity referred to in paragraph (b), of the description of A indirectly holds an ownership interest in the constituent entity.

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (2) apply to fiscal years of a qualifying MNE group that begin on or after December 31, 2023.
Subsection 17(6) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If a constituent entity is a flow-through entity, the following rules apply in determining the financial accounting income for a fiscal year of the flow-through entity and any other group entities in respect of the net income or loss of the flow-through entity:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: amounts in respect of the flow-through entity√¢¬Ä¬ôs net income or loss that are attributable to ownership interests of persons or entities that are not group entities and that hold their ownership interests in the flow-through entity directly, or through one or more other flow-through entities that are group entities in respect of the flow-through entity, must not be included in computing the financial accounting income of any group entity, unless

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the flow-through entity is an ultimate parent entity, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the flow-through entity is owned, directly or through one or more flow-through entities that are group entities in respect of the flow-through entity, by an ultimate parent entity that is also a flow-through entity, in which case this paragraph does not apply to amounts in respect of the flow-through entity√¢¬Ä¬ôs net income or loss to the extent those amounts are attributable to persons or entities that hold their ownership interests in the flow-through entity through that ultimate parent entity;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if a particular group entity has an ownership interest in the flow-through entity, an amount that, in the absence of this paragraph √¢¬Ä¬î and, for greater certainty, after excluding amounts to which paragraph (a) applies and amounts allocated to a permanent establishment in accordance with paragraph (1)(b) √¢¬Ä¬î would be included in the financial accounting income of the flow-through entity is excluded from its financial accounting income and included in the financial accounting income of the particular group entity in accordance with the particular group entity√¢¬Ä¬ôs ownership interest (determined having regard only to ownership interests held by group entities) in the flow-through entity, to the extent that

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the flow-through entity is not an ultimate parent entity,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the flow-through entity is fiscally transparent in relation to the particular group entity,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the particular group entity is not a flow-through entity, other than an ultimate parent entity, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: the particular group entity holds its ownership interest in the flow-through entity

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: directly, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: indirectly, through a tax transparent structure; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any amount of the net income or loss of the flow-through entity that is not excluded in computing its financial accounting income because of paragraph (a) or (b), or subsection (3), is included in the financial accounting income of the flow-through entity.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to fiscal years of a qualifying MNE group that begin on or after December 31, 2023.
Section 18 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (26):

If the conditions in subsection 25(9) are met in respect of a particular impairment loss or reversal of an impairment loss in respect of an asset of a constituent entity, the constituent entity√¢¬Ä¬ôs GloBE income or loss is to be determined

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: using the adjusted GloBE carrying value (within the meaning assigned by subsection 25(8)) of the asset after taking into account

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
