Subsection.Text: Subsection 48(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:
In applying subsection (1),

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a deferred tax asset that is reflected in a constituent entity√¢¬Ä¬ôs financial accounts at the beginning of the GloBE transition year of its MNE group in respect of the jurisdiction in which the constituent entity is located must not be taken into account in determining the constituent entity√¢¬Ä¬ôs total deferred tax adjustment amount if the deferred tax asset arises

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: as a result of a transaction (which includes any agreement, ruling, decree, grant or similar arrangement with a general government or any change to such an arrangement) that occurs on or after December 1, 2021, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in respect of

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: an amount that would not be taken into account in computing the constituent entity√¢¬Ä¬ôs GloBE income or loss for the GloBE transition year if the amount arose in the GloBE transition year, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: an amount that

Subclause.Text: is not included in the constituent entity√¢¬Ä¬ôs income or profits in respect of which an entity is subject to an income or profits tax imposed by the government of the jurisdiction, and
would be included in computing the constituent entity√¢¬Ä¬ôs GloBE income or loss for the GloBE transition year if the amount arose in the GloBE transition year;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for greater certainty, a deferred tax asset must not be taken into account in determining a constituent entity√¢¬Ä¬ôs total deferred tax adjustment amount to the extent that the deferred tax asset is attributable to

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a tax credit or other tax relief a specific entitlement to which arises under an agreement, ruling, decree, grant or similar arrangement with a general government if that arrangement is concluded, or any change to such an arrangement is made, on or after December 1, 2021, unless no critical aspect of the tax credit or other tax relief relies on the exercise of discretion by the general government,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: an election or choice that is made or changed on or after December 1, 2021 and that results in a change to the treatment of a transaction in determining the constituent entity√¢¬Ä¬ôs taxable income for a taxation year in respect of which the tax authority has made an assessment, or a tax return has been filed, before the election or choice is made or changed, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: differences between the accounting carrying values and tax bases of assets or liabilities if

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: those tax bases were established under a corporate income tax regime of any jurisdiction that was enacted on or after December 1, 2021, and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the jurisdiction did not have a corporate income tax regime immediately prior to the enactment of the regime referred to in clause (A); and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph (a) is deemed to apply to any deferred tax asset to the extent that the deferred tax asset is attributable to a loss √¢¬Ä¬î under a corporate income tax regime of a jurisdiction enacted on or after December 1, 2021 where the jurisdiction did not have a corporate income tax regime immediately prior to the enactment of the regime √¢¬Ä¬î for a fiscal year of the constituent entity preceding the five fiscal years that immediately precede the fiscal year that includes the date of such enactment.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Definition of pre-GloBE arrangement deferred tax asset
Subsection.Text: In this section, pre-GloBE arrangement deferred tax asset, of a constituent entity of an MNE group located in a jurisdiction, means a deferred tax asset that is reflected in the constituent entity√¢¬Ä¬ôs financial accounts to the extent that the deferred tax asset is attributable to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a tax credit or other tax relief described in subparagraph (2)(b)(i), unless it arises under an arrangement with a general government that is concluded or amended after November 18, 2024;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an election or choice described in subparagraph (2)(b)(ii) that is not made or changed after November 18, 2024; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any differences described in subparagraph (2)(b)(iii), unless the corporate income tax regime described in clause (2)(b)(iii)(A) is enacted after the earlier of November 18, 2024 and the first day of the GloBE transition year of the MNE group in respect of the jurisdiction.

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (2.3), the following rules apply to the deferred tax expense, for a particular fiscal year, in respect of the reversal of a pre-GloBE arrangement deferred tax asset of a constituent entity of an MNE group located in a jurisdiction:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in applying subsection (1), despite subsection (2) but subject to paragraphs (b) and (c), the deferred tax expense may be taken into account in determining the constituent entity√¢¬Ä¬ôs total deferred tax adjustment amount for the particular fiscal year;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the amount of the deferred tax expense that may be taken into account in determining the constituent entity√¢¬Ä¬ôs total deferred tax adjustment amount or qualifying income tax expense (as defined in subsection 47(1)) for the particular fiscal year must not exceed the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount of the deferred tax asset reflected in the constituent entity√¢¬Ä¬ôs financial accounts at the later of

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the time when it is originally reflected in the accounts, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the beginning of the first fiscal year that the MNE group is a qualifying MNE group,

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the greater of

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the minimum rate, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the tax rate applicable in determining the amount referred to in the description of A,

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the minimum rate, and

is the total of all amounts each of which is an amount of deferred tax expense in respect of the deferred tax asset taken into account in determining the constituent entity√¢¬Ä¬ôs total deferred tax adjustment amount or qualifying income tax expense (as defined in subsection 47(1)) for a fiscal year preceding the particular fiscal year; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: no amount of the deferred tax expense may be taken into account in determining the constituent entity√¢¬Ä¬ôs total deferred tax adjustment amount or qualifying income tax expense (as defined in subsection 47(1)) if the particular fiscal year

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of a deferred tax asset that satisfies the condition in subparagraph (2.1)(a) or (b), begins after December 31, 2025 or ends after June 30, 2027, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of a deferred tax asset that satisfies the condition in subparagraph (2.1)(c), begins before December 31, 2024 or after December 30, 2026 or ends after June 30, 2028.

Subsection.Text: Any amount of deferred tax expense in respect of the reversal of a pre-GloBE arrangement deferred tax asset of a constituent entity that may, in the absence of this subsection, be taken into account in determining the constituent entity√¢¬Ä¬ôs total deferred tax adjustment amount or qualifying income tax expense (as defined in subsection 47(1)) for a fiscal year shall not be taken into account for those purposes to the extent that it arises from any of the following occurrences taking place after November 18, 2024:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a change in

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: law,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the accounting methodology used, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the terms of an agreement, ruling, decree, grant or similar arrangement with a general government; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the making or changing of an election or choice.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subparagraph 48(5)(b)(ii) of the Act before clause (B) is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the transferee is deemed to have a deferred tax asset reflected in its financial accounts in an amount equal to the total of the following amounts, adjusted for any capitalized expenditure, amortization and depreciation in respect of the asset that are referred to in subparagraphs (a)(i) and (ii):

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the portion of the current tax expense for a fiscal year of the transferor (or, if a group taxation regime applies, the portion of the current tax expense for the fiscal year of the entity that pays taxes in respect of the transferor under that regime) in respect of covered taxes that is demonstrated to have resulted from the transfer,

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 48(5)(b)(ii) of the Act is amended by striking out √¢¬Ä¬úand√¢¬Ä¬ù at the end of clause (A), by adding √¢¬Ä¬úand√¢¬Ä¬ù at the end of clause (B) and by adding the following after clause (B):

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: the amount of any deferred tax asset (other than a pre-GloBE arrangement deferred tax asset) of the transferor (or, if a group taxation regime applies, the entity that pays taxes in respect of the transferor under that regime) that

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: would have been taken into account under subsection (1), and

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: is demonstrated to have been reversed or not created solely because any gain arising on the transfer was included in the domestic taxable income of the transferor.

Subsection.Text: Section 48 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (5):
The creation of a deferred tax asset under subparagraph (5)(b)(ii) does not reduce the adjusted covered taxes of any constituent entity for any fiscal year.
The description of B in paragraph 48(9)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount of any deferred tax asset (other than a pre-GloBE arrangement deferred tax asset) in respect of a tax loss of the transferor that

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: would have been taken into account under subsection (1), and
is demonstrated to have been reversed or to not have been created solely because any gain arising from the transfer of the asset was included in the taxable income of the transferor,

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) to (6) apply to fiscal years of a qualifying MNE group that begin on or after December 31, 2023.
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 49:

Section.Text: The following definitions apply in this Part.

Definition.Text: Canadian share of the total UTPR top-up amount√¢¬Ä¬Çhas the same meaning as in section 49.4.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(part canadienne du montant compl√É¬©mentaire RPII total)
Canadian UTPR percentage√¢¬Ä¬Çhas the same meaning as in subsection 49.7(1).√¢¬Ä¬Ç(pourcentage RPII canadien)
Canadian UTPR top-up amount√¢¬Ä¬Çhas the same meaning as in section 49.3.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(montant compl√É¬©mentaire RPII canadien)
initial phase of international activity year, of an MNE group, means a fiscal year

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: throughout which the constituent entities (other than investment entities, insurance investment entities and flow-through entities that are stateless constituent entities) of the MNE group are located in no more than six different jurisdictions; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for which the total of all amounts, each of which is the net book value, for the fiscal year, of a tangible asset (other than any tangible asset, of a flow-through entity, that is physically located in the reference jurisdiction) of a constituent entity (other than an investment entity or insurance investment entity and including, for greater certainty, a flow-through entity) of the MNE group that is located in a jurisdiction other than the reference jurisdiction, does not exceed √¢¬Ç¬¨50 million.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(ann√É¬©e de la phase de d√É¬©marrage des activit√É¬©s internationales)

Definition.Text: net book value, for a fiscal year, of a tangible asset, means the average of the beginning and end values of the tangible asset for the fiscal year after taking into account accumulated depreciation, depletion and impairment, as recorded in the financial statements.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(valeur nette comptable)

number of employees, of a constituent entity of an MNE group for a fiscal year, means the total number of employees of the constituent entity, on a full-time equivalent basis for the fiscal year, determined in accordance with the following rules:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an independent contractor participating in the ordinary operating activities of the constituent entity is an employee;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an employee is an employee of a permanent establishment or main entity to the extent that the payroll costs of the employee are included in the separate financial accounts of the permanent establishment (as adjusted in accordance with clause 17(1)(b)(ii)(B) or subsection 17(2)) or main entity, as the case may be;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: to the extent that an employee is, under paragraph (b), an employee of a permanent establishment, the employee must not be taken into account in determining the number of employees of the main entity of the permanent establishment; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: if a constituent entity was not a member of the MNE group throughout the fiscal year, the number of full-time equivalent employees of the constituent entity for the fiscal year is to be determined on a just and reasonable basis.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(nombre d√¢¬Ä¬ôemploy√É¬©s)

Definition.Text: reference jurisdiction, of an MNE group, means a particular jurisdiction if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: at least one constituent entity of the MNE group is located in the particular jurisdiction in the first fiscal year that any constituent entity of, or joint venture entity in respect of, the MNE group is subject to a qualified UTPR or Part 3; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: there is no other jurisdiction for which the total of the net book values, for that fiscal year, of tangible assets held by constituent entities of the MNE group that are located in that other jurisdiction is greater than the total of the net book values, for that fiscal year, of tangible assets held by constituent entities of the MNE group that are located in the particular jurisdiction.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(juridiction de r√É¬©f√É¬©rence)

Definition.Text: tangible assets, of a constituent entity for a fiscal year, means the tangible assets of the constituent entity for the fiscal year determined in accordance with the following rules:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: for greater certainty, tangible assets do not include cash or cash equivalents, intangibles or financial assets;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the tangible assets of a constituent entity that is a permanent establishment are only those that are reflected in the separate financial accounts of the permanent establishment as adjusted in accordance with clause 17(1)(b)(ii)(B) or subsection 17(2) (referred to in this definition as √¢¬Ä¬úpermanent establishment assets√¢¬Ä¬ù); and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the tangible assets of a constituent entity that is a main entity in respect of one or more permanent establishments do not include any permanent establishment assets of those permanent establishments.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(actifs corporels)

Definition.Text: total UTPR top-up amount√¢¬Ä¬Çhas the same meaning as in section 49.5.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(montant compl√É¬©mentaire RPII total)
UTPR top-up amount√¢¬Ä¬Çhas the same meaning as in section 49.6.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(montant compl√É¬©mentaire RPII)

Subsection.Text: A person must pay a tax under this Part in respect of a constituent entity of an MNE group for a fiscal year in an amount equal to the Canadian UTPR top-up amount of the constituent entity for the fiscal year, if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the constituent entity is located in Canada for the fiscal year;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the MNE group is a qualifying MNE group for the fiscal year; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: either

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the person is the constituent entity, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: both of the following conditions are met:

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the constituent entity is not a person,

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the person would, under the relevant assumptions, include in its income for the purposes of Part I of the Income Tax Act income of the constituent entity for the fiscal year.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of clause (1)(c)(ii)(B), the relevant assumptions are that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the constituent entity referred to in that clause has income for the fiscal year that would be included in computing its income for the purposes of Part I of the Income Tax Act if it were a person resident in Canada; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the person referred to in that clause is resident in Canada for the purposes of the Income Tax Act.

Section.MarginalNote: Definition of Canadian UTPR top-up amount
Section.Text: Canadian UTPR top-up amount, for a fiscal year, of a constituent entity (other than a securitization entity) of an MNE group located in Canada means the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the Canadian share of the total UTPR top-up amount of the MNE group for the fiscal year;
is the percentage determined by the formula
is the number of employees of the constituent entity for the fiscal year, and
is the total of all amounts, each of which is the number of employees of a constituent entity (other than a securitization entity) of the MNE group located in Canada for the fiscal year; and
is the percentage determined by the formula
is the net book value of tangible assets of the constituent entity for the fiscal year, and

is the total of all amounts, each of which is the net book value of tangible assets of a constituent entity (other than a securitization entity) of the MNE group located in Canada for the fiscal year.

Section.MarginalNote: Definition of Canadian share of the total UTPR top-up amount
Section.Text: Canadian share of the total UTPR top-up amount, of an MNE group for a fiscal year, means the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total UTPR top-up amount of the MNE group for the fiscal year; and
is the Canadian UTPR percentage of the MNE group for the fiscal year.

Section.MarginalNote: Definition of total UTPR top-up amount
Section.Text: total UTPR top-up amount, of an MNE group for a fiscal year, means the total of all amounts, each of which is the UTPR top-up amount of a constituent entity of the MNE group for the fiscal year.
UTPR top-up amount, of a constituent entity of an MNE group for the fiscal year, means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: nil, if

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the constituent entity is the ultimate parent entity of the MNE group and it is subject to a qualified IIR in the jurisdiction where it is located, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: all of the ultimate parent entity√¢¬Ä¬ôs ownership interests in the constituent entity are held, directly or indirectly, by one or more relevant parent entities of the MNE group that are located in jurisdictions where the constituent entity is subject to a qualified IIR; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in any other case, the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the constituent entity is a joint venture entity in respect of the MNE group, the amount that would be the ultimate parent entity√¢¬Ä¬ôs allocable share of the top-up amount of the joint venture entity for the fiscal year if the ultimate parent entity were a relevant parent entity of the MNE group for the fiscal year, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: in any other case, the top-up amount of the constituent entity for the fiscal year, and

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total of all amounts, each of which is an amount determined by the formula

is the allocable share, of the top-up amount of the constituent entity for the fiscal year, of a relevant parent entity that is located in a jurisdiction where the constituent entity is subject to a qualified IIR, and

is the total of all amounts, each of which is a portion of the top-up amount of the constituent entity for the fiscal year that is included in both the amount determined for C in respect of the relevant parent entity for the fiscal year and the allocable share, of the top-up amount of the constituent entity for the fiscal year, of another relevant parent entity √¢¬Ä¬î that is located in a jurisdiction where the constituent entity is subject to a qualified IIR √¢¬Ä¬î through which the relevant parent entity indirectly holds an ownership interest in the constituent entity.

Subsection.Text: Canadian UTPR percentage of an MNE group for a fiscal year means the percentage determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the percentage determined by the formula
is the total of all amounts, each of which is the number of employees of a constituent entity, of the MNE group, located in Canada for the fiscal year, and

is the total of all amounts, each of which is the number of employees of a constituent entity, of the MNE group, that is located in a jurisdiction in which a qualified UTPR is in force for the fiscal year; and
is the percentage determined by the formula
is the total of all amounts, each of which is the net book value of tangible assets of a constituent entity, of the MNE group, located in Canada for the fiscal year, and

is the total of all amounts, each of which is the net book value of tangible assets of a constituent entity, of the MNE group, located in a jurisdiction in which a qualified UTPR is in force for the fiscal year.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of determining the Canadian UTPR percentage of an MNE group for a fiscal year, a jurisdiction is deemed not to have a qualified UTPR in force for the fiscal year if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: under a provision, of the qualified UTPR of that jurisdiction for a preceding fiscal year, that is substantially similar to the definition Canadian share of the total UTPR top-up amount, an amount in respect of the total UTPR top-up amount of the MNE group for the preceding fiscal year was allocated to the jurisdiction in respect of the MNE group;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the top-up allocation has not yet resulted in an additional cash tax expense, for the constituent entities of the MNE group located in that jurisdiction, equal to the allocated amount; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: this subsection would not, in the absence of this paragraph, apply in respect of every jurisdiction, in which one or more constituent entities of the MNE group are located, that would otherwise have a qualified UTPR in force for the fiscal year.

Section.MarginalNote: Employees and tangible assets √¢¬Ä¬î special rule
Section.Text: In determining the number of employees and the tangible assets of a constituent entity of an MNE group for a fiscal year for the purposes of this Part, other than for the purposes of paragraph (b) of the definition initial phase of international activity year,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an investment entity or insurance investment entity is deemed to have no employees and no tangible assets; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: employees or tangible assets that would, in the absence of this paragraph, be employees or tangible assets, as the case may be, of a flow-through entity that is a stateless entity (and that are not employees or tangible assets, as the case may be, of a permanent establishment of the flow-through entity)

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: are deemed to be employees or tangible assets, as the case may be, of any constituent entities of the MNE group that are located in the jurisdiction in which the flow-through entity was created, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if there are no constituent entities described in subparagraph (i), are deemed not to be employees or tangible assets, as the case may be.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this Part, other than the definitions initial phase of international activity year and Canadian UTPR top-up amount, any reference to a constituent entity of an MNE group includes a joint venture entity in respect of the MNE group.

Despite subsection (1), a reference to a constituent entity of an MNE group in the definition Canadian UTPR top-up amount for a fiscal year includes a joint venture entity in respect of the MNE group if, for the fiscal year,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: one or more joint venture entities in respect of the MNE group are located in Canada; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: no constituent entity of the MNE group other than a joint venture entity in respect of the MNE group is located in Canada.

Section.MarginalNote: Initial phase of international activity √¢¬Ä¬î exclusion

Subsection.Text: Despite any other provision of this Part, other than subsection (2), the total UTPR top-up amount of an MNE group for a fiscal year is deemed to be nil if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the fiscal year

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: begins on or before the day that is five years after the first day of the first fiscal year that a constituent entity of the MNE group is subject to a qualified UTPR, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: is an initial phase of international activity year; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: all fiscal years preceding the fiscal year are initial phase of international activity years.

Subsection.Text: If Canada is the reference jurisdiction of an MNE group, for a fiscal year for which the total UTPR top-up amount of the MNE group would, in the absence of this subsection, be deemed to be nil under subsection (1),

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subsection (1) does not apply for the purpose of determining the MNE group√¢¬Ä¬ôs total UTPR top-up amount for the fiscal year;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for the purpose of determining the total UTPR top-up amount of the MNE group for the fiscal year, the UTPR top-up amount of a constituent entity of the MNE group that is located in Canada for the fiscal year is deemed to be nil; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: despite any other provision of this Part, the Canadian UTPR percentage of the MNE group for the fiscal year is deemed to be 100%.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to fiscal years of a qualifying MNE group that begin on or after December 31, 2025.
The portion of subsection 51(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

A particular person must pay a tax under this Part in respect of a constituent entity (other than a securitization entity) of an MNE group for a fiscal year in an amount equal to the domestic top-up amount of the constituent entity for the fiscal year, if
Subsection (1) applies to fiscal years of a qualifying MNE group that begin on or after December 31, 2023.
The portion of subsection 52(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subject to subsection 53(3), the domestic top-up amount, of a constituent entity (other than a securitization entity) of an MNE group that is located in Canada for a fiscal year, means the amount that would be the top-up amount of the constituent entity for the fiscal year determined under subsection 30(1), 34(2), 35(1) or 36(2) (as adjusted under any other applicable provision of Part 2), as the case may be, if that amount (and any amounts or results relevant to the determination of that amount) were required to be determined and
Paragraph 52(1)(e) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: this Act were read without reference to section 44.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 52(1)(e) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (2), is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: this Act were read without reference to sections 44 and 46.1.

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (2) apply to fiscal years of a qualifying MNE group that begin on or after December 31, 2023.
Subsection (3) applies to fiscal years of a qualifying MNE group that begin on or after January 1, 2026.
Subsections 53(1) and (2) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Definition of initial phase of international activity year

Subsection.Text: In this section, initial phase of international activity year has the same meaning as in section 49.1.
Subsection (1) applies to fiscal years of a qualifying MNE group that begin on or after December 31, 2025.
Paragraph (b) of the definition designated filing entity in subsection 55(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is located in a jurisdiction in the fiscal year that has a qualified IIR, a qualified UTPR or a qualified domestic minimum top-up tax that has qualified domestic minimum top-up tax safe harbour status in effect for the fiscal year.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(entit√É¬© d√É¬©clarante d√É¬©sign√É¬©e)

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to fiscal years of a qualifying MNE group that begin on or after December 31, 2023.
Section 61 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):

A person that is liable to pay tax under Part 2.1 for a fiscal year must file in the prescribed form and manner with the Minister, on or before the GIR due date, a return for the fiscal year containing an estimate of the tax payable.
Subsection 61(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If more than one person, in respect of an MNE group, is required to file a return under subsection (1) to (2) for a fiscal year, one of those persons that is resident in Canada may file a return on behalf of all the persons that would, in the absence of subsection (4), be required to file a return for the fiscal year, if the person is appointed, with the consent of the person, as the Canadian filing entity in the return.
Paragraph 61(4)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Canadian filing entity must file the return √¢¬Ä¬î on behalf of all persons that are required to file returns under any of subsections (1) to (2) in respect of the MNE group for the fiscal year √¢¬Ä¬î on or before the GIR due date for the fiscal year; and

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) to (3) apply to fiscal years of a qualifying MNE group that begin on or after December 31, 2025.
Subsection 66(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Part 2 √¢¬Ä¬î joint and several, or solidary, liability

Subsection.Text: A particular constituent entity (other than a securitization entity) that is located in Canada is, in respect of tax and other amounts payable under this Part or Part 2 by another constituent entity of the same MNE group, jointly and severally, or solidarily, liable with the other constituent entity to pay those amounts and this Part applies to the particular constituent entity in respect of those amounts, with any modifications that the circumstances require.
Subsection 66(1) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (1), is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Parts 2 and 2.1 √¢¬Ä¬î joint and several, or solidary, liability

Subsection.Text: A particular constituent entity (other than a securitization entity) that is located in Canada is, in respect of tax and other amounts payable under this Part, Part 2 or Part 2.1 by another constituent entity of the same MNE group, jointly and severally, or solidarily, liable with the other constituent entity to pay those amounts and this Part applies to the particular constituent entity in respect of those amounts, with any modifications that the circumstances require.
Subsections 66(2) and (3) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Part 3 √¢¬Ä¬î joint and several, or solidary, liability
Subsection.Text: A particular constituent entity (other than a securitization entity) that is located in Canada is, in respect of tax and other amounts payable under this Part or Part 3 by another constituent entity of the same MNE group or a joint venture entity in respect of the same MNE group, jointly and severally, or solidarily, liable with the other constituent entity or joint venture entity to pay those amounts and this Part applies to the particular constituent entity in respect of those amounts, with any modifications that the circumstances require.
Subsection 66(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Parts 2.1 and 3 √¢¬Ä¬î joint and several, or solidary, liability of joint venture entities
Subsection.Text: A particular joint venture entity that is located in Canada is, in respect of tax and other amounts payable under this Part, Part 2.1 or Part 3 by another joint venture entity of the same joint venture group, jointly and severally, or solidarily, liable with the other joint venture entity to pay those amounts and this Part applies to the particular joint venture entity in respect of those amounts, with any modifications that the circumstances require.
Subsections (1) and (3) apply to fiscal years of a qualifying MNE group that begin on or after December 31, 2023.
Subsections (2) and (4) apply to fiscal years of a qualifying MNE group that begin on or after December 31, 2025.
Paragraph 85(2)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: with the written consent of the person, to dispose of an appeal of the person or another person; or

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to fiscal years of a qualifying MNE group that begin on or after December 31, 2023.

Section.Text: Schedule II to the Access to Information Act is amended by replacing the reference to √¢¬Ä¬úsection 121√¢¬Ä¬ù opposite the reference to √¢¬Ä¬úGlobal Minimum Tax Act√¢¬Ä¬ù with a reference to √¢¬Ä¬úsection 123√¢¬Ä¬ù.

Subsection.Text: The Income Tax Conventions Interpretation Act is amended by adding the following after section 4.3:

Section.MarginalNote: Application of the Global Minimum Tax Act
Section.Text: Notwithstanding the provisions of a convention or the Act giving the convention the force of law in Canada, in applying the provisions of the convention, nothing prevents the application of the Global Minimum Tax Act, as amended from time to time, or obliges Canada to provide relief in any form for tax imposed under similar legislation in the other state.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2024.

Section.Text: Subparagraph 18.29(3)(a)(vi.01) of the Tax Court of Canada Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (vi.01)
Subparagraph.Text: section 89 or 91 of the Global Minimum Tax Act,

Heading.Label: PART 3
Heading.TitleText: Amendments to the Excise Tax Act, the Excise Act, the Excise Act, 2001 and Other Related Texts

Heading.Label: DIVISION 1
Heading.TitleText: Excise Tax Act and Other Related Texts (GST/HST)

Subsection.Text: The definition bank in subsection 123(1) of the Excise Tax Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: bank√¢¬Ä¬Çmeans a bank or an authorized foreign bank, as those terms are defined in section 2 of the Bank Act, but does not include a credit union;√¢¬Ä¬Ç(banque)

Subsection.Text: Subsection 123(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association√¢¬Ä¬Çmeans an association of persons formed on the plan known as Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs, whereby each member of the association participating in an insurance policy becomes liable for a stated, limited or proportionate part of the whole amount payable under the insurance policy;√¢¬Ä¬Ç(association Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs)

Subsection.Text: Section 123 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (4):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Meaning of administration or enforcement of this Part
Subsection.Text: For greater certainty, a reference in this Part to administration or enforcement of this Part includes the collection of any amount payable or remittable under this Part and the administration of a listed international agreement or of a tax treaty (as defined in subsection 248(1) of the Income Tax Act).
Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on December 19, 2012.
Subsection (2) is deemed to have come into force on August 10, 2022.
Paragraph 149(5)(a) of the Act is amended by adding the following after subparagraph (iv.1):

Subparagraph.Label: (iv.2)
Subparagraph.Text: a first home savings account,

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies in respect of any taxation year of a person that begins after August 12, 2024.
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 149:

For the purposes of this Part, if a Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association that is registered under Subdivision D of Division V would, in the absence of this subsection, be regarded as having ceased to exist, the Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association is deemed not to have ceased to exist until the registration of the Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association is cancelled.

For the purposes of this Part, if the membership of a Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association changes but the number assigned to the Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association by, or under the authority of, the council constituted by section 3 of the Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs Act 1982, c. 14 (United Kingdom) does not change as a result of that change in membership, the Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association is deemed to continue as the same Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association.

For the purposes of this Part, if the members of a Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association participate in an insurance policy and each member is liable for a stated, limited or proportionate part of the whole amount payable under the insurance policy, the Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association is deemed to be an insurer, to have issued the insurance policy and to be insuring any risk that is insured under the insurance policy.

If, under the terms of a particular agreement entered into by the members of a particular Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association (referred to in this subsection as the √¢¬Ä¬úreinsured members√¢¬Ä¬ù) and either the members of another Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association (referred to in this subsection as the √¢¬Ä¬úreinsuring members√¢¬Ä¬ù) or a particular person that is prescribed, the reinsuring members or the particular person, as the case may be, agree to discharge, procure the discharge of or indemnify the reinsured members against all of the known and unknown liabilities of the reinsured members arising out of all of the insurance policies issued by the particular Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association that are allocated to a particular time period for accounting purposes (referred to in this subsection as the √¢¬Ä¬úcovered policies√¢¬Ä¬ù), the following rules apply:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: for the purposes of this Part and throughout the time the particular agreement is in effect, the other Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association or the particular person, as the case may be, is deemed to be an insurer, to have issued the covered policies and to be insuring any risk that is insured under the covered policies; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the particular Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association, the other Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association and the reinsuring members or, as the case may be, the particular Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association and the particular person are jointly and severally, or solidarily, liable for

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the payment of any amount of tax under Division II or III, section 218, subsection 218.1(1) or Division IV.1 that becomes payable by the particular Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association, but only to the extent that the amount can reasonably be considered to relate to the covered policies,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the remittance of any amount of net tax for any reporting period of the particular Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association, but only to the extent that the amount can reasonably be considered to relate to the covered policies,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the payment of any amount of tax under section 218.01 or subsection 218.1(1.2) that is determined for a specified year (as defined in section 217) of the particular Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association, but only to the extent that the amount can reasonably be considered to relate to the covered policies, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: the payment or remittance of any amount for which a third Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association and the particular Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association are jointly and severally, or solidarily, liable as a result of the application of this paragraph in respect of another agreement that was entered into by the members of the third Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association and the members of the particular Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association prior to the entering into of the particular agreement and that is in respect of one or more of the covered policies, but only to the extent that the amount can reasonably be considered to relate to those covered policies.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on August 10, 2022.
Paragraph (b) of the description of A in subsection 193(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Text.Ins: whichever of the following amounts is applicable:

FormulaParagraph.Text: if subparagraph (ii) does not apply, the tax that is or would be, in the absence of section 167, payable in respect of the particular taxable supply, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the registrant is a selected listed financial institution at the particular time, the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the tax that is or would be, in the absence of section 167, payable in respect of the particular taxable supply,
is the total of all amounts, each of which is determined for a participating province by the formula
is the tax under subsection 165(1) that is or would be, in the absence of section 167, payable in respect of the particular taxable supply,
is the registrant√¢¬Ä¬ôs percentage for the participating province determined for the purposes of subsection 225.2(2) for the registrant√¢¬Ä¬ôs taxation year that includes the particular time,
is the tax rate for the participating province, and
is the rate set out in subsection 165(1), and
is the tax under subsection 165(2) that is or would be, in the absence of section 167, payable in respect of the particular taxable supply, and

Subsection.Text: The description of C in paragraph 193(2.1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

FormulaDefinition.Text: is

FormulaParagraph.Text: if subparagraph (ii) does not apply, the tax that is or would be, in the absence of section 167, payable in respect of the particular taxable supply, or

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the public sector body is a selected listed financial institution at the particular time, the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the tax that is or would be, in the absence of section 167, payable in respect of the particular taxable supply,
is the total of all amounts, each of which is determined for a participating province by the formula
is the tax under subsection 165(1) that is or would be, in the absence of section 167, payable in respect of the particular taxable supply,

is the public sector body√¢¬Ä¬ôs percentage for the participating province determined for the purposes of subsection 225.2(2) for the public sector body√¢¬Ä¬ôs taxation year that includes the particular time,
is the tax rate for the participating province, and
is the rate set out in subsection 165(1), and
is the tax under subsection 165(2) that is or would be, in the absence of section 167, payable in respect of the particular taxable supply.

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (2) apply in respect of any supply in respect of which tax becomes payable, or would become payable in the absence of section 167 of the Act, after August 14, 2025.
Paragraph 205(4)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in acquiring a business or part of a business from a registrant, a financial institution that is a registrant is deemed under subsection 167(1.1) to have acquired property for use exclusively in commercial activities of the institution, and

Subsection.Text: Subsection 205(5) of the Act is repealed.
The definition Canadian activity in section 217 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: Canadian activity√¢¬Ä¬Çof a person means an activity of the person carried on, engaged in or conducted in Canada or an activity of the person in respect of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an insurance policy that is issued by the person and that insures a risk in respect of property ordinarily situated in Canada or in respect of a person resident in Canada;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a debt that arises from

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the deposit of funds in Canada, if the instrument issued as evidence of the deposit is a negotiable instrument, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the lending of money that is primarily for use in Canada;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a debt for all or part of the consideration for a supply of real property that is situated in Canada;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a debt for all or part of the consideration for a supply of personal property that is for use primarily in Canada; or

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: a debt for all or part of the consideration for a supply of a service that is to be performed primarily in Canada.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(activit√É¬© au Canada)

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on August 13, 2024. It also applies in respect of the specified year of a person that includes August 13, 2024.
Paragraph 217.1(6)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the whole or part of property (in this section referred to as √¢¬Ä¬úattributable property√¢¬Ä¬ù) or of a qualifying service (in this section referred to as an √¢¬Ä¬úattributable service√¢¬Ä¬ù) to which the qualifying expenditure is attributable is deemed to have been acquired by the qualifying taxpayer at the time at which the outlay was made or the expense was incurred;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 217.1(7)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the whole or part of property (in this section referred to as √¢¬Ä¬úinternal property√¢¬Ä¬ù) or of a qualifying service (in this section referred to as an √¢¬Ä¬úinternal service√¢¬Ä¬ù) to which the outlay or expense is attributable is deemed to have been supplied to the qualifying taxpayer at the time the outlay was made or the expense was incurred;

Subsection.Text: Section 217.1 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (8):
For the purposes of this Division, the following rules apply in respect of a Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the members of the Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association are liable at any time in a specified year of the Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association under an insurance policy that was issued by the Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association and that insures a risk in respect of property ordinarily situated in Canada or in respect of a person resident in Canada, the Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association is deemed to be a qualifying taxpayer throughout the specified year;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any activity of a member of the Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association in respect of an insurance policy that was issued by the Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association and that insures a risk in respect of property ordinarily situated in Canada or in respect of a person resident in Canada is deemed to be a Canadian activity of the Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if a member of the Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association makes an outlay, or incurs an expense, outside Canada, if the outlay or expense may reasonably be regarded as being applicable to a Canadian activity of the Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association and if a particular amount in respect of the outlay or expense would be qualifying consideration for a particular specified year of the member if the outlay or expense were applicable to a Canadian activity of the member and the member were a qualifying taxpayer throughout the particular specified year, the amount determined by the following formula is deemed to be qualifying consideration for the specified year of the Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association in which the particular specified year ends:

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the particular amount, and
is the total of all amounts, each of which is an amount that is paid to the member as a reimbursement of the outlay or expense;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: if an amount in respect of an outlay or expense is a particular amount of qualifying consideration for a specified year of the Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association and an amount in respect of the same outlay or expense is another amount of qualifying consideration for a specified year of a member of the Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association, the other amount of qualifying consideration is not to be included in determining tax under section 218.01 or subsection 218.1(1.2) for the specified year of the member;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: if a member of the Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association makes an outlay, or incurs an expense, outside Canada, if the outlay or expense may reasonably be regarded as being applicable to a Canadian activity of the Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association and if a particular amount in respect of the outlay or expense would be an external charge for a particular specified year of the member if the outlay or expense were applicable to a Canadian activity of the member and the member were a qualifying taxpayer throughout the particular specified year, the amount determined by the following formula is deemed to be an external charge for the specified year of the Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association in which the particular specified year ends:

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the particular amount, and
is the total of all amounts, each of which is an amount that is paid to the member as a reimbursement of the outlay or expense;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: if an amount in respect of an outlay or expense is a particular external charge for a specified year of the Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association and an amount in respect of the same outlay or expense is another external charge for a specified year of a member of the Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association, the other external charge is not to be included in determining tax under section 218.01 or subsection 218.1(1.2) for the specified year of the member;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: if a transaction or dealing between a particular qualifying establishment in Canada of a member of the Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association and another qualifying establishment of the member in a country other than Canada may reasonably be regarded as being applicable to a Canadian activity of the Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association and if a part of an amount in respect of the transaction or dealing would be an internal charge for a particular specified year of the member if the member were a qualifying taxpayer throughout the particular specified year,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount determined by the following formula is deemed to be an internal charge for the specified year of the Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association in which the particular specified year ends:

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the part of the amount in respect of the transaction or dealing, and

is the total of all amounts, each of which is an amount that is paid to the member as a reimbursement of an outlay made, or expense incurred, outside Canada by the member that is included in the determination of the part of the amount in respect of the transaction or dealing, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: for each outlay made, or expense incurred, outside Canada by the member that is included in the determination of the part of the amount in respect of the transaction or dealing, the outlay or expense is deemed not to have been made or incurred by the member and the amount determined by the following formula is deemed to be an outlay made, or expense incurred, outside Canada by the Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association:

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount of the outlay or expense, and
is the total of all amounts, each of which is an amount that is paid to the member as a reimbursement of the outlay or expense;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: if a part of an amount in respect of a transaction or dealing is a particular internal charge for a specified year of the Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association and a part of the same amount is another internal charge for a specified year of a member of the Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association, the other internal charge is not to be included in determining tax under section 218.01 or subsection 218.1(1.2) for the specified year of the member;

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: the Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association is deemed not to deal at arm√¢¬Ä¬ôs length with

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the society incorporated by the Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs Act, 1871, c. 21 (United Kingdom), by the name of Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a person referred to in any of subparagraphs 14(2)(a)(ii) to (vii) of the Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs Act 1982, c. 14 (United Kingdom), as amended from time to time; and

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: the members of the Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association are deemed not to deal at arm√¢¬Ä¬ôs length with the persons referred to in subparagraph (i)(i) or (ii) in respect of any supply, transaction or series of transactions that may reasonably be regarded as being applicable to an activity of the Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of applying subsections (6) and (7) and determining an input tax credit under section 169 in respect of an amount of tax under section 218.01 or subsection 218.1(1.2) for a specified year of a Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the amount is in respect of qualifying consideration that is deemed to be qualifying consideration for the specified year under paragraph (9)(c) or an external charge that is deemed to be an external charge for the specified year under paragraph (9)(e), the attributable property or attributable service to which the qualifying consideration or external charge relates is deemed to have been acquired by the Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the amount is in respect of an internal charge that is deemed to be an internal charge for the specified year under paragraph (9)(g), the internal property or internal service to which the internal charge relates is deemed to have been acquired by the Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this Division, if an agreement that is described in subsection 149.1(4) is entered into by the members of a particular Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association and either the members of another Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association or a particular person that is prescribed for the purposes of that subsection and if the agreement is in respect of one or more insurance policies that insure a risk in respect of property ordinarily situated in Canada or in respect of a person resident in Canada, the following rules apply:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the other Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association or the particular person, as the case may be, is deemed to be a qualifying taxpayer throughout every specified year of the other Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association or of the particular person that includes a time at which the agreement is in effect; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the agreement is entered into by a particular person that is prescribed for the purposes of subsection 149.1(4), the particular person is deemed not to deal at arm√¢¬Ä¬ôs length with

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the society incorporated by the Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs Act, 1871, c. 21 (United Kingdom), by the name of Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a person referred to in any of subparagraphs 14(2)(a)(ii) to (vii) of the Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs Act 1982, c. 14 (United Kingdom), as amended from time to time.

Subsection.Text: Despite subsection (1), a person that is a member of a Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association at any time in a specified year of the person and that is a person referred to in subparagraph 149(1)(a)(v) at any time in the specified year is not a qualifying taxpayer throughout the specified year if, throughout the specified year, the person

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is not a person referred to in any of subparagraphs 149(1)(a)(i) to (iv) and (vi) to (xi);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is not a financial institution because of paragraph 149(1)(b) or (c); and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: does not carry on an insurance business in Canada outside of any Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association.

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (2) are deemed to have come into force on August 10, 2022.

Subsection (3) applies in respect of any specified year of a person that ends after August 9, 2022, except that, in applying paragraph 217.1(9)(b) of the Act (as enacted by subsection (3)) in respect of any specified year of a person that ends after August 9, 2022 but before August 13, 2024, that paragraph is to be read as follows:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any Canadian activity of a member of the Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association in respect of an insurance policy that was issued by the Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association is deemed to be a Canadian activity of the Lloyd√¢¬Ä¬ôs association;

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 238(2.1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Filing by certain selected listed financial institutions
Subsection.Text: Despite paragraph (1)(b) and subsection (2), if a selected listed financial institution√¢¬Ä¬ôs reporting period is a period other than a fiscal year, the financial institution must
Section 238 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (3):

Despite subsections (1) to (3), if an individual dies on a particular day and if a return, interim return or final return for a reporting period of the individual or of the estate of the individual would, in the absence of this subsection, be required to be filed under any of subsections (1) to (3) on or before a day that is on or after the particular day and is less than six months after the particular day, the estate of the individual must file the return, interim return or final return, as the case may be, with the Minister on or before the last day of the sixth month after the month that includes the particular day.
Subsection (1) applies in respect of reporting periods that end after August 15, 2025.

Subsection (2) applies in respect of returns, interim returns and final returns that, in the absence of subsection 238(3.1) of the Act (as enacted by subsection (2)), would be required to be filed under any of subsections 238(1) to (3) of the Act, as amended by subsection (1), on or before a day that is after August 15, 2025.
Subsection 273.2(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subject to subsection (3.1), a reporting institution must file an information return with the Minister for a fiscal year of the reporting institution in prescribed form containing prescribed information on or before the day that is six months after the end of the fiscal year.

If an individual dies on a particular day and an information return for a fiscal year of the individual would, in the absence of this subsection, be required to be filed under subsection (3) on or before a day that is on or after the particular day and is less than six months after the particular day, the estate of the individual must file the information return with the Minister on or before the last day of the sixth month after the month that includes the particular day.

Subsection (1) applies in respect of information returns that, in the absence of subsection 273.2(3.1) of the Act (as enacted by subsection (1)), would be required to be filed under subsection 273.2(3) of the Act on or before a day that is after August 15, 2025.

Section.Text: The portion of paragraph 288(1)(c) of the Act before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: require any person to give the authorized person all reasonable assistance, to answer all proper questions relating to the administration or enforcement of this Part and

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 289(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Requirement to provide documents or information

Subsection.Text: Despite any other provision of this Part, the Minister may, subject to subsection (2), for any purpose related to the administration or enforcement of this Part, by a notice served or sent in accordance with subsection (1.1), require that any person provide the Minister, within any reasonable time that is stipulated in the notice, with
Paragraph 289(3)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the requirement is made to verify compliance by the person or persons in the group with any duty or obligation under this Part or is made for a purpose related to the administration of a listed international agreement or of a tax treaty (as defined in subsection 248(1) of the Income Tax Act).

Section.Text: Subsection 292(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Definition of foreign-based information or document

Subsection.Text: In this section, foreign-based information or document means any information or document that is available or located outside Canada and that may be relevant to the administration or enforcement of this Part.

TitleText.XRefExternal: Financial Services and Financial Institutions (GST/HST) Regulations

Subsection.Text: Section 3 of the Financial Services and Financial Institutions (GST/HST) Regulations is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: Any service in relation to the clearing and settlement of cheques and other payment items under the national payments system of the Canadian Payments Association that is supplied by the Association or any of its members, other than a person that is entitled to be a member of the Association only because of the application of paragraph 4(2)(i) of the Canadian Payments Act, is prescribed for the purposes of paragraph (m) of the definition financial service in subsection 123(1) of the Act.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on September 25, 2025.
The schedule to the Amalgamations and Windings-Up Continuation (GST/HST) Regulations is amended by adding the following in numerical order:
Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on August 4, 2023.

TitleText.XRefExternal: Selected Listed Financial Institutions Attribution Method (GST/HST) Regulations

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (a) of the definition investment plan in subsection 1(1) of the Selected Listed Financial Institutions Attribution Method (GST/HST) Regulations is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a trust governed by a registered retirement savings plan, a registered retirement income fund, a registered disability savings plan, a TFSA or a first home savings account; or

Subsection.Text: Subsection 1(2) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

For the purposes of these Regulations, deferred profit sharing plan, employee benefit plan, employee life and health trust, employee trust, employees profit sharing plan, first home savings account, investment corporation, mortgage investment corporation, mutual fund corporation, mutual fund trust, non-resident-owned investment corporation, registered disability savings plan, registered education savings plan, registered retirement income fund, registered retirement savings plan, registered supplementary unemployment benefit plan, retirement compensation arrangement, TFSA and unit trust have the same meanings as in subsection 248(1) of the Income Tax Act.
Subsections (1) and (2) apply in respect of any reporting period of a person that ends after August 12, 2024.
The portion of paragraph 3(a) of the Regulations before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 3(b) and (c) of the Regulations are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if a financial institution (other than a bank, credit union or investment plan) is an insurer that, at any time in a taxation year of the financial institution, is insuring a risk in respect of property ordinarily situated in a province or in respect of a person resident in a province, the financial institution is deemed to have a permanent establishment in the province throughout the taxation year;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if a financial institution (other than a bank, credit union, insurer or investment plan) is a trust and loan corporation, a trust corporation or a loan corporation and if, at any time in a taxation year of the financial institution, the financial institution conducts business (other than business in respect of loans) in a province or, at any time in that year, a loan that was made by the financial institution is outstanding and is secured by real property situated in a province or, if not secured by real property, is owing by a person resident in a province, the financial institution is deemed to have a permanent establishment in the province throughout the taxation year;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: if a financial institution is a master pension entity, the financial institution is deemed to have a permanent establishment in a province throughout a taxation year of the financial institution if, at any time in the taxation year,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)

Subsection 14(1) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

If an investment plan is, or reasonably expects to be, a qualifying small investment plan or a qualifying private investment plan for a fiscal year of the investment plan and if no application by the investment plan under subsection 15(1) in respect of the fiscal year has been approved by the Minister, the investment plan may make an election to be a prescribed financial institution for the purpose of paragraph 225.2(1)(b) of the Act that is effective from the first day of the fiscal year.
Subsection 14(6) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

If the Minister allows an investment plan to revoke an election made under subsection (1) on the first day of a fiscal year that begins less than three years after the election became effective and the investment plan is a qualifying small investment plan or a qualifying private investment plan for the fiscal year, section 9 does not apply in respect of any reporting period in the fiscal year.
Subsections (1) and (2) apply in respect of any fiscal year of a person that ends after August 9, 2022.
Subsection 15(2) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

On receipt of an application made by an investment plan under subsection (1) in respect of a particular fiscal year of the investment plan and the fiscal year of the investment plan following the particular fiscal year, the Minister must, within 90 days of that receipt, consider the application and, if it is reasonable, based on the information in the possession of the Minister, to expect that the investment plan will be a qualifying small investment plan or a qualifying private investment plan for each of those two fiscal years, approve the application or, in any other case, refuse the application, and must, within that time limit, notify the investment plan in writing of the decision.
Paragraphs 15(3)(a) and (b) of the Regulations are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the investment plan is a qualifying small investment plan or a qualifying private investment plan for the following fiscal year, section 9 does not apply in respect of any reporting period in the following fiscal year.

if an election under section 49 or 61 is in effect in respect of the particular predecessor immediately before the plan merger, the particular predecessor√¢¬Ä¬ôs percentage for the participating province as of the last day on which that percentage is required to be determined for the purposes of subsection 225.2(2) of the Act, as adapted by these Regulations, before the plan merger, and
in any other case, the particular predecessor√¢¬Ä¬ôs percentage for the participating province and for the last particular period of the particular predecessor ending before the plan merger, and

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) to (4) apply in respect of any reporting period of a person that begins after August 9, 2022.

Despite subsection (1), if on a particular day a plan merger between two or more predecessors occurs to form a particular non-stratified investment plan that is an exchange-traded fund and a selected listed financial institution, the following rules apply:
The portion of subparagraph (i) of the description of A in paragraph 34(3)(a) of the Regulations before the formula is replaced by the following:

is the total of all amounts, each of which is the total value immediately before the plan merger of the units of a series of the particular predecessor (including the predecessor series) that were converted, by any means, into units of the particular investment plan by virtue of the plan merger,

Section 40 of the Regulations is amended by adding the following after paragraph (a):

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: any amount of tax that became payable, or that was paid without having become payable, by a surety √¢¬Ä¬î being a person acting as a surety under a performance bond in respect of a contract for the supply of construction services relating to real property situated in Canada √¢¬Ä¬î in respect of property or a service acquired, imported or brought into a participating province

Subparagraph.Text: exclusively and directly for consumption, use or supply in the course of carrying on, or of engaging another person to carry on, construction that is undertaken in full or partial satisfaction of the surety√¢¬Ä¬ôs obligations under the performance bond, and

Subparagraph.Text: otherwise than for use as capital property of the surety or in improving capital property of the surety;

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies in respect of any amount of tax that becomes payable, or that is paid without having become payable, during a reporting period of a person that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: ends after August 15, 2025; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: ends after October 8, 1998 and on or before August 15, 2025, unless the amount of tax is included by the person in determining the positive or negative amounts that the person is required to add, or may deduct, under subsection 225.2(2) of the Excise Tax Act in determining the net tax of the person for the reporting period.

Section.Text: If a plan merger occurs to form an investment plan or a series of an investment plan and if the investment plan is, immediately after the plan merger, a selected listed financial institution, the following rules apply:

Heading.Label: DIVISION 2
Heading.TitleText: Excise Act, Excise Act, 2001 and Other Related Texts

Section.Text: Section 99 of the Excise Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (4):

Subsection.Text: The Minister may, on or before the day that is 10 calendar years after the end of a period for which a return is required by this Act to be made by a person, or on application by the person on or before that day, waive or reduce any penalty payable by the person under subsection (4) in respect of the return.

Section.Text: Section 110.1 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Waiving or reducing penalty or interest
Subsection.Text: The Minister may, on or before the particular day that is 10 calendar years after the day an amount was required to be paid by a person under this Act, or on application by the person on or before the particular day, waive or reduce any penalty or interest on the amount payable by the person under subsection (1).

Section.Text: Paragraph (a) of the definition sale price in section 2 of the Excise Act, 2001 is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the amount charged as the price for the cigars before an amount payable in respect of a tax under Part IX of the Excise Tax Act is added and before an amount of duty imposed on the cigars under section 43 is added,

Section.Text: Section 4 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Meaning of administration or enforcement of this Act
Section.Text: For greater certainty, a reference in this Act to administration or enforcement of this Act includes the collection of any amount payable under this Act and the administration of a listed international agreement or of a tax treaty (as defined in subsection 248(1) of the Income Tax Act).
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 6:

Except as specifically otherwise provided, if an amount or a number is required under this Act to be determined or calculated by or in accordance with an algebraic formula and the amount or number when so determined or calculated would, in the absence of this section, be a negative amount or number, it is deemed to be zero.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 158.25(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Specified province √¢¬Ä¬î duty on cannabis taken for use
Subsection.Text: If a particular person is responsible for cannabis products at a particular time when the cannabis products are taken for use, the cannabis products are relieved of the duty, if any, imposed under subsection 158.2(1) and a duty in respect of a specified province is imposed, in addition to the duty imposed under subsection (1), on the cannabis products in prescribed circumstances in the amount determined in prescribed manner.
Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on August 12, 2024.
Subsection 158.26(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Specified province √¢¬Ä¬î duty on unaccounted cannabis
Subsection.Text: If a particular person that is responsible at a particular time for cannabis products cannot account for the cannabis products as being, at the particular time, in the possession of a cannabis licensee or in the possession of another person in accordance with subsection 158.11(3) or paragraph 158.11(5)(a), the cannabis products are relieved of the duty, if any, imposed under subsection 158.2(1) and a duty in respect of a specified province is imposed, in addition to the duty imposed under subsection (1), on the cannabis products in prescribed circumstances in the amount determined in prescribed manner.
Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on August 12, 2024.
Paragraph 158.6(1)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the vaping products are relieved of the duty imposed under section 158.57; and

Subsection.Text: Subsection 158.6(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Specified vaping province √¢¬Ä¬î taken for use
Subsection.Text: If a particular person is responsible for vaping products at a particular time when the vaping products are taken for use, the vaping products are relieved of the duty, if any, imposed under section 158.58 and a duty in respect of a specified vaping province is imposed, in addition to the duty imposed under subsection (1), on the vaping products in prescribed circumstances in the amount determined in prescribed manner.
Subsections (1) and (2) are deemed to have come into force on August 12, 2024.
Paragraph 158.61(1)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the vaping products are relieved of the duty imposed under section 158.57; and

Subsection.Text: Subsection 158.61(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Specified vaping province √¢¬Ä¬î unaccounted vaping products
Subsection.Text: If a particular person that is responsible at a particular time for vaping products cannot account for the vaping products as being, at the particular time, in the possession of a vaping product licensee or in the possession of another person in accordance with subsection 158.44(3), the vaping products are relieved of the duty, if any, imposed under section 158.58 and a duty in respect of a specified vaping province is imposed, in addition to the duty imposed under subsection (1), on the vaping products in prescribed circumstances in the amount determined in prescribed manner.
Subsections (1) and (2) are deemed to have come into force on August 12, 2024.
The portion of subsection 208(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Requirement to provide records or information

Subsection.Text: Despite any other provision of this Act, the Minister may, subject to subsection (2), for any purpose related to the administration or enforcement of this Act, by a notice served or sent in accordance with subsection (1.1), require any person to provide the Minister, within any reasonable time that is stipulated in the notice, with
Paragraph 208(3)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the requirement is made to verify compliance by the person or persons in the group with any duty or obligation under this Act or is made for a purpose related to the administration of a listed international agreement or of a tax treaty (as defined in subsection 248(1) of the Income Tax Act).

TitleText.XRefExternal: Regulations Respecting Excise Licences and Registrations

Section.Text: Paragraph 4(b) of the Regulations Respecting Excise Licences and Registrations is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a spirits licence, a wine licence, a user√¢¬Ä¬ôs licence, a vaping product licence or an excise warehouse licence (other than an excise warehouse licence authorizing a person to possess in their excise warehouse manufactured tobacco or cigars that are not stamped), shall not exceed three years; or

TitleText.XRefExternal: Stamping and Marking of Tobacco, Cannabis and Vaping Products Regulations

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 2(a) of the Stamping and Marking of Tobacco, Cannabis and Vaping Products Regulations is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: raw leaf tobacco is packaged in a prescribed package when it is packaged

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: in a package that contains no more than 500 grams of raw leaf tobacco, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in the smallest package √¢¬Ä¬î including any outer wrapper, package, box or other container √¢¬Ä¬î in which it is sold to the consumer;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 2(c) of the French version of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: c)
