Paragraph.Text: dans le cas d√¢¬Ä¬ôun produit du cannabis ou d√¢¬Ä¬ôun produit de vapotage, le plus petit emballage, y compris l√¢¬Ä¬ôenveloppe ext√É¬©rieure, l√¢¬Ä¬ôemballage, la bo√É¬Æte ou autre contenant, dans lequel il est vendu au consommateur.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) comes into force on the first day of the month after the month in which this Act receives royal assent.

Subsection 5(2) of the Regulations is amended by striking out √¢¬Ä¬úor√¢¬Ä¬ù at the end of paragraph (c), by adding √¢¬Ä¬úor√¢¬Ä¬ù at the end of paragraph (d) and by adding the following after paragraph (d):

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: 500 grams of packaged raw leaf tobacco.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) comes into force on the first day of the month after the month in which this Act receives royal assent.

Heading.Label: DIVISION 1
Heading.TitleText: Legislation Related to Financial Institutions (Bearer Instruments)

Section.Text: Section 36 of the Trust and Loan Companies Act is renumbered as subsection 36(1) and is amended by adding the following:

Subsection.Text: If a company, before it was continued under this Act, had issued a document in registered form that is convertible to bearer form and that evidences conversion privileges, options or rights to acquire a share, it shall not, if a holder of such a document exercises the conversion privilege attached, issue a document in bearer form that evidences conversion privileges, options or rights to acquire a share.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 72:

Subsection.Text: Despite section 72, a company shall not issue, in bearer form, documents that evidence conversion privileges, options or rights to acquire a share of the company.

On the request of a holder of a document that evidences conversion privileges, options or rights to acquire a share of a company, that is in bearer form and that was issued before the day on which this section comes into force, the company shall issue in exchange to the holder, in registered form, a document that evidences the conversion privileges, options or rights.

Section.Text: Section 93 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A company may issue for each fractional share a certificate in registered form or a scrip certificate in registered form that entitles the holder to receive a certificate for a full share in exchange for scrip certificates equalling a full share.

On the request of a holder of a certificate for a fractional share or scrip certificate that is in bearer form and was issued before the day on which this subsection comes into force, a company shall issue in exchange to the holder, in registered form, a certificate for a fractional share or scrip certificate, as the case may be.

Section.Text: Section 102 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Unless otherwise agreed and subject to the provisions of this Act, of any other Act of Parliament, of any Act of the legislature of a province or of any regulation made under any of those Acts or to any applicable stock exchange rule, a person required to deliver securities may deliver any security of the specified issue.
Section 38 of the Bank Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Subsection.Text: If a bank, before it was continued under this Act, had issued a document in registered form that is convertible to bearer form and that evidences conversion privileges, options or rights to acquire a share, it shall not, if a holder of such a document exercises the conversion privilege attached, issue a document in bearer form that evidences conversion privileges, options or rights to acquire a share.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 69:

Subsection.Text: Despite section 69, a bank shall not issue, in bearer form, documents that evidence conversion privileges, options or rights to acquire a share of the bank.

On the request of a holder of a document that evidences conversion privileges, options or rights to acquire a share of a bank, that is in bearer form and that was issued before the day on which this section comes into force, the bank shall issue in exchange to the holder, in registered form, a document that evidences the conversion privileges, options or rights.

Section.Text: Section 90 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A bank may issue for each fractional share a certificate in registered form or a scrip certificate in registered form that entitles the holder to receive a certificate for a full share in exchange for scrip certificates equalling a full share.

On the request of a holder of a certificate for a fractional share or scrip certificate that is in bearer form and was issued before the day on which this subsection comes into force, a bank shall issue in exchange to the holder, in registered form, a certificate for a fractional share or scrip certificate, as the case may be.

Section.Text: Section 99 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Unless otherwise agreed and subject to the provisions of this Act, of any other Act of Parliament, of any Act of the legislature of a province or of any regulation made under any of those Acts or to any applicable stock exchange rule, a person required to deliver securities may deliver any security of the specified issue.
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 713:

Subsection.Text: Despite section 713, a bank holding company shall not issue, in bearer form, documents that evidence conversion privileges, options or rights to acquire a share of the bank holding company.

On the request of a holder of a document that evidences conversion privileges, options or rights to acquire a share of a bank holding company, that is in bearer form and that was issued before the day on which this section comes into force, the bank holding company shall issue in exchange to the holder, in registered form, a document that evidences the conversion privileges, options or rights.

Section.Text: Section 37 of the Insurance Companies Act is renumbered as subsection 37(1) and is amended by adding the following:

Subsection.Text: If a company, before it was continued under this Act, had issued a document in registered form that is convertible to bearer form and that evidences conversion privileges, options or rights to acquire a share, it shall not, if a holder of such a document exercises the conversion privilege attached, issue a document in bearer form that evidences conversion privileges, options or rights to acquire a share.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 73:

Subsection.Text: Despite section 73, a company shall not issue, in bearer form, documents that evidence conversion privileges, options or rights to acquire a share of the company.

On the request of a holder of a document that evidences conversion privileges, options or rights to acquire a share of a company, that is in bearer form and that was issued before the day on which this section comes into force, the company shall issue in exchange to the holder, in registered form, a document that evidences the conversion privileges, options or rights.

Section.Text: Section 94 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A company may issue for each fractional share a certificate in registered form or a scrip certificate in registered form that entitles the holder to receive a certificate for a full share in exchange for scrip certificates equalling a full share.

On the request of a holder of a certificate for a fractional share or scrip certificate, if the certificate is in bearer form and was issued before the day on which this subsection comes into force, a company shall issue in exchange to the holder, in registered form, a certificate for a fractional share or scrip certificate, as the case may be.

Section.Text: Section 103 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Unless otherwise agreed and subject to the provisions of this Act, of any other Act of Parliament, of any Act of the legislature of a province or of any regulation made under any of those Acts or to any applicable stock exchange rule, a person required to deliver securities may deliver any security of the specified issue.
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 752:

Subsection.Text: Despite section 752, an insurance holding company shall not issue, in bearer form, documents that evidence conversion privileges, options or rights to acquire a share of the insurance holding company.

On the request of a holder of a document that evidences conversion privileges, options or rights to acquire a share of an insurance holding company, that is in bearer form and that was issued before the day on which this section comes into force, the insurance holding company shall issue in exchange to the holder, in registered form, a document that evidences the conversion privileges, options or rights.

Heading.Label: DIVISION 2
Heading.TitleText: Legislation Related to Financial Institutions (Immunity √¢¬Ä¬î Good Faith Actions)

Section.Text: The Trust and Loan Companies Act is amended by adding the following after section 530:

No action lies against His Majesty in right of Canada, the Minister, the Commissioner, the Superintendent, any officer or employee of His Majesty in right of Canada or any person acting under the direction of the Minister, the Commissioner or the Superintendent for anything done or omitted to be done in good faith in the administration or discharge of any powers or duties that under this Act are intended or authorized to be executed or performed.
The Bank Act is amended by adding the following after section 977:

No action lies against His Majesty in right of Canada, the Minister, the Commissioner, the Superintendent, any officer or employee of His Majesty in right of Canada or any person acting under the direction of the Minister, the Commissioner or the Superintendent for anything done or omitted to be done in good faith in the administration or discharge of any powers or duties that under this Act are intended or authorized to be executed or performed.
The Insurance Companies Act is amended by adding the following after section 1020:

No action lies against His Majesty in right of Canada, the Minister, the Commissioner, the Superintendent, any officer or employee of His Majesty in right of Canada or any person acting under the direction of the Minister, the Commissioner or the Superintendent for anything done or omitted to be done in good faith in the administration or discharge of any powers or duties that under this Act are intended or authorized to be executed or performed.
The Bank Act is amended by adding the following after Part VIII:
The following definitions apply in this Part.

Definition.Text: deposit product√¢¬Ä¬Çincludes a deposit-type instrument, a principal-protected note and a retail deposit account, as those terms are defined in subsection 627.01(1).√¢¬Ä¬Ç(produit de d√É¬©p√É¬¥t)
institution√¢¬Ä¬Çhas the same meaning as in subsection 627.01(1).√¢¬Ä¬Ç(institution)

Subsection.Text: A bank must not directly offer or sell in Canada a deposit product of another Canadian financial institution unless the bank does so in a non-discriminatory manner.
An authorized foreign bank must not directly offer or sell in Canada a deposit product of a Canadian financial institution unless the authorized foreign bank does so in a non-discriminatory manner.

Section.Text: An institution must not offer or sell one of its deposit products in Canada through any of its affiliates unless the affiliate offers or sells the deposit product in a non-discriminatory manner.
The Minister may make regulations respecting what constitutes offering or selling a deposit product in a non-discriminatory manner for the purposes of section 463.2 or 463.3.
This Division comes into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 41(1) of the Financial Administration Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Regulations respecting conditions under which contracts awarded

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may make regulations with respect to the conditions under which contracts may be entered into by His Majesty or a Crown corporation and, despite any other Act of Parliament,
Paragraphs 41(1)(a) and (b) of the English version of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: may direct that no contract by the terms of which payments are required in excess of an amount that the Governor in Council may prescribe shall be entered into or have any force or effect unless entry into the contract has been approved by the Governor in Council or the Treasury Board; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: may make regulations with respect to the security to be given to and in the name of His Majesty to secure the due performance of contracts.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 41(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Restriction √¢¬Ä¬î Canada Pension Plan Investment Board
Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may only make regulations under subsection (1) with respect to the conditions under which contracts may be entered into by the Canada Pension Plan Investment Board if those contracts are covered by any provision of a trade agreement.

TitleText.XRefExternal: Department of Public Works and Government Services Act

Section.Text: Paragraph 22.1(3)(b) of the Department of Public Works and Government Services Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: review any complaint that is in respect of the award of a contract for the acquisition of materiel or services and that is prescribed by the regulations;

Section.Text: Subsection 22.2(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A person may file a complaint referred to in paragraph 22.1(3)(b) or (c) only if they meet any requirements prescribed by the regulations.

Section.Text: Paragraph 23.1(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the complaints referred to in paragraphs 22.1(3)(b) and (c), including the contracts and subject-matter that may be reviewed, the persons who may file complaints, any conditions that must be met before a complaint may be filed and the manner in which complaints must be filed; and

Heading.TitleText: Related Amendment to the National Capital Act

Section.Text: Subsection 15(3) of the National Capital Act is repealed.

Subsection.Text: Sections 247 and 251 come into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
Sections 248 to 250 come into force on a day or days to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.

Section.Text: Paragraphs 18(1)(a) and (b) of the Tax Court of Canada Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the aggregate of all amounts in issue is equal to or less than $50,000; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the amount of the loss that is determined under subsection 152(1.1) of that Act and that is in issue is equal to or less than $100,000.

Section.Text: Section 18.1 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Every judgment that allows an appeal referred to in subsection 18(1) is deemed to include a statement that the aggregate of all amounts in issue not be reduced by more than $50,000 or that the amount of the loss in issue not be increased by more than $100,000, as the case may be.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 18.11(2)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the aggregate of the following amounts exceeds $50,000:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: all amounts in issue in the appeal,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: all amounts likely to be affected in the other appeal referred to in subparagraph (a)(i), and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: all amounts likely to be affected in the other assessment or proposed assessment referred to in subparagraph (a)(ii).

Subsection.Text: Subsection 18.11(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:
The Court shall grant an application under subsection (1) if the amount of interest that is in issue in an appeal exceeds $50,000.
Paragraphs 18.12(1)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the aggregate of all amounts in issue exceeds $50,000; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the amount of the loss in issue exceeds $100,000.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 18.12(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:
Subsection (1) does not apply if the appellant elects to limit the aggregate of all amounts in issue to $50,000 or the amount of the loss in issue to $100,000, as the case may be.
Paragraphs 18.13(1)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the aggregate of all amounts in issue exceeds $50,000; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the amount of loss in issue exceeds $100,000.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 18.13(2)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the appellant elects to limit the aggregate of all amounts in issue to $50,000 or the amount of the loss in issue to $100,000, as the case may be; or

Section.Text: Section 18.27 of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Governor in Council may make regulations increasing the amount in dispute referred to in paragraphs 18.3002(3)(c) and 18.3008(c) and subparagraph 18.3009(1)(c)(i) to any amount that does not exceed $12,000.
Subparagraph 18.3001(c)(ii) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount in dispute does not exceed $100,000.

Section.Text: Section 18.30012 of the Act is replaced by the following:
Every judgment that allows an appeal referred to in paragraph 18.3001(c) is deemed to include a statement that the amount in dispute not be reduced by more than $100,000.
Section 18.30022 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Order for general procedure √¢¬Ä¬î Excise Tax Act
Section.Text: If, before the start of the hearing of an appeal referred to in paragraph 18.3001(c), it appears to the Court that the amount in dispute exceeds $100,000, the Court shall order that sections 17.1 to 17.8 apply in respect of the appeal unless the appellant elects to limit the appeal to $100,000.
The portion of section 18.30024 of the Act before paragraph (b) is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Order at hearing √¢¬Ä¬î Excise Tax Act
Section.Text: If, after the hearing of an appeal referred to in paragraph 18.3001(c) has started but before a judgment is rendered on the appeal, it appears to the Court that the amount in dispute exceeds $100,000, the Court shall, on motion of either party or of its own motion, order that sections 17.1 to 17.8 apply with respect to the appeal unless

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the appellant elects to limit the appeal to $100,000; or

Section.Text: Sections 18, 18.1, 18.11, 18.12, 18.13, 18.3001, 18.30012, 18.30022 and 18.30024 of the Tax Court of Canada Act, as they read immediately before the day on which this Division comes into force, continue to apply in respect of appeals for which a notice of appeal is filed with the Registry of the Tax Court of Canada on or before that day.
The Maximum Amounts for Informal Procedure Regulations are repealed.
Schedule II to the Access to Information Act is amended by adding, in alphabetical order, a reference to

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Loi sur les activit√É¬©s associ√É¬©es aux paiements de d√É¬©tail

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: and a corresponding reference to √¢¬Ä¬úsubsections 62(1) and 63(1)√¢¬Ä¬ù.

Heading.Label: DIVISION 7
Heading.TitleText: Outstanding Guarantee and Protected Loan Limits

Section.Text: Section 15 of the National Housing Act is replaced by the following:

Despite anything in this Act, the total of the outstanding guaranteed amounts of principal for all issues of securities in respect of which guarantees that have been given under section 14 are in force must not exceed the sum of

Paragraph.Text: any additional amounts authorized by Parliament under an appropriation Act or other Act of Parliament on or after the day on which the Budget 2025 Implementation Act, No. 2 receives royal assent.

TitleText.XRefExternal: Protection of Residential Mortgage or Hypothecary Insurance Act

Section.Text: The portion of section 27 of the Protection of Residential Mortgage or Hypothecary Insurance Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

The aggregate outstanding principal amount of the following loans must not at any time exceed $500,000,000,000 or any other amount authorized by Parliament under an appropriation Act or other Act of Parliament on or after the day on which the Budget 2025 Implementation Act, No. 2 receives royal assent:
The definition court in section 2 of the Bankruptcy and Insolvency Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: court, except in sections 9.2 and 9.3, paragraphs 178(1)(a) and (a.1) and sections 204.1 to 204.3, means a court referred to in subsection 183(1) or (1.1) or a judge of that court, and includes a registrar when exercising the powers of the court conferred on a registrar under this Act;√¢¬Ä¬Ç(tribunal)

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 9:
A person engages in reviewable conduct if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: not being a licensed trustee, the person uses the title √¢¬Ä¬útrustee√¢¬Ä¬ù or √¢¬Ä¬úlicensed trustee√¢¬Ä¬ù or a variation or abbreviation of either of those titles, or any words, name or designation, in a manner that leads to a reasonable belief that the person holds a licence as a trustee;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: not being a licensed trustee, the person does any act of, or represents itself to be, a licensed trustee;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the person directly or indirectly solicits or canvasses any person to make an assignment or a proposal under this Act, or to file an application for a bankruptcy order; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the person makes a representation to the public relating to bankruptcy or insolvency that is false or misleading in a material respect.

Section.Text: In section 9.3, court means a court set out in subsection 183(1) or (1.1) or the Federal Court.

Subsection.Text: If, on application by the Superintendent, a court determines that a person is engaging in or has engaged in reviewable conduct referred to in section 9.1, the court may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: order the person not to engage in the conduct or substantially similar reviewable conduct;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: order the person to publish or otherwise disseminate a notice, in such manner and at such times as the court may specify, to bring to the attention of the class of persons likely to have been reached or affected by the conduct the name under which the person carries on business and the determination made under this section, including

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a description of the conduct,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the time period and geographical area to which the conduct relates, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: a description of the manner in which any representation or advertisement was disseminated, including, where applicable, the name of the publication or other medium employed;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: order the person to make restitution to any person affected by the reviewable conduct or the trustee of the bankrupt of any amount of money that the person or estate has been deprived of as a result of the reviewable conduct, including the amounts paid for any product or service related to the reviewable conduct; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: grant any other relief that the court considers appropriate.

MarginalNote.Ins: Certain matters need not be established

Subsection.Text: For greater certainty, in proceedings under subsection (1), it is not necessary to establish that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any person was deceived or misled;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any member of the public to whom the representation was made was in Canada; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the representation was made in a place to which the public had access.

Subsection.Text: In proceedings under subsection (1), the general impression conveyed by a representation as well as its literal meaning shall be taken into account in determining whether or not the person that made the representation engaged in the reviewable conduct.
Paragraph 202(1)(a) of the Act is repealed.
Paragraph 202(1)(f) of the Act is repealed.
Section 202 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):
Every person that, not being a licensed trustee, does any act as, or represents itself to be, a licensed trustee is guilty of an offence punishable on summary conviction and is liable

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a person other than an individual, to a fine not exceeding $1,000,000; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an individual, to a fine not exceeding $100,000 or to imprisonment for a term not exceeding one year, or to both.

Subsection.Text: Every person that directly or indirectly solicits or canvasses any person to make an assignment or a proposal under this Act or to file an application for a bankruptcy order is guilty of an offence punishable on summary conviction and is liable

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a person other than an individual, to a fine not exceeding $1,000,000; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an individual, to a fine not exceeding $100,000 or to imprisonment for a term not exceeding one year, or to both.

Section.Text: The Canada Labour Code is amended by adding the following after section 237:

Heading.Label: DIVISION XI.1
Heading.TitleText: Non-Compete Clauses and Other Employment-Related Restrictions

Section.Text: The following definitions apply in this Division.

Definition.Text: non-compete clause√¢¬Ä¬Çmeans a term or condition of employment, or a clause in an agreement, that prohibits an employee from engaging in any business, work, occupation or trade, profession, project or other activity that is in competition with the employer√¢¬Ä¬ôs federal work, undertaking or business after the employee ceases to be employed.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(clause de non-concurrence)

other employment-related restriction√¢¬Ä¬Çmeans a term or condition of employment, or a clause in an agreement, that is not a non-compete clause and is part of a class specified in the regulations.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(autre restriction li√É¬©e √É¬Ý l√¢¬Ä¬ôemploi)

Subsection.Text: It is prohibited for an employer to agree to a non-compete clause or other employment-related restriction with an employee or trade union or impose one on an employee, including by inducing the employee to agree to one.
A non-compete clause or other employment-related restriction is void or, in Quebec, null.
Subject to the regulations, subsections (1) and (2) do not apply in respect of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a person who leases or transfers, by sale, merger or otherwise, their work, undertaking or business, or any part of it, to an employer and, immediately following the lease or transfer, becomes an employee of that employer, if the person agrees to a non-compete clause or other employment-related restriction as part of the lease or transfer and if the work, undertaking or business

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: is a federal work, undertaking or business, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: becomes a federal work, undertaking or business due to the lease or transfer;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a person who holds the position or performs the functions of chief executive officer;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: an employee who reports directly to the person holding the position or performing the functions of chief executive officer and is the only employee who holds the position or performs the functions of president, chief operating officer, chief financial officer, chief human resources officer, chief information officer, chief technology officer or chief legal officer, unless, despite holding that position or performing its functions, the employee is not a manager within the meaning of subsection 167(3); or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: an employee who reports directly to the person holding the position or performing the functions of chief executive officer and holds or performs the functions of a position, or a position that is part of a class of positions, that is specified in the regulations.

Section.Text: It is prohibited for an employer to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: dismiss, suspend, lay off, demote or discipline an employee because the employee has refused to agree to or comply with a non-compete clause or other employment-related restriction; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: take into account an employee√¢¬Ä¬ôs refusal to agree to a non-compete clause or other employment-related restriction in any decision to promote or train that employee, unless the promotion is to a position subject to the exemption under paragraph 237.2(3)(b), (c) or (d) or the training is a requirement for such a promotion.

Section.Text: If, in any proceeding under this Part other than a prosecution, or in any proceedings under Part IV in respect of a violation that is related to this Division, an employer alleges that a term or condition of employment or a clause of an agreement is not a non-compete clause or other employment-related restriction or that a non-compete clause or other employment-related restriction is not void or, in Quebec, not null, the burden of proof is on the employer.
The Governor in Council may make regulations

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: specifying, for the purposes of the definition other employment-related restriction in section 237.1, classes of terms or conditions of employment, or classes of clauses of agreements, that, in the Governor in Council√¢¬Ä¬ôs opinion, unreasonably restrict the ability of employees to engage in any business, work, occupation or trade, profession, project or other activity;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: defining any term for the purposes of this Division;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: imposing terms and conditions for the purposes of subsection 237.2(3); or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: specifying, for the purposes of paragraph 237.2(3)(d), positions, or classes of positions, if, in the Governor in Council√¢¬Ä¬ôs opinion, the negative impact on employers of the positions, or classes of positions, not being specified outweighs the negative impact of the positions, or classes of positions, being specified on the employees who hold or perform the functions of those positions or the positions of those classes.

Section.Text: Paragraph 246.1(1)(a.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: the employer has taken action against the employee in contravention of paragraph 210.5(a) or (b) or 237.3(a) or (b);

Section.Text: Subsection 237.2(2) of the Canada Labour Code does not apply until the first anniversary of the day on which subsection 237.2(1) of that Act comes into force in respect of a non-compete clause or other employment-related restriction, as those terms are defined in section 237.1 of that Act, that is in effect on the day on which that subsection 237.2(1) comes into force, including a non-compete clause or other employment-related restriction that is agreed to by an employer whose work, undertaking or business becomes a federal work, undertaking or business, as defined in section 2 of that Act, during the period beginning on the day on which that subsection 237.2(1) comes into force and ending on the first anniversary of that day.
This Division comes into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
Subsections 26(1) and (2) of the Canadian Human Rights Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A commission is established to be known as the Canadian Human Rights Commission, in this Act referred to as the √¢¬Ä¬úCommission√¢¬Ä¬ù, consisting of a Chief Commissioner, a member referred to as the √¢¬Ä¬úAccessibility Commissioner√¢¬Ä¬ù, a member referred to as the √¢¬Ä¬úPay Equity Commissioner√¢¬Ä¬ù and not less than three and not more than six other members, to be appointed by the Governor in Council.

The Chief Commissioner, the Accessibility Commissioner and the Pay Equity Commissioner are full-time members of the Commission and the other members may be appointed as full-time or part-time members.

Section.Text: Subsections 31(2) and (3) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Absence or incapacity of Chief Commissioner
Subsection.Text: If the Chief Commissioner is absent or incapacitated or the office of the Chief Commissioner is vacant, the full-time member, other than the Accessibility Commissioner, with the most seniority has all the powers and may perform all the duties and functions of the Chief Commissioner.

Section.Text: The person occupying the position of Deputy Chief Commissioner of the Canadian Human Rights Commission immediately before the day on which this section comes into force is deemed, on that day, to have been appointed as Chief Commissioner under section 26 of the Canadian Human Rights Act for a term equal to the remainder of that person√¢¬Ä¬ôs term as Deputy Chief Commissioner.
Section 3 of the International Development Research Centre Act is replaced by the following:

A corporation is established, to be called the International Development Research Centre, consisting of a Board of Governors that is composed of a Chairperson, the President and not more than 10 other governors.
Subsections 10(1) and (2) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Chairperson, the Vice-chairperson and at least half of the other governors must be Canadian citizens.
At least six of the governors must have experience in the field of international development or experience or training in the natural or social sciences or technology.

Section.Text: Subsection 16(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Six governors, at least four of whom are Canadian citizens, constitute a quorum of the Board. However, the proceedings of any meeting of the Board that is attended by more than six governors are not valid unless a majority of those governors are Canadian citizens.

Section.Text: Section 60.1 of the Tobacco and Vaping Products Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Minister must, within the five-year period beginning on July 1, 2026 and then within every five-year period beginning on the day after the day on which a report referred to in subsection (2) has been tabled in both Houses of Parliament, undertake a review of the provisions and operation of this Act.

The Minister must cause a report on the review to be tabled in each House of Parliament within the six-month period beginning on the day after the day on which the applicable five-year period ends or, if either House is not sitting on the last day of that six-month period, on any of the next 30 days during which that House is sitting.

Section.Text: Subsection 16(2) of the Pest Control Products Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Minister shall, no later than 15 years after the later of the following days, initiate an assessment to determine, on the basis of available information, whether the health or environmental risks posed by a registered pest control product have increased significantly since that day:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the day on which the most recent decision statement in respect of a decision of a type referred to in paragraph 28(1)(a) or (b) in relation to that product was made public, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the day on which the most recent assessment of that product initiated under this subsection was completed.

Subsection.Text: Without limiting the generality of subsection (1), the Minister shall initiate a re-evaluation of the registered pest control product if, after carrying out the assessment, the Minister has reasonable grounds to believe that the health or environmental risks of that product have increased significantly.

If a special review has been initiated in relation to a registered pest control product for which an assessment is required under subsection (2), the Minister may delay the assessment until the decision statement respecting that special review is made public under subsection 28(5).

If a re-evaluation is initiated following an assessment that was delayed under subsection (2.2), the Minister may narrow the scope of the re-evaluation to exclude any aspect of the registered pest control product that prompted the special review.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 17.1:

The Minister may terminate a special review that has been initiated in relation to a registered pest control product at any time before a decision statement is made public under subsection 28(5) respecting the special review if the Minister initiates a re-evaluation of that product under subsection 16(1) or (2.1) and the re-evaluation includes the aspect of the product that prompted the special review.
Section 17.2 of the Act is replaced by the following:
The Minister shall make public each of the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a determination made under subsection 16(2.1) that, after carrying out an assessment, the Minister has reasonable grounds to believe that the health or environmental risks of a registered pest control product have increased significantly and the reasons for that determination;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a decision made under subsection 16(2.2) to delay an assessment and the reasons for that decision;

a decision made under subsection 17(7) to expand the scope of a re-evaluation or special review to include an aspect that would otherwise prompt a new special review under subsection 17(2) and the reasons for that decision;

a decision made under subsection 17.1(1) or (2) not to initiate a special review in relation to an aspect that would otherwise prompt such a review under subsection 17(2) and the reasons for that decision; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: a decision made under section 17.11 to terminate a special review and the reasons for that decision.

Subsection.Text: In this section, Act means the Pest Control Products Act.
Unless the context otherwise requires, words and expressions used in this section have the same meaning as in the Act.

Subject to subsection (4), if a re-evaluation was initiated under subsection 16(2) of the Act, as it read immediately before the day on which this Division comes into force, and is not complete on that day, the re-evaluation continues in accordance with the Act, as amended on that day.

If a registered pest control product is under a re-evaluation that is continued under subsection (3), the Minister may, at any time, initiate an assessment of that product to determine, on the basis of available information, whether the health or environmental risks posed by that product have increased significantly since the day on which the most recent decision statement in respect of a decision of a type referred to in paragraph 28(1)(a) or (b) of the Act in relation to that product was made public. If, after carrying out the assessment, the Minister does not have reasonable grounds to believe that the health or environmental risks of that product have increased significantly since that day, then the re-evaluation is terminated.
An assessment initiated under subsection (4) is deemed to be an assessment initiated under subsection 16(2) of the Act.

Section.Text: The Territorial Lands Act is amended by adding the following after section 12:

Subsection.Text: If the Governor in Council is of the opinion that it is in the national interest, the Governor in Council may, by order, on the recommendation of the Minister, take any of the following measures with respect to territorial lands in Nunavut:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: cancel a licence, the recording of a claim or a lease of a recorded claim;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: provide, for any period that the Governor in Council may specify, that a claim that includes the lands that are specified in the order is not to be recorded;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: provide that a lease of a recorded claim is not to be issued;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: provide that a licence or a lease of a recorded claim is not to be renewed;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: provide that the transfer of a recorded claim, a lease of a recorded claim or an interest in either of them is not to be recorded;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: prohibit, for any period that the Governor in Council may specify,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a former licencee whose licence has been cancelled under paragraph (a) and any person related to them from making an application for a licence,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a former claim holder in respect of whose claim the recording has been cancelled under paragraph (a) and any person related to them from making an application to record a claim that includes any unit that was included in the claim in respect of which the recording has been cancelled or from acquiring a legal or beneficial interest in respect of that claim,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: a former lessee whose lease of a recorded claim has been cancelled under paragraph (a) and any person related to them from making an application to obtain a lease of a recorded claim that includes any unit that was included in the claim in respect of which the lease has been cancelled,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: a holder of a recorded claim in respect of which a lease is not to be issued under paragraph (c) and any person related to them from making an application to obtain a lease of a recorded claim that includes any unit that was included in the claim in respect of which a lease is not to be issued,

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: a licencee whose licence is not to be renewed under paragraph (d) and any person related to them from making an application for a licence or an application to renew the licence,

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: a lessee of a recorded claim whose lease is not to be renewed under paragraph (d) and any person related to them from making an application to renew the lease or an application to obtain a lease of a recorded claim that includes any unit that was included in the claim in respect of which the lease is not to be renewed,

Subparagraph.Label: (vii)
Subparagraph.Text: a holder of a recorded claim √¢¬Ä¬î or a holder of an interest in a recorded claim √¢¬Ä¬î for which the transfer is not to be recorded under paragraph (e) and any person related to them from making an application for the transfer of a recorded claim √¢¬Ä¬î or of an interest in a recorded claim √¢¬Ä¬î that includes any unit that was included in the claim in respect of which the transfer is not to be recorded or in respect of which the transfer of an interest is not to be recorded, or

Subparagraph.Label: (viii)
Subparagraph.Text: a lessee of a recorded claim √¢¬Ä¬î or a holder of an interest in a lease of a recorded claim √¢¬Ä¬î for which the transfer is not to be recorded under paragraph (e) and any person related to them from making an application for the transfer of a lease √¢¬Ä¬î or of an interest in a lease √¢¬Ä¬î of a recorded claim that includes any unit that was included in the claim in respect of which the transfer of the lease or the interest in it is not to be recorded; or

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: provide that the lands that were covered by a claim the recording of which has been cancelled under paragraph (a) are not to be opened for prospecting for any period that the Governor in Council may specify.

Subsection.Text: The Minister shall, as soon as feasible after an order is made under subsection (1), give notice of the order to the holder of the right or interest that is the subject of the order.
The Minister shall, within 180 days after the day on which notice is given under subsection (2), determine whether compensation is to be paid and, if so, the amount that is to be paid to a

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: former claim holder in respect of whose claim the recording has been cancelled under paragraph (1)(a);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: former lessee whose lease of a recorded claim has been cancelled under paragraph (1)(a);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: holder of a recorded claim in respect of which a lease is not to be issued under paragraph (1)(c); or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: lessee of a recorded claim whose lease is not to be renewed under paragraph (1)(d).

Subsection.Text: The Minister may extend the 180-day period set out in subsection (3) by up to 180 days.

The Minister shall, as soon as feasible after making a determination under subsection (3), give notice to the former claim holder, former lessee, holder of a recorded claim or lessee referred to in that subsection of whether compensation is to be paid to them and, if so, the amount of the compensation.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Address or email address for notice
Subsection.Text: For the purposes of subsections (2) and (5), the Minister shall send the notice to the last known address or email address that the holder of the right or interest referred to in subsection (2) or the former claim holder, former lessee, holder of a recorded claim or lessee referred to in subsection (3) has provided

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: under section 3 of the Nunavut Mining Regulations; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in writing to the Minister.

Subsection.Text: A person shall not have any right to claim or receive any compensation, damages, indemnity or other form of relief from His Majesty in right of Canada or from any servant or agent of His Majesty in right of Canada for any acquired, vested, future or potential right, interest or entitlement that is affected by an order made under subsection (1), other than any compensation that is determined by the Minister under subsection (3).
The Statutory Instruments Act does not apply to an order made under subsection (1), but the order shall be published in the Canada Gazette within 23 days after the order is made.
The Governor in Council may make regulations respecting

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the implementation of orders made under subsection (1); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the compensation referred to in subsection (3).

Subsection.Text: Unless the context otherwise requires, words and expressions used in this section have the same meaning as in the Nunavut Mining Regulations.

Section.Text: The definition excluded Act in section 11 of the Red Tape Reduction Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: excluded Act√¢¬Ä¬Çmeans the Access to Information Act, the Auditor General Act, the Canada Elections Act, the Conflict of Interest Act, the Criminal Code, the Export and Import Permits Act, the Financial Administration Act, the Foreign Influence Transparency and Accountability Act, the Investment Canada Act, the Lobbying Act, the Nuclear Safety and Control Act, the Official Languages Act, the Privacy Act, the Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering) and Terrorist Financing Act or the Public Servants Disclosure Protection Act.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(loi exclue)

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 12(1)(c) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a provision of an Act of Parliament, except an excluded Act, or a provision of an instrument made under an Act of Parliament, except an instrument made under an excluded Act, if the minister administers or enforces the provision.

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 12(3)(a)(ii) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: qu√¢¬Ä¬ôelle permettrait de mettre √É¬Ý l√¢¬Ä¬ôessai, entre autres, un produit, un service, un proc√É¬©d√É¬©, une proc√É¬©dure ou une mesure r√É¬©glementaire dans le but de faciliter la conception, la modification ou l√¢¬Ä¬ôadministration d√¢¬Ä¬ôun r√É¬©gime r√É¬©glementaire et d√¢¬Ä¬ôainsi stimuler l√¢¬Ä¬ôinnovation, la comp√É¬©titivit√É¬© ou la croissance √É¬©conomique dans le secteur des technologies propres ou des technologies financi√É¬®res,

Section.Text: Subsection 14.2(2) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Le rapport fait √É¬©tat de l√¢¬Ä¬ôarr√É¬™t√É¬© et des renseignements vis√É¬©s √É¬Ý l√¢¬Ä¬ôalin√É¬©a 14(1)a) ainsi que d√¢¬Ä¬ôune √É¬©valuation de toute disposition d√¢¬Ä¬ôune loi f√É¬©d√É¬©rale ou d√¢¬Ä¬ôun texte pris en vertu d√¢¬Ä¬ôune loi f√É¬©d√É¬©rale qui pourrait √É¬™tre modifi√É¬©e ou abrog√É¬©e en vue de stimuler l√¢¬Ä¬ôinnovation, la comp√É¬©titivit√É¬© ou la croissance √É¬©conomique dans le secteur des technologies propres ou des technologies financi√É¬®res.

Heading.Label: DIVISION 16
Heading.TitleText: Defence Investment Agency Act and Defence Production Act

Section.Text: The Defence Investment Agency Act is enacted as follows:

BillInternal.LongTitle: An Act to establish the Defence Investment Agency

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the Defence Investment Agency Act.
The following definitions apply in this Act.

Definition.Text: Agency√¢¬Ä¬Çmeans the Defence Investment Agency established under section 4.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(Agence)
Minister√¢¬Ä¬Çmeans the member of the King√¢¬Ä¬ôs Privy Council for Canada designated under section 3.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(ministre)

Section.Text: The Governor in Council may, by order, designate a member of the King√¢¬Ä¬ôs Privy Council for Canada as the Minister for the purposes of this Act.
The Defence Investment Agency is established.

The mandate of the Agency is to assist the Minister in the exercise or performance of the Minister√¢¬Ä¬ôs powers, duties and functions under this Act or the Defence and National Security Production and Procurement Act.
The Minister presides over the Agency and has the management and direction of it.

Subsection.Text: The Chief Executive Officer of the Agency is to be appointed by the Governor in Council to hold office during pleasure for a renewable term of up to five years.
The Chief Executive Officer has the rank and status of a deputy head of a department.
The Chief Executive Officer is to be paid the remuneration fixed by the Governor in Council.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Absence or incapacity of Chief Executive Officer
Subsection.Text: In the event of the absence or incapacity of the Chief Executive Officer, or a vacancy in that office, the Minister may authorize any person to act as Chief Executive Officer, but no person so authorized has authority to act for a term of more than 90 days without the Governor in Council√¢¬Ä¬ôs approval.

The Chief Executive Officer may engage on a temporary basis the services of persons having technical or specialized knowledge of any matter relating to the Chief Executive Officer√¢¬Ä¬ôs work to advise and assist the Chief Executive Officer in the exercise of their powers and performance of their duties and functions and, with the approval of the Treasury Board, may fix and pay the remuneration and expenses of those persons.

Section.Text: The officers and employees necessary for the proper conduct of the work of the Agency are to be appointed in accordance with the Public Service Employment Act.
A department, board or agency of the Government of Canada may provide services and facilities to the Agency.
The Agency may provide services and facilities to a department, board or agency of the Government of Canada.

Subsection.Text: The powers, duties and functions of the Minister extend to and include all matters over which Parliament has jurisdiction, not by law assigned to any other department, board or agency of the Government of Canada, relating to production, procurement and investment in respect of national defence or national security.

Without restricting the generality of subsection (1), the Minister may initiate, recommend, implement and promote policies and programs relating to production, procurement and investment in respect of national defence or national security.

Section.Text: In exercising or performing the powers, duties and functions relating to production, procurement and investment in respect of national defence or national security, the Minister must consider

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the efficiency of national defence or national security procurement;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the stimulation of investment in the national defence sector or national security sector;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the stimulation and promotion of research, development and innovation in the national defence sector or national security sector;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the possibility of joint procurement and other forms of cooperation with associated governments, as defined in section 2 of the Defence and National Security Production and Procurement Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the protection and acquisition of intellectual property rights.

Subsection.Text: The Minister may, on behalf of the Government of Canada, enter into contracts for the purposes of exercising or performing the Minister√¢¬Ä¬ôs powers, duties or functions.
The Minister may fix terms of contracts and give instructions and fix terms in respect of other documents relating to those contracts and their formation.
The terms and instructions may be identified by number or other designation and may be incorporated in a contract or other document by reference to their number or other designation.
The Minister may, by regulation, prescribe the electronic or other means by which a term or instruction, including its identification number or other designation, must be published.

Subject to its express terms, a contract, or any document relating to the contract or its formation, entered into by the Minister that provides that instructions or terms identified by number or other designation are applicable to or form part of the contract or other document is to be read and construed as if the instructions or terms that are referred to were expressly set out in the contract or other document.

The Minister may, subject to any terms that the Minister may specify, delegate any of the Minister√¢¬Ä¬ôs powers, duties or functions to an appropriate minister, other than a power exercised or duty or function performed under

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 11(2) or section 15; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 15.1(1), 17(1) or 26(2) of the Defence and National Security Production and Procurement Act.

Subsection.Text: For greater certainty, nothing in subsection (1) is to be construed as preventing an appropriate minister from doing the activities described in subsection 15.1(1) of the Defence and National Security Production and Procurement Act under any other Act of Parliament or any other law.

The Minister may, with respect to a department over which the Minister does not preside but for which the Minister is the appropriate minister, delegate any of the Minister√¢¬Ä¬ôs powers, duties or functions other than those referred to in paragraph (1)(a) or (b) to the chief executive of that department.

The appropriate minister for a department may, subject to and in accordance with the delegation under subsection (1), subdelegate to the chief executive of that department any of the powers, duties or functions that were delegated to the appropriate minister under that subsection.

The chief executive of a department may, subject to and in accordance with the delegation under subsection (3) or the subdelegation under subsection (4), subdelegate to any person under their jurisdiction any of the powers, duties or functions that were delegated or subdelegated to them under subsection (3) or (4).
The following definitions apply in this section.

Definition.Text: appropriate minister√¢¬Ä¬Çhas the same meaning as in section 2 of the Financial Administration Act.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(ministre comp√É¬©tent)
chief executive√¢¬Ä¬Çmeans

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: with respect to a department named in Part I of Schedule VI to the Financial Administration Act, its deputy minister;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: with respect to a department named in Part II or III of that Schedule, the person occupying the position set out opposite that name; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: with respect to a department that is not named in that Schedule, the chief executive officer, deputy head or the person who occupies any other similar position in that department.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(administrateur principal)

Subsection.Text: The Minister may appoint advisers and establish advisory committees to advise and aid the Minister in exercising and performing the powers, duties and functions relating to production, procurement and investment in respect of national defence or national security, and may provide for their membership, duties, functions and operation.
The Minister may fix the remuneration that advisers and members of an advisory committee are to be paid for the performance of their duties and functions.
Advisers and members of an advisory committee are to be reimbursed for the travel, living and other expenses incurred in the performance of their duties and functions.

Section.Text: The Governor in Council may make regulations for the purpose of carrying out the purposes and provisions of this Act.
The following definitions apply in sections 292 to 295.

Definition.Text: former agency√¢¬Ä¬Çmeans the portion of the federal public administration, within the Department of Public Works and Government Services, known as the Defence Investment Agency.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(ancienne agence)
new agency√¢¬Ä¬Çmeans the Defence Investment Agency established under section 4 of the Defence Investment Agency Act.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(nouvelle agence)

Subsection.Text: Any contract, agreement or other arrangement respecting the acquisition of defence supplies or the construction of defence projects entered into by the Minister of Public Works and Government Services are deemed to have been entered into by the Minister designated under section 3 of the Defence Investment Agency Act.

Any power, duty or function that is exercisable by an officer or employee of the former agency under any Act, order, rule or regulation or under any contract, lease, licence or other document is to be exercised by the appropriate officer or employee of the new agency.

Section.Text: The person who occupies the position of Chief Executive Officer of the former agency immediately before the day on which the Defence Investment Agency Act comes into force continues in office and is deemed to have been appointed as the Chief Executive Officer of the new Agency under subsection 7(1) of that Act for the remainder of the term for which they had been appointed.

Nothing in the Defence Investment Agency Act is to be construed as affecting the status of an employee who, immediately before the day on which that Act comes into force, occupied a position in the former agency, except that the employee, beginning on that day, occupies that position in the new agency.

Any amount that is appropriated, for the fiscal year in which the Defence Investment Agency Act comes into force, by an appropriation Act based on the Estimates for that year for defraying the federal public administration√¢¬Ä¬ôs charges and expenses for the former agency and that is unexpended on the day on which that Act comes into force is deemed, on that day, to be an amount appropriated for defraying the federal public administration√¢¬Ä¬ôs charges and expenses for the new agency.
Schedule I to the Access to Information Act is amended by adding the following, in alphabetical order, under the heading √¢¬Ä¬úOther Government Institutions√¢¬Ä¬ù:

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Agence de l√¢¬Ä¬ôinvestissement pour la d√É¬©fense

Section.Text: Schedule I.1 to the Financial Administration Act is amended by adding, in alphabetical order in column I, a reference to

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Agence de l√¢¬Ä¬ôinvestissement pour la d√É¬©fense

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: and a corresponding reference in column II to √¢¬Ä¬úMember of the King√¢¬Ä¬ôs Privy Council for Canada designated as the Minister for the purposes of the Defence Investment Agency Act√¢¬Ä¬ù.

Section.Text: Schedule IV to the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Agence de l√¢¬Ä¬ôinvestissement pour la d√É¬©fense

Section.Text: Part II Schedule VI to the Act is amended by adding, in alphabetical order in column 1, a reference to

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Agence de l√¢¬Ä¬ôinvestissement pour la d√É¬©fense

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: and a corresponding reference in column II to √¢¬Ä¬úChief Executive Officer√¢¬Ä¬ù.

Section.Text: The schedule to the Privacy Act is amended by adding the following, in alphabetical order, under the heading √¢¬Ä¬úOther Government Institutions√¢¬Ä¬ù:

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Agence de l√¢¬Ä¬ôinvestissement pour la d√É¬©fense

Section.Text: Part I of Schedule I to the Public Service Superannuation Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Agence de l√¢¬Ä¬ôinvestissement pour la d√É¬©fense

Section.Text: Schedule I to the Public Sector Compensation Act is amended by adding the following, in alphabetical order, under the heading √¢¬Ä¬úOther Portions of the Public Service√¢¬Ä¬ù:

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Agence de l√¢¬Ä¬ôinvestissement pour la d√É¬©fense

Section.Text: The reference to √¢¬Ä¬úMinister of Public Works and Government Services√¢¬Ä¬ù set out in the Description of Goods of tariff item No. 9982.00.00 in the List of Tariff Provisions set out in the schedule to the Customs Tariff is replaced by the following:

Provision.Text: Minister designated under section 3 of the Defence Investment Agency Act or the Minister of National Defence

Section.Text: The long title of the Defence Production Act is replaced by the following:

AmendedText.LongTitle: An Act respecting production and procurement in relation to defence and national security and respecting the control of certain goods

Section.Text: Section 1 of the Act is replaced by the following:
This Act may be cited as the Defence and National Security Production and Procurement Act.
The heading before section 2 of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The definition sale in section 2 of the Act is repealed.
The definitions associated government, defence projects and government issue in section 2 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: associated government√¢¬Ä¬Çmeans

Paragraph.Text: His Majesty√¢¬Ä¬ôs Government in the United Kingdom or any other government of the Commonwealth,
the government of a country that is a member of the North Atlantic Treaty Organisation,
the government of a country, or an international organization of states, that is recognized by the North Atlantic Treaty Organisation as a partner in good standing of that Organisation,

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the European Union or the government of any of its member states, and
the government of any other country designated by the Governor in Council as being a country the defence of which is vital to the defence of Canada;√¢¬Ä¬Ç(gouvernement associ√É¬©)

Definition.Text: defence projects√¢¬Ä¬Çmeans

Paragraph.Text: military works, including buildings, aerodromes, airports, dockyards, roads and defence fortifications,
works required for the production, maintenance or storage of defence supplies,

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: works in relation to residential communities, including non-military residential communities, on federal real property or federal immovables under the administration of the Minister of National Defence, or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: works required for the purposes of national security;√¢¬Ä¬Ç(ouvrages de d√É¬©fense)

Definition.Text: government issue√¢¬Ä¬Çmeans machinery, machine tools, equipment or defence supplies that are furnished by the Minister or by an agent of His Majesty on behalf of His Majesty or of an associated government, or that are acquired on behalf of His Majesty or of an associated government with funds provided by the Minister, an agent of His Majesty or an associated government;√¢¬Ä¬Ç(fournitures d√¢¬Ä¬ô√É¬âtat)

Subsection.Text: The definition Minister in section 2 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: Minister√¢¬Ä¬Çmeans

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in relation to Part 1, the Minister designated under section 3 of the Defence Investment Agency Act, and
in relation to Part 2, the Minister of Public Works and Government Services;√¢¬Ä¬Ç(ministre)

Subsection.Text: The definition defence contract in section 2 of the Act is amended by striking out √¢¬Ä¬úand√¢¬Ä¬ù at the end of paragraph (a) and by adding the following after that paragraph:

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: a contract with His Majesty or an agent of His Majesty, or with an associated government, that in any way relates to defence services, and

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (a) of the definition defence subcontract in section 2 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: to perform all or any part of the work or service or make or furnish anything for the purpose of any other defence contract,

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (c) of the definition defence subcontract in section 2 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: under which any part of the services performed or to be performed consists of soliciting, attempting to negotiate or negotiating any other defence contract or soliciting or negotiating for the acquisition or disposition of anything, or for the acquisition or provision of any services, required to fulfill any other defence contract,

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs (a) to (c) of the definition defence supplies in section 2 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: anything √¢¬Ä¬î including any physical thing, animal, data or intangible or incorporeal property √¢¬Ä¬î that is required or used for the purposes of national defence or national security or for cooperative efforts for defence or security being carried on by the Government of Canada and an associated government,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a vessel of any kind, whether floating or submersible, other than a pleasure craft, and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: anything used for the production or supply of anything referred to in paragraph (a) or (b) or for the construction of defence projects;√¢¬Ä¬Ç(mat√É¬©riel de d√É¬©fense)

Subsection.Text: Section 2 of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: acquire√¢¬Ä¬Çincludes to lease;√¢¬Ä¬Ç(acqu√É¬©rir)

defence services√¢¬Ä¬Çmeans services that are required or provided for the purposes of national defence or national security, for cooperative efforts for defence or security being carried on by the Government of Canada and an associated government or for the construction of defence projects, but does not include legal services and services provided by an individual in their capacity as an officer or employee of His Majesty or as a member of the Canadian Forces;√¢¬Ä¬Ç(services de d√É¬©fense)
dispose√¢¬Ä¬Çincludes to consign and to lease;√¢¬Ä¬Ç(disposer)

Section.Text: The headings before section 3 and sections 3 to 9 of the Act are replaced by the following:

For greater certainty, works, things and services are not excluded, respectively, from the definitions defence projects, defence supplies or defence services in section 2 merely because the works, things or services are required, used or provided for an additional purpose beyond what is set out in the applicable definition.
The powers, duties and functions conferred by or under this Act may be exercised or performed despite anything in the Department of Public Works and Government Services Act.
No information with respect to an individual business that has been obtained under or by virtue of this Act shall be disclosed without the consent of the person carrying on that business, except

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: to a government department, or any person authorized by a government department, requiring the information for the purpose of the discharge of the functions of that department; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for the purposes of any prosecution for an offence under this Act or, with the consent of the Minister, for the purposes of any civil suit or other proceeding at law.

Section.Text: The Canadian Commercial Corporation, or a company to which the Government Corporations Operation Act applies, has capacity and power to make arrangements to act on behalf of the Minister under this Act or to enter into contracts to act as agent of His Majesty under this Act and the making of those arrangements or the entry into those contracts and the carrying out of those arrangements or contracts shall be deemed to be included in the objects and purposes for which the Corporation or the company was incorporated.
The purposes of this Part are to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: provide for the supply of things and services, and for the construction of works, in relation to national defence and national security, including by fostering the capabilities and capacities of persons and industries to supply those things and services; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: regulate procurement in relation to national defence and national security.

Section.Text: Subsections 10(1) and (2) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Minister has the exclusive authority to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: acquire defence supplies and defence services required for the purposes of a department, board or agency of the Government of Canada; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: construct defence projects required for the purposes of any department, board or agency referred to in paragraph (a), except for defence projects to be constructed by individuals in their capacity as officers or employees of His Majesty or as members of the Canadian Forces.

Subsection.Text: The exercise of the powers referred to in subsection (1) is subject to any specification made by the Governor in Council under paragraph 6(a) or (c) of the Shared Services Canada Act.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 10:

Nothing in this Part or in the Defence Investment Agency Act precludes any federal department or agency from invoking a national security exception provided for in an agreement or arrangement relating to domestic or international trade to which the Government of Canada is a party.
Section 11 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Acts on behalf of associated government
Section.Text: The Minister, if authorized by the Governor in Council to do so, may do or undertake, on behalf of an associated government, any act or thing that the Minister is authorized to do or undertake by this Part on behalf of His Majesty.
Section 12 of the Act and the heading before it are replaced by the following:
The Minister shall

Paragraph.Text: examine into, organize, mobilize and conserve the resources of Canada that contribute to defence supplies and their sources of supply;

examine into, organize, mobilize and conserve the workforce, agencies and facilities available for the supply of defence supplies, the provision of defence services and the construction of defence projects;

explore, estimate and provide for the fulfillment of the present and future needs of the Government of Canada and the community with respect to the supply of defence supplies, the provision of defence services and the construction of defence projects; and

generally take steps to mobilize, conserve and coordinate all economic and industrial facilities in respect of defence supplies, defence services and defence projects and their supply, provision or construction.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 13(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:
The Minister may, by notice in writing, require that a person referred to in subsection (2) provide the Minister with any information with respect to

Paragraph.Text: defence supplies produced, dealt in or controlled by the person or that the person holds, has contracted for or contemplates acquiring, as well as the sources of supply for them;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: defence services provided by the person or that the person has contracted to provide or contemplates providing, as well as the sources of supply for them; and

the facilities or accommodation that the person has available for or that are adaptable to the production or storage of defence supplies, the provision of defence services or the construction of defence projects.

Subsection.Text: The person referred to in the notice shall provide the requested information at the times, and in the form and manner, specified in the notice.
The portion of subsection 13(2) of the English version of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
Subsection (1) applies with respect to any person who

Subsection 13(2) of the Act is amended by striking out √¢¬Ä¬úor√¢¬Ä¬ù at the end of paragraph (a), by adding √¢¬Ä¬úor√¢¬Ä¬ù at the end of paragraph (b) and by adding the following after paragraph (b):

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: provides defence services or carries on a business that, in the opinion of the Minister, is suitable for providing defence services.

Section.Text: Section 14 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Other departments to assist Minister in obtaining information
Section.Text: If a government department has, under any Act, the power to obtain, for any purpose, information on matters with respect to which the Minister is empowered to require that information be provided under subsection 13(1), that department shall, if so required by the Minister, exercise that power for the purpose of assisting the Minister in obtaining that information.
Section 15 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subject to this Part, the Minister may, on behalf of His Majesty, acquire, store, maintain, transport or dispose of anything designated by the Governor in Council.
For the purposes of subsection (1), the Governor in Council may designate anything that is essential to the needs of Canada, of a community in or outside of Canada or of an associated government for

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the purposes of national defence, of national security, including economic security, or of the defence or security of an associated government or other state; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the purpose of safeguarding against possible shortages of the thing.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 15:

Subsection.Text: For a purpose related to production, procurement or investment in respect of national defence or national security, or for a purpose related to defence supplies, defence services or a defence project or to subsection 15(1), the Minister may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: make loans to any person;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: make advance payments to any person;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: with the approval of the Minister of Finance, guarantee the repayment of, or provide loan insurance or credit insurance in respect of, any financial obligation undertaken by any person;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: make grants and contributions to any person;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: with the approval of the Minister of Finance, acquire, exercise, assign or dispose of any shares, as defined in paragraph 90(5)(e) of the Financial Administration Act, of a corporation or other financial or similar instrument; and

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: where authorized by the Governor in Council on the recommendation of the Minister with the concurrence of the Minister of Finance, enter into any other financial agreement or arrangement.

Subsection.Text: For greater certainty,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister√¢¬Ä¬ôs powers set out in subsection (1) are in addition to the Minister√¢¬Ä¬ôs other powers under this Part; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: loan insurance or credit insurance provided under paragraph (1)(c) constitutes a guarantee for the purposes of subsection 29(1) of the Financial Administration Act.

Section.Text: The heading before section 16 of the Act is repealed.

Subsection.Text: The portion of section 16 of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: The Minister may, on behalf of His Majesty and subject to this Part,

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 16(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: acquire, utilize, store, transport or dispose of defence supplies;

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 16(c) to (g) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: construct, acquire or dispose of defence projects;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: acquire defence services or professional or commercial services other than defence services;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: acquire or dispose of real or personal property or any interest in real or personal property, or an immovable or a movable or any right in an immovable or a movable, that, in the Minister√¢¬Ä¬ôs opinion, is or is likely to be useful for any purpose mentioned in paragraph (a), (b) or (c); and

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: do all things that, in the Minister√¢¬Ä¬ôs opinion, are incidental to or useful for any of the matters referred to in paragraphs (a) to (e) or that may be authorized by the Governor in Council with respect to the procurement, construction or disposition of defence supplies or defence projects or the procurement of defence services.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 16:
Anything that is acquired or produced, any service that is acquired, and any work that is acquired or constructed, under this Part may be dealt with under this Part for any purpose.
Sections 17 to 19 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Minister may pay out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund any amount payable in respect of the exercise of any power under any of subsection 15(1), paragraphs 15.1(1)(a) and (b) and section 16 or in respect of any other purpose prescribed by regulation.
The aggregate amount paid out under subsection (1) must not at any time exceed $1 billion.
The following amounts, if they are in respect of the same item for which a payment has been made under subsection (1), are to be deducted from the aggregate amount referred to in subsection (2):

Paragraph.Label: (a)
