Paragraph.Text: any amount deposited in the Consolidated Revenue Fund that is received from the disposition of anything under this Part;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any amount deposited in the Consolidated Revenue Fund in repayment of the principal amount of a loan or in repayment of an advance payment;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any amount charged to another appropriation that is authorized by Parliament;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: any amount deposited in the Consolidated Revenue Fund that is received from an associated government or an agent of His Majesty; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: any amount deposited in the Consolidated Revenue Fund in relation to an amount paid out in respect of a purpose prescribed by regulation under paragraph 33(2)(c).

Subsection.Text: The total of the amounts deducted under subsection (3) in respect of any item must not exceed the total payments made under subsection (1) with respect to the same item.

A loss sustained in the acquisition and subsequent disposition of anything, or on account of any loan or advance payment or otherwise, and in respect of which a payment has been made under subsection (1) may be deducted from the aggregate amount referred to in subsection (2) if Parliament authorizes the deduction in an appropriation Act.
The maximum amount set out to in subsection (2) may be amended by an appropriation Act.
In this section, item means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a thing referred to in paragraph (3)(a);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a loan or advance payment referred to in paragraph (3)(b);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the subject matter of an appropriation referred to in paragraph (3)(c);

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the subject matter of an amount received that is referred to in paragraph (3)(d); or

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the subject matter of an amount referred to in paragraph (3)(e).

Section.MarginalNote: Loans from the Consolidated Revenue Fund
Section.Text: Despite subsection 17(1) but subject to any regulations made under paragraph 33(2)(c), the Minister may not make, in respect of the exercise of a power under paragraph 15.1(1)(a), a payment out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund under subsection 17(1) if the exercise of the power is for the purpose of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the construction, acquisition, extension or improvement of capital equipment or works by any person; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: research, development or innovation in relation to defence supplies or defence services.

Section.Text: Paragraph 20(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subject to any provisions in the contract, His Majesty or the associated government in whom the title is vested is entitled at any time to remove or dispose of the government issue or building.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 25:

Subsection.Text: Sections 27 to 29 apply with respect to a defence contract, or a contract to acquire things or services under section 15, that provides for the payment of any money by His Majesty and that is not a lease of real property or immovables.
The Minister has the exclusive authority to determine any question as to whether a contract is one that is described in subsection (1).

Subsection.MarginalNote: Application of regulations √¢¬Ä¬î inconsistency or conflict
Subsection.Text: Nothing in sections 27 to 29 precludes the application of regulations made under section 41 of the Financial Administration Act to contracts described in subsection (1). However, regulations made in respect of sections 27 to 29 prevail √¢¬Ä¬î and, for greater certainty, those sections also prevail √¢¬Ä¬î over regulations made under that section 41 to the extent of any inconsistency or conflict.

Section.Text: Subject to section 28, the Minister shall conduct a competitive procurement process for a contract before entering into it.
The Minister may dispense with conducting a competitive procurement process with respect to a contract if, in the Minister√¢¬Ä¬ôs opinion,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the contract is for the purposes of addressing an urgent operational requirement;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the dispensation is necessary for the purposes of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: conducting military operations,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: constructing, maintaining or operating critical defence projects, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: safeguarding national security;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the dispensation is warranted in order to support a sector of the Canadian economy that is important to national defence or to national security, including economic security;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the contract is for defence supplies or defence services that are interoperable with defence projects, defence supplies or defence services √¢¬Ä¬î or that are interchangeable with defence supplies or defences services √¢¬Ä¬î of the Government of Canada or of an associated government;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the contract is in relation to sensitive technology;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: only one person is capable of performing the contract;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: the purpose of the contract is, for operational reasons, to fulfill an interim requirement for defence supplies or defence services or to ensure defence logistical capabilities on an interim basis;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: the contract is related to a contract referred to in paragraph (g);

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: the contract is for the purpose of research, development or innovation in relation to defence supplies or defence services;

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: the contract is for defence supplies or defence services that were the subject of funding provided by the Government of Canada for research, development or innovation;

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: the contract is to be entered into

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: with a government entity,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: under an agreement or arrangement with a government entity, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: under a procedure of an international organization of states of which Canada is a member;

Paragraph.Label: (l)
Paragraph.Text: any exception, set out in regulations made under section 41 of the Financial Administration Act, to a requirement to conduct a competitive procurement process applies, with any necessary modifications, in the circumstances; or

Paragraph.Label: (m)
Paragraph.Text: any other exception prescribed by regulation applies in the circumstances.

Subsection.Text: The Minister may exclude any person from participating in a competitive procurement process if the Minister has reasonable grounds to believe that the person, any person related to that person or anything proposed to be used in the performance of the contract poses a risk to national defence, national security or public safety.
The Minister is not required to provide a person who has been excluded under subsection (1) with the reasons for the exclusion.

Section.Text: Sections 30 to 32 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If the Minister considers that it is likely to facilitate the carrying out of the purposes and provisions of this Part, the Minister may, with the approval of the Governor in Council, procure the incorporation of any one or more corporations for the purpose of undertaking or carrying out any acts or things that the Minister is authorized to undertake or carry out under this Part.

The Minister may remove any members, directors or officers of a corporation incorporated under this section at any time and may appoint others in their stead or may appoint additional persons as members thereof.
A corporation incorporated under this section is for all purposes an agent of His Majesty and it may exercise its powers only as an agent of His Majesty.

Section.Text: Actions, suits or proceedings in respect of any right or obligation that a corporation incorporated under section 30 acquires or incurs on behalf of His Majesty, whether in its name or in the name of His Majesty, may be brought or taken by or against the corporation in the name of the corporation in any court that would have jurisdiction if the corporation were not an agent of His Majesty.
The accounts of a corporation incorporated under section 30 shall be audited by the Auditor General of Canada.

Subsection.Text: Notwithstanding that a corporation is an agent of His Majesty, the Minister may, on behalf of His Majesty, enter into a contract under this Part with the corporation as if it were not an agent of His Majesty.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Contract with person to act as agent of His Majesty
Subsection.Text: The Minister may, with the approval of the Governor in Council, enter into a contract with a person authorizing that person to act, under the control and direction of the Minister, as an agent of His Majesty, for any of the purposes for which the Minister is authorized to act on behalf of His Majesty under this Part.

Section.Text: Section 33 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may make regulations to carry out the purposes and provisions of this Part, including regulations

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the exercise of the Minister√¢¬Ä¬ôs powers under section 15;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: authorizing the Minister, for the purposes of national defence or national security or if faced with an emergency, to require that any person do anything, under the direction and control of the Minister, that the Minister may do under section 15;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: fixing terms that are deemed to be expressly set out in contracts, or classes of contracts, entered into under this Part or in documents, or classes of documents, relating to such contracts and their formation;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the provision of information in relation to the costing of contracts described in subsection 26(1);

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the procedure for conducting a competitive procurement process under section 27; and

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the meaning of urgent operational requirement, sensitive technology and government entity for the purposes of section 28.

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may, on the recommendation of the Minister with the concurrence of the Minister of Finance, make regulations

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the loans that may be made, the guarantees that may be given and the loan insurance and credit insurance that may be provided under paragraph 15.1(1)(a) or (c);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: specifying the circumstances in which and the manner in which the Minister may exercise the powers set out in paragraph 15.1(1)(e); and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: providing for any other purpose for which an amount may be paid out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund under subsection 17(1).

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may make orders to carry out the purposes and provisions of this Part.

Nothing in paragraph (1)(c) precludes the application of regulations made under section 42 of the Financial Administration Act to contracts entered into under this Part. However, regulations made under paragraph (1)(c) prevail over regulations made under that section 42 to the extent of any inconsistency or conflict.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 33:

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may make regulations establishing an administrative monetary penalties scheme for the purpose of promoting compliance with respect to the regulations made under paragraph 33(1)(b) or (d), including regulations

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: respecting appeals of any orders or decisions in proceedings relating to the scheme; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in relation to the recovery, as a debt, of unpaid penalties and any additional penalty to be paid in respect of those unpaid penalties.

Subsection.Text: The regulations shall provide a right to request a review by the Minister of any acts or omissions that constitute an alleged violation or the amount of a penalty.
The maximum penalty in respect of a violation is $2,000,000.
If an act or omission can be proceeded with as a violation under a regulation made under subsection (1) or as an offence under this Act, proceeding in one manner precludes proceeding in the other.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 36:

Section.MarginalNote: Minister√¢¬Ä¬ôs functions dischargeable by persons deputed by Minister
Section.Text: The Minister may authorize any person, on behalf and under the control and direction of the Minister, to do any act or thing or to exercise any power that the Minister may do or exercise under this Part.

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may appoint advisers and establish advisory committees or other boards to advise and aid the Minister in exercising the powers and performing the duties and functions under this Part and may provide for their membership, duties, functions and operation.
The Governor in Council may fix the remuneration of persons appointed under this section for the performance of their duties and functions.

The Minister may determine whether advisers, members of a committee or of a board are to be reimbursed for the travel, living and other expenses incurred in the performance of their duties and functions while absent from their ordinary place of residence.

Section.Text: Subsection 45(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Every person who contravenes any other provision of this Act, with the exception of section 27, or any provision of the regulations is guilty of an offence punishable on summary conviction and liable to a fine of not more than $25,000 or to imprisonment for a term of not more than 12 months, or to both.

Section.Text: The schedule to the Act is amended by replacing every reference to √¢¬Ä¬úDefence Production Act√¢¬Ä¬ù with a reference to √¢¬Ä¬úDefence and National Security Production and Procurement Act√¢¬Ä¬ù.
The following definitions apply in sections 330 to 332.

Definition.Text: Act√¢¬Ä¬Çmeans the Defence and National Security Production and Procurement Act.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(Loi)
commencement day√¢¬Ä¬Çmeans the day on which section 323 comes into force.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(date de r√É¬©f√É¬©rence)
designated Minister√¢¬Ä¬Çmeans the Minister designated under section 3 of the Defence Investment Agency Act.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(ministre d√É¬©sign√É¬©)

Section.MarginalNote: Powers, duties and functions √¢¬Ä¬î sections 30 and 32.1
Section.Text: For greater certainty, the designated Minister is, as of the commencement day, the Minister who may exercise or perform any powers, duties and functions under sections 30 and 32.1 of the Act, as it reads on that day.
The incorporation of Defence Construction (1951) Limited is deemed to have been procured under section 30 of the Act, as it reads on the commencement day.

Any contract entered into with Defence Construction (1951) Limited by the Minister of Public Works and Government Service prior to the commencement day is deemed to have been entered into by the designated Minister.

Heading.TitleText: Consequential Amendments to the Access to Information Act

Section.Text: Schedule II to the Access to Information Act is amended by striking out the reference to

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Loi sur la production de d√É¬©fence

Section.Text: Schedule II to the Act is amended by adding, in alphabetical order, a reference to

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemEn: Defence and National Security Production and Procurement Act
BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Loi sur la production et l√¢¬Ä¬ôapprovisionnement pour la d√É¬©fense et la s√É¬©curit√É¬© nationale

Section.Text: Every reference to √¢¬Ä¬úDefence Production Act√¢¬Ä¬ù is replaced by a reference to √¢¬Ä¬úDefence and National Security Production and Procurement Act√¢¬Ä¬ù in the following provisions:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the Export Permits Regulations,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the definition DPA controlled goods in section 1, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: clauses 3(3)(b)(i)(A) and (B) and subparagraph 3(3)(b)(ii);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the definition Act in section 1 of the Controlled Goods Regulations; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in the Shipment by Post of Certain Prohibited Firearms and Prohibited Devices by Certain Businesses Regulations,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: subsection 2(2),

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: subsection 2.1(2), and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: subsection 2.2(2).

Section.MarginalNote: Other references to Defence Production Act
Section.Text: Unless the context requires otherwise, every reference to the Defence Production Act in any provision of an Act of Parliament or of a statutory instrument, as defined in subsection 2(1) of the Statutory Instruments Act, is to be read as a reference to the Defence and National Security Production and Procurement Act.

Subsection.Text: The Minister designated under section 3 of the Defence Investment Agency Act must undertake a review of the operation of the amendments to the Defence and National Security Production and Procurement Act made by this Subdivision within three years after the day on which this subsection comes into force.
The Minister must cause a report on the review to be laid before each House of Parliament as soon as feasible after the report is completed.

Section.Text: The provisions of this Division come into force on a day or days to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
Subsection 85.01(2) of the Canada Transportation Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A carrier, on receipt of a written request to deal with a claim, must communicate to the claimant its decision on the claim within 30 days after the day on which the carrier received the request.

Section.Text: Sections 85.02 and 85.03 of the Act are repealed.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 85.04(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
A person may file a complaint in writing with the Minister if

Subsection 85.04(1) of the Act is amended by striking out √¢¬Ä¬úand√¢¬Ä¬ù at the end of paragraph (c), by adding √¢¬Ä¬úand√¢¬Ä¬ù at the end of paragraph (d) and by adding the following after paragraph (d):

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the person does not have a proceeding before, and has not obtained a final decision from, a court of competent jurisdiction in respect of the failure referred to in paragraph (a).

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 85.04(2) of the Act before paragraph (b) is replaced by the following:
The Minister may refuse to deal with a complaint or, at any time, cease dealing with it, if the Minister is of the opinion that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any of the conditions set out in subsection (1) has not been met;

Section.Text: Sections 85.05 and 85.06 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If the Minister does not refuse under subsection 85.04(2) to deal with a complaint, the Minister must offer to mediate the complaint and, if the parties consent, start the mediation no later than the 30th day after the day on which the complaint is filed.
An agreement that is reached as a result of mediation may be made an order of the Federal Court or of any superior court, in which case it is enforceable in the same manner as such an order.

Section.Text: If no agreement is reached as a result of mediation and the Minister does not cease dealing with the complaint under subsection 85.04(2), the Minister must, no later than the 90th day after the day on which the complaint is filed, and based on the information provided by the complainant and the carrier,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: make an order under subsection 85.07(1); or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: make an order dismissing the complaint.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 85.07(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

If the Minister finds that the carrier that is the subject of the complaint has failed to apply a fare, rate, charge or term or condition of carriage applicable to the air service it offers that is set out in its tariffs, the Minister may order the carrier to
Subsection 85.07(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:
An order made under subsection (1) may be made an order of the Federal Court or of any superior court, in which case it is enforceable in the same manner as such an order.

Section.Text: Section 85.08 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Prior decisions to be taken into account
Section.Text: In dealing with a complaint in respect of a flight, the Minister must, with respect to the issue of whether a flight delay, flight cancellation or denial of boarding is within a carrier√¢¬Ä¬ôs control, is within a carrier√¢¬Ä¬ôs control but is required for safety reasons or is outside a carrier√¢¬Ä¬ôs control, take into account any prior decision on that issue that is contained in an order made in respect of that flight by any person who resolved a complaint under this Part.
Section 85.09 of the Act is repealed.
Sections 85.1 to 85.13 of the Act are replaced by the following:

A carrier must pay any compensation or refund that it is required to pay under an agreement reached as a result of mediation under section 85.05, or under an order made under subsection 85.07(1), within 30 days after the day on which the agreement is reached or the order is made.
Sections 85.14 and 85.15 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Publication √¢¬Ä¬î order or summary of order

Subsection.Text: For each order made under paragraph 85.06(b) or subsection 85.07(1), the Minister must make public

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the number of the flight to which the order relates;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the date of departure of the flight that is indicated on the complainant√¢¬Ä¬ôs ticket;

any decision contained in the order with respect to the issue of whether any flight delay, flight cancellation or denial of boarding was within the carrier√¢¬Ä¬ôs control, was within the carrier√¢¬Ä¬ôs control but was required for safety reasons or was outside the carrier√¢¬Ä¬ôs control; and
a statement as to whether or not the carrier was ordered to provide compensation or a refund as set out in the carrier√¢¬Ä¬ôs tariffs or compensation for expenses incurred.

Subsection.Text: The Minister may, at the request of a complainant or carrier, decide to keep confidential any part of the order, other than the information referred to in subsection (1).

Section.Text: Each year, the Minister must make public the number and nature of the complaints filed under subsection 85.04(1), the names of the carriers against whom the complaints were made, the number of complaints for which an order was made under subsection 85.07(1) and the systemic trends observed.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 85.16(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Minister may establish fees or charges for the purpose of recovering all or a portion of the costs that the Minister determines to be related to the process of dealing with complaints √¢¬Ä¬î other than complaints disposed of under subsection 85.04(2) √¢¬Ä¬î under sections 85.05 to 85.08.
Subsections 85.16(3) and (4) of the Act are replaced by the following:
Before establishing fees or charges, the Minister must consult with any persons or organizations that the Minister considers to be interested in the matter.
The Minister must publish the fees and charges on a website.
Subsection 85.16(6) of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Minister √¢¬Ä¬î or the Agency, if it is required under section 85.17 to administer sections 85.04 to 85.14 √¢¬Ä¬î may spend the amounts obtained under this section in the fiscal year in which they are paid or in the next fiscal year.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 85.16:

Subsection.Text: The Minister may, by order, require the Agency to administer sections 85.04 to 85.14. If an order is made, the Agency is to exercise the powers and perform the duties and functions of the Minister under those sections in accordance with any directions that the Minister may set out in the order.
If the Minister makes an order under subsection (1),

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: complaints filed with the Agency are deemed not to be proceedings before the Agency; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: sections 17, 25 and 36.1 do not apply in respect of any matter that may be dealt with under sections 85.04 to 85.08.

Heading.TitleText: Alternative Resolution Process for Air Travel Complaints

Section.Text: The following definitions apply in sections 85.2 to 85.29.

Definition.Text: designated person√¢¬Ä¬Çmeans a person designated by the Minister under subsection 85.2(1).√¢¬Ä¬Ç(personne d√É¬©sign√É¬©e)
specified complaint√¢¬Ä¬Çmeans a complaint to which an order made under subsection 85.19(1) applies.√¢¬Ä¬Ç(plainte d√É¬©termin√É¬©e)

Subsection.Text: For the purpose of having complaints resolved in accordance with sections 85.22 to 85.25, the Governor in Council may, by order, suspend the application of sections 85.04 to 85.14 with respect to complaints, or any class of complaints, that allege that a carrier failed to apply a fare, rate, charge or term or condition of carriage applicable to the air service it offers that is set out in its tariffs.
An order made under subsection (1) may only relate to complaints that are filed on or after the day on which the order comes into force.
The Minister may designate a person as a person authorized to resolve specified complaints and may impose as part of the designation any conditions that the Minister considers appropriate.
The Minister must cause a list of designated persons to be published on a website.
The Minister may suspend or cancel a designation at any time and may vary its conditions.

Every carrier that offers an air service to, from or within Canada must, within 90 days after the day on which an order is made under subsection 85.19(1), enter into an agreement or arrangement with a designated person for the purposes of resolving specified complaints against the carrier.
The carrier must publish the name of the designated person on the carrier√¢¬Ä¬ôs website.
His Majesty is not liable for any costs related to the agreement or arrangement.

Section.Text: A person may file a specified complaint in writing with a designated person with whom a carrier has an agreement or arrangement if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the person alleges that the carrier failed to apply a fare, rate, charge or term or condition of carriage applicable to the air service it offers that is set out in its tariffs;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the person is adversely affected by that failure;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the person seeks compensation or a refund as set out in the carrier√¢¬Ä¬ôs tariffs or compensation for expenses incurred as a result of that failure;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the person made a written request to the carrier to resolve the matters to which the specified complaint relates but they were not resolved within 30 days after the day on which the request was made; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the person does not have a proceeding before, and has not obtained a final decision from, a court of competent jurisdiction in respect of the failure referred to in paragraph (a).

Subsection.Text: In resolving a specified complaint, a designated person may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: refuse to deal with the specified complaint or, at any time, cease dealing with it, if they are of the opinion that

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: any of the conditions set out in section 85.22 has not been met,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: it is clear on the face of the specified complaint that the carrier has complied with the obligations set out in its tariffs, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the complaint is vexatious or made in bad faith;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: mediate the specified complaint and endorse any agreement that may result;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: make an order dismissing the specified complaint; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: if they find that the carrier has failed to apply a fare, rate, charge or term or condition of carriage applicable to the air service it offers that is set out in its tariffs, order the carrier to

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: apply a fare, rate, charge or term or condition of carriage that is set out in its tariffs, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: compensate the complainant for any expenses the complainant incurred as a result of the carrier√¢¬Ä¬ôs failure.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Prior decisions to be taken into account
Subsection.Text: Section 85.08 applies with respect to the designated person as if they were the Minister.
An endorsement of a mediated agreement under paragraph (1)(b) is considered to be a decision for the purposes of sections 85.24 and 85.25.

An agreement endorsed under paragraph (1)(b) or an order made under paragraph (1)(d) may be made an order of the Federal Court or of any superior court, in which case it is enforceable in the same manner as such an order.

Section.Text: A decision of a designated person on a specified complaint is binding on the complainant and the carrier.
A carrier must pay any compensation or refund that it is required to pay under a decision of a designated person within 30 days after the day on which the decision is made.

Subsection.Text: If the Minister requests information with respect to the exercise of a designated person√¢¬Ä¬ôs powers or the performance of their duties or functions under their agreement or arrangement with a carrier, the designated person must provide the information to the Minister within 30 days after the day on which the request is made.
The designated person must provide the information in the form and manner specified by the Minister.

Section.Text: If the Governor in Council amends or revokes an order made under subsection 85.19(1),

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: every designated person must provide the Minister with all the information and assistance necessary to permit the Minister to resolve any complaints filed with the designated person to which sections 85.04 to 85.14 apply as a result of the amendment or revocation;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subject to subsection 85.04(2), the Minister must resolve those complaints as soon as feasible; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the deadlines set out in subsection 85.05(1) and section 85.06 do not apply in respect of those complaints.

Subsection.Text: If a person√¢¬Ä¬ôs designation under section 85.2 is suspended, the person is not authorized to exercise any of the powers set out in subsection 85.23(1).
If a person√¢¬Ä¬ôs designation under section 85.2 is cancelled, any carrier that had an agreement or arrangement with that person must

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: comply with subsection 85.21(1) within 90 days after the day on which the designation is cancelled; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: ensure that any complaints that have been filed with the person and in respect of which a decision has not yet been made are transferred to a designated person with whom the carrier has an agreement or arrangement.

Subsection.Text: If the Minister makes a payment to a designated person in respect of the resolution of a specified complaint, the carrier that is the subject of the complaint is liable to the Minister for the payment and for any costs incurred in relation to it.
The amounts for which the carrier is liable constitute a debt to His Majesty in right of Canada and may be recovered as such in a court of competent jurisdiction.
The portion of subsection 86.11(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
The Minister may make regulations in relation to flights to, from and within Canada, including connecting flights,
Subsection 86.11(1) of the Act is amended by adding √¢¬Ä¬úand√¢¬Ä¬ù at the end of paragraph (e), by striking out √¢¬Ä¬úand√¢¬Ä¬ù at the end of paragraph (f) and by repealing paragraph (g).
Subsection 86.11(2) of the Act is repealed.
Subsection 86.11 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (4):

The regulations made under subsection (1) that apply in respect of a complaint filed under subsection 85.04(1) or section 85.22 are the regulations that were in force on the date of purchase of the ticket for the flight to which the complaint relates.
Subparagraphs 177(1)(a)(i) and (ii) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: any provision of this Act or of any regulation, order or direction made pursuant to this Act, other than a provision of a regulation made under subsection 86.11(1),

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the requirements of any provision referred to in subparagraph (i), other than a requirement of a provision of a regulation made under subsection 86.11(1), or

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 177(1)(b)(ii) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: $25,000 √¢¬Ä¬î or, in the case of a violation involving a contravention of a provision of a regulation made under subsection 86.11(1), $1,000,000 √¢¬Ä¬î in the case of a corporation.

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 177(1)(b)(ii) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: $25,000, in the case of a corporation.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 177(2)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: designate as a provision or requirement the contravention of which may be proceeded with as a violation in accordance with sections 179 and 180 any provision of section 51 or 51.2, any provision of any regulation made under section 50 or 51 or subsection 86.11(1), or any requirement of section 51 or 51.2 or of any provision of such a regulation; and

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 177(2)(b)(ii) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: $25,000 √¢¬Ä¬î or, in the case of a violation involving a contravention of a provision of a regulation made under subsection 86.11(1) or a contravention of a requirement of such a provision, $1,000,000 √¢¬Ä¬î in the case of a corporation.

Subsection.Text: Section 177 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2.01):

The contravention of subsection 85.01(2), section 85.09, subsection 85.21(1), section 85.25 or 85.26 or paragraph 85.27(a) or 85.28(2)(a) or (b) may be proceeded with as a violation in accordance with sections 179 and 180. The maximum amount payable for each violation is $1,000,000.
The portion of subsection 178(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

The Agency, in respect of a violation referred to in subsection 177(1), (2.1) or (3) √¢¬Ä¬î or the Minister, in respect of a violation referred to in subsection 177(2), (2.001), (2.01) or (2.2), other than a violation involving a contravention of a provision of a regulation made under subsection 86.11(1) or a contravention of a requirement of such a provision √¢¬Ä¬î may
Section 178 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Notices of violation √¢¬Ä¬î air travel claims and complaints
Subsection.Text: The Agency may exercise the powers set out in subsection (1) in respect of violations referred to in subsection 177(2.02). Persons who are designated as enforcement officers, whether individually or by class, are deemed to be designated under paragraph (1)(a).
Section 178 of the Act is amended by adding the following before subsection (2):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Notices of violation √¢¬Ä¬î air passenger regulations
Subsection.Text: The Agency may exercise the powers set out in subsection (1) in respect of violations involving a contravention of a provision of a regulation made under subsection 86.11(1) or a contravention of a requirement of such a provision. Persons who are designated as enforcement officers, whether individually or by class, are deemed to be designated under paragraph (1)(a).

Section.Text: Section 180.1 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Air travel claims and complaints √¢¬Ä¬î compliance agreement
Subsection.Text: A person who has been served with a notice of violation that identifies a violation referred to in subsection 177(2.02) may, in lieu of the options referred to in subsection (1), request, within the time and in the manner set out in the notice, to enter into a compliance agreement with the Agency for the purpose of ensuring the person√¢¬Ä¬ôs compliance with the provision to which the violation relates.
In this section and sections 355 to 362, Act means the Canada Transportation Act.
Unless the context requires otherwise, words and expressions used in sections 355 to 362 have the same meaning as in the Act.

For the purpose of having complaints resolved in accordance with sections 357 to 359, the Governor in Council may, by order, suspend the application of subsection 85.04(2) and sections 85.05 to 85.14 of the Act with respect to complaints that are filed with the Agency under subsection 85.04(1) of the Act before the day on which the order comes into force and of which no complaint resolution officer is seized before that day.
For greater certainty,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an order may apply to a class of complaints;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the scope of an order is limited to complaints that are filed before the day on which subsection 341(1) comes into force; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: an order may apply to a complaint referred to in section 473 of the Budget Implementation Act, 2023, No. 1.

Section.Text: The Minister, or the Agency, if directed by the Minister, may enter into a contract with a person to resolve complaints to which an order made under subsection 355(1) applies.

Subsection.Text: In resolving a complaint to which an order made under subsection 355(1) applies, a person referred to in section 356 may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: refuse to deal with the complaint, or, at any time, cease dealing with it, if they are of the opinion that

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: any of the conditions set out in subsection 85.04(1) of the Act has not been met,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: it is clear on the face of the complaint that the carrier has complied with the obligations set out in its tariffs, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the complaint is vexatious or made in bad faith;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: mediate the complaint and endorse any agreement that may result;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: make an order dismissing the complaint; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: if they find that the carrier has failed to apply a fare, rate, charge or term or condition of carriage applicable to the air service it offers that is set out in its tariffs, order the carrier to

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: apply a fare, rate, charge or term or condition of carriage that is set out in its tariffs, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: compensate the complainant for any expenses the complainant incurred as a result of the carrier√¢¬Ä¬ôs failure.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Prior decisions to be taken into account
Subsection.Text: In dealing with a complaint in respect of a flight, a person referred to in section 356 must, with respect to the issue of whether a flight delay, flight cancellation or denial of boarding is within a carrier√¢¬Ä¬ôs control, is within a carrier√¢¬Ä¬ôs control but is required for safety reasons or is outside a carrier√¢¬Ä¬ôs control, take into account any prior decision on that issue that is contained in an order made in respect of that flight by any person who resolved a complaint under subsection (1) or under Part II of the Act.
An endorsement of a mediated agreement under paragraph (1)(b) is considered to be a decision for the purposes of sections 358 and 359.

An agreement endorsed under paragraph (1)(b) or an order made under paragraph (1)(d) may be made an order of the Federal Court or of any superior court, in which case it is enforceable in the same manner as such an order.

Section.Text: A decision made under subsection 357(1) is binding on the complainant and the carrier.

Subsection.Text: A carrier must pay any compensation or refund that it is required to pay under a decision made under subsection 357(1) within 30 days after the day on which the decision is made.
The contravention of subsection (1) may be proceeded with as a violation in accordance with sections 179 and 180 of the Act. The maximum amount payable for the violation is $1,000,000.

The Agency may exercise the powers set out in subsection 178(1) of the Act in respect of the violation. Persons who are designated as enforcement officers, whether individually or by class, are deemed to be designated under paragraph 178(1)(a) of the Act.

Sections 178.1 to 180.7, 181 and 181.1 of the Act apply to proceedings for the violation, with any necessary modifications, and every reference to the Minister in sections 180.3 to 180.7 of the Act is to be read as a reference to the Agency or to a person designated by the Agency.

Despite subsection 180.1(1) of the Act, a carrier that has been served with a notice of violation in respect of the violation may request, within the time and in the manner set out in the notice, to enter into a compliance agreement with the Agency for the purpose of ensuring the carrier√¢¬Ä¬ôs compliance with subsection (1).

Section.Text: Sections 85.02 to 85.16 of the Act, as they read immediately before the day on which subsection 341(1) comes into force, continue to apply in respect of any complaint filed before that day under subsection 85.04(1) of the Act, except as otherwise provided in an order made under subsection 355(1).

If an order is made under subsection 355(1), the Agency must indicate, as part of its annual report, the number of complaints for which an order was made under paragraph 357(1)(d) and the systemic trends observed in relation to the resolution of complaints by persons referred to in section 356.

Subsection 86.11(5) of the Act, as enacted by subsection 350(4), does not apply in respect of complaints that are filed under subsection 85.04(1) of the Act before the day on which subsection 350(4) comes into force.

Subsection.Text: In subsections (2) to (7), other Act means the Budget Implementation Act, 2023, No. 1.
On the first day on which both section 461 of the other Act and section 344 of this Act are in force, section 85.08 of the Canada Transportation Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Prior decisions to be taken into account
Section.Text: In dealing with a complaint in respect of a flight, the Minister must, with respect to the issue of whether an exception specified by regulations made under paragraph 86.11(1)(b.1) applies, take into account any prior decision on that issue that is contained in an order made in respect of that flight by any person who resolved a complaint under this Part.

Subsection.Text: If section 462 of the other Act comes into force before section 347 of this Act, then that section 347 is amended by replacing the paragraph 85.14(1)(c) that it enacts with the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any decision contained in the order with respect to the issue of whether an exception specified by regulations made under paragraph 86.11(1)(b.1) applies; and

Subsection.Text: If section 347 of this Act comes into force before section 462 of the other Act, then that section 462 is replaced with the following:

Section.Text: Paragraph 85.14(1)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any decision contained in the order with respect to the issue of whether an exception specified by regulations made under paragraph 86.11(1)(b.1) applies; and

Subsection.Text: If section 462 of the other Act comes into force on the same day as section 347 of this Act, then that section 462 is deemed to come into force first and subsection (3) applies as a consequence.
If section 463 of the other Act comes into force before section 348 of this Act, then that section 348 is repealed.
If section 463 of the other Act comes into force on the same day as section 348 of this Act, then that section 348 is deemed to have come into force first.

Sections 339 and 340, subsections 341(1) and (3), sections 342 to 344 and 346 to 349, subsections 350(4), 351(6) and 352(2) and section 353 come into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
Subsections 350(1) to (3), 351(1) and (3) to (5) and 352(1) and (3) come into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
